== Kamo Shrine ([[:ja:鴨神社|鴨神社]]) == <!--for some reason this has an independent Japanese article [[:ja:鴨神社]]--> '''Kamo Shrine''' (鴨神社) is a Shinto shrine whose name includes the character "Kamo" (鴨).
* [[鴨神社_(玉城町)]] * {{ill|Kamo Shrine (Kamo-sha)|ja|賀茂神社|lt=Kamo Shrine (Kamo-sha)|lt_ja=鴨社}} ({{ill|Kyoto|ja|京都府}}, {{ill|Kyoto City|ja|京都市}}) - Another name for {{ill|Kamo Shrines|ja|賀茂神社}}, which collectively refers to {{ill|Kamigamo Shrine|ja|賀茂別雷神社}} and {{ill|Shimogamo Shrine|ja|賀茂御祖神社}}. * {{ill|Kamo Shrine (Tamaki)|ja|鴨神社 (玉城町)|lt=Kamo Shrine|lt_ja=鴨神社}} ({{ill|Mie Prefecture|ja|三重県}}, {{ill|Tamaki|ja|玉城町}}, Yamagami) * {{ill|Kamo Shrine (Takatuki)|ja|鴨神社 (高槻市)|lt=Kamo Shrine|lt_ja=鴨神社}} ({{ill|Osaka Prefecture|ja|大阪府}}, {{ill|Takatuki|ja|高槻市}}, Akaōji-chō) * {{ill|Kamo Shrine (Kawanishi)|ja|鴨神社 (川西市)|lt=Kamo Shrine|lt_ja=鴨神社}} ({{ill|Hyogo Prefecture|ja|兵庫県}}, {{ill|Kawanishi|ja|川西市}}, Kamo) * {{ill|Kamo Shrine (Tamano)|ja|鴨神社 (玉野市)|lt=Kamo Shrine|lt_ja=鴨神社}} ({{ill|Okayama Prefecture|ja|岡山県}}, {{ill|Tamano|ja|玉野市}}, Nagao) * {{ill|Kamo Shrine (Akaiwa)|ja|鴨神社 (赤磐市)|lt=Kamo Shrine|lt_ja=鴨神社}} (Okayama Prefecture, {{ill|Akaiwa|ja|赤磐市}}, Nibori-nishi) * {{ill|Kamo Shrine (Asakuchi)|ja|鴨神社 (浅口市)|lt=Kamo Shrine|lt_ja=鴨神社}} (Okayama Prefecture, {{ill|Asakuchi|ja|浅口市}}, Kamogata-chō Kamo) * {{ill|Kamo Shrine (Kibichuo)|ja|鴨神社 (吉備中央町)|lt=Kamo Shrine|lt_ja=鴨神社}} (Okayama Prefecture, {{ill|Kibichuo|ja|吉備中央町}}, Kamikamo) * {{ill|Kamo Shrine (Sanyo-Onoda)|ja|鴨神社 (山陽小野田市)|lt=Kamo Shrine|lt_ja=鴨神社}} ({{ill|Yamaguchi Prefecture|ja|山口県}}, {{ill|Sanyo-Onoda|ja|山陽小野田市}}, Asa) * {{ill|Kamo Shrine (Higashimiyoshi)|ja|鴨神社 (東みよし町)|lt=Kamo Shrine|lt_ja=鴨神社}} ({{ill|Tokushima Prefecture|ja|徳島県}}, {{ill|Higashimiyoshi|ja|東みよし町}}, Kamo) * {{ill|Kamo Shrine (Sakaide)|ja|鴨神社 (坂出市)|lt=Kamo Shrine|lt_ja=鴨神社}} - An {{ill|Ancient shrine of Sanuki Province|ja|讃岐国|阿野郡}}. Theories of its location include {{ill|Higashi Kamo Shrine|ja|鴨神社 (坂出市)#東鴨神社|lt=Higashi Kamo Shrine}} and {{ill|Nishi Kamo Shrine|ja|鴨神社 (坂出市)#西鴨神社|lt=Nishi Kamo Shrine}} (both in {{ill|Sakaide|ja|坂出市}}, Kamochō).

'''Kamo Shrine''' (加茂神社・賀茂神社) refers to Shinto shrines with the name "Kamo."
Most of these shrines were branches of the famous {{ill|Kamo Shrines|ja|賀茂神社}}, {{ill|Kamigamo Shrine|ja|賀茂別雷神社}} (Upper Kamo Shrine) and {{ill|Shimogamo Shrine|ja|賀茂御祖神社}} (Lower Kamo Shrine), located in Kyoto. Many shrines are also historically tied to the ancient {{ill|Kamo clan|ja|賀茂県主氏|Kamo clan=Kamo clan|lt=Kamo-no-agatanushi}} clan (賀茂県主氏).
There are multiple spellings so this article will cover all of them

== Kamo Shrine ([[:ja:鴨神社|鴨神社]]) == <!--for some reason this has an independent Japanese article [[:ja:鴨神社]]--> '''Kamo Shrine''' (鴨神社) is a Shinto shrine whose name includes the character "Kamo" (鴨).
* [[鴨神社_(玉城町)]] * {{ill|Kamo Shrine (Kamo-sha)|ja|賀茂神社|lt=Kamo Shrine (Kamo-sha)|lt_ja=鴨社}} ({{ill|Kyoto|ja|京都府}}, {{ill|Kyoto City|ja|京都市}}) - Another name for {{ill|Kamo Shrines|ja|賀茂神社}}, which collectively refers to {{ill|Kamigamo Shrine|ja|賀茂別雷神社}} and {{ill|Shimogamo Shrine|ja|賀茂御祖神社}}. * {{ill|Kamo Shrine (Tamaki)|ja|鴨神社 (玉城町)|lt=Kamo Shrine|lt_ja=鴨神社}} ({{ill|Mie Prefecture|ja|三重県}}, {{ill|Tamaki|ja|玉城町}}, Yamagami) * {{ill|Kamo Shrine (Takatuki)|ja|鴨神社 (高槻市)|lt=Kamo Shrine|lt_ja=鴨神社}} ({{ill|Osaka Prefecture|ja|大阪府}}, {{ill|Takatuki|ja|高槻市}}, Akaōji-chō) * {{ill|Kamo Shrine (Kawanishi)|ja|鴨神社 (川西市)|lt=Kamo Shrine|lt_ja=鴨神社}} ({{ill|Hyogo Prefecture|ja|兵庫県}}, {{ill|Kawanishi|ja|川西市}}, Kamo) * {{ill|Kamo Shrine (Tamano)|ja|鴨神社 (玉野市)|lt=Kamo Shrine|lt_ja=鴨神社}} ({{ill|Okayama Prefecture|ja|岡山県}}, {{ill|Tamano|ja|玉野市}}, Nagao) * {{ill|Kamo Shrine (Akaiwa)|ja|鴨神社 (赤磐市)|lt=Kamo Shrine|lt_ja=鴨神社}} (Okayama Prefecture, {{ill|Akaiwa|ja|赤磐市}}, Nibori-nishi) * {{ill|Kamo Shrine (Asakuchi)|ja|鴨神社 (浅口市)|lt=Kamo Shrine|lt_ja=鴨神社}} (Okayama Prefecture, {{ill|Asakuchi|ja|浅口市}}, Kamogata-chō Kamo) * {{ill|Kamo Shrine (Kibichuo)|ja|鴨神社 (吉備中央町)|lt=Kamo Shrine|lt_ja=鴨神社}} (Okayama Prefecture, {{ill|Kibichuo|ja|吉備中央町}}, Kamikamo) * {{ill|Kamo Shrine (Sanyo-Onoda)|ja|鴨神社 (山陽小野田市)|lt=Kamo Shrine|lt_ja=鴨神社}} ({{ill|Yamaguchi Prefecture|ja|山口県}}, {{ill|Sanyo-Onoda|ja|山陽小野田市}}, Asa) * {{ill|Kamo Shrine (Higashimiyoshi)|ja|鴨神社 (東みよし町)|lt=Kamo Shrine|lt_ja=鴨神社}} ({{ill|Tokushima Prefecture|ja|徳島県}}, {{ill|Higashimiyoshi|ja|東みよし町}}, Kamo) * {{ill|Kamo Shrine (Sakaide)|ja|鴨神社 (坂出市)|lt=Kamo Shrine|lt_ja=鴨神社}} - An {{ill|Ancient shrine of Sanuki Province|ja|讃岐国|阿野郡}}. Theories of its location include {{ill|Higashi Kamo Shrine|ja|鴨神社 (坂出市)#東鴨神社|lt=Higashi Kamo Shrine}} and {{ill|Nishi Kamo Shrine|ja|鴨神社 (坂出市)#西鴨神社|lt=Nishi Kamo Shrine}} (both in {{ill|Sakaide|ja|坂出市}}, Kamochō).

'''Kamo Shrine''' (加茂神社・賀茂神社) refers to Shinto shrines with the name "Kamo."
Most of these shrines were branches of the famous {{ill|Kamo Shrines|ja|賀茂神社}}, {{ill|Kamigamo Shrine|ja|賀茂別雷神社}} (Upper Kamo Shrine) and {{ill|Shimogamo Shrine|ja|賀茂御祖神社}} (Lower Kamo Shrine), located in Kyoto. Many shrines are also historically tied to the ancient {{ill|Kamo clan|ja|賀茂県主氏|Kamo clan=Kamo clan|lt=Kamo-no-agatanushi}} clan (賀茂県主氏).
There are multiple spellings so this article will cover all of them

Theories of its location include {{ill|Higashi Kamo Shrine|ja|鴨神社 (坂出市)#東鴨神社|lt=Higashi Kamo Shrine}} and {{ill|Nishi Kamo Shrine|ja|鴨神社 (坂出市)#西鴨神社|lt=Nishi Kamo Shrine}} (both in {{ill|Sakaide|ja|坂出市}}, Kamochō).

revision.comment: 「賀茂神社 (曖昧さ回避)」へ分割（節「主な賀茂神社」の移動）
revision.text:
{{otheruses|日本各地の「加茂神社」|京都にある神社|賀茂神社|同音異字の賀茂神社|賀茂神社 (曖昧さ回避)|鴨神社|鴨神社}} '''加茂神社'''（かもじんじゃ）は、｢加茂」を社名に持つ[[神社]]。
その大半は、京都の[[賀茂神社]]と呼ばれる[[賀茂別雷神社]]（上賀茂神社）と[[賀茂御祖神社]]（下鴨神社）から勧請されたもので、古代氏族・[[賀茂県主氏]]に関係するものもある。
== 主な加茂神社 ==
=== 東北・関東地方 === * [[加茂神社 (大船渡市)|加茂神社]] （[[岩手県]][[大船渡市]]大船渡町） * [[加茂神社 (大崎市)|加茂神社]] （[[宮城県]][[大崎市]]田尻） * [[加茂神社 (大仙市)|加茂神社]] （[[秋田県]][[大仙市]]下深井） * [[加茂神社 (天童市)|加茂神社]] （[[山形県]][[天童市]]老野森） * [[加茂神社 (かすみがうら市)|加茂神社]] （[[茨城県]][[かすみがうら市]]加茂） * [[加茂神社 (那須烏山市)|加茂神社]] （[[栃木県]][[那須烏山市]]月次） * [[加茂神社 (さいたま市)|加茂神社]] （[[埼玉県]][[さいたま市]][[北区 (さいたま市)|北区]][[宮原町 (さいたま市)|宮原町]]） * [[加茂神社 (市原市)|加茂神社]] ([[千葉県]][[市原市]]加茂） * [[加茂神社 (鴨川市)|加茂神社]] （千葉県[[鴨川市]][[成川 (鴨川市)|成川]]） * [[加茂神社 (小田原市)|加茂神社]] （[[神奈川県]][[小田原市]]鴨宮）
=== 中部地方 === * [[加茂神社 (妙高市)|加茂神社]] （[[新潟県]][[妙高市]]小出雲） * [[加茂神社 (佐渡市)|加茂神社]] （新潟県[[佐渡市]]栗野江） * [[加茂神社 (富山市)|加茂神社]] （[[富山県]][[富山市]]任海） * [[加茂神社 (射水市)|加茂神社]] （富山県[[射水市]]加茂中部） – 下村加茂神社 * [[加茂神社 (射水市海老江)|加茂神社]] （富山県射水市海老江） * [[加茂神社 (福井市)|加茂神社]] （[[福井県]][[福井市]]加茂町） * [[加茂神社 (小浜市)|加茂神社]] （福井県[[小浜市]]矢代） * [[加茂神社 (丹波山村)|加茂神社]] （[[山梨県]][[北都留郡]][[丹波山村]]鴨沢） * [[加茂神社 (長野市西長野)|加茂神社]] （[[長野県]][[長野市]][[西長野]]） * [[加茂神社 (長野市鬼無里)|加茂神社]] （長野県長野市鬼無里） * [[加茂神社 (高山市)|加茂神社]] （[[岐阜県]][[高山市]]国府町） * [[加茂神社 (美濃加茂市蜂屋町)|加茂神社]] （岐阜県[[美濃加茂市]]蜂屋町） * [[加茂神社 (美濃加茂市加茂野町)|加茂神社]] （岐阜県美濃加茂市加茂野町）
=== 近畿地方 === * [[加茂神社 (鳥羽市)|加茂神社]] （[[三重県]][[鳥羽市]]松尾町） * [[加茂神社 (甲賀市土山町)|加茂神社]] （[[滋賀県]][[甲賀市]]土山町） * [[加茂神社 (甲賀市水口町)|加茂神社]] （滋賀県甲賀市水口町） * [[加茂神社 (高島市安曇川町)|加茂神社]] （滋賀県[[高島市]]安曇川町） * [[加茂神社 (高島市新旭町)|加茂神社]] （滋賀県高島市新旭町） * [[加茂神社 (米原市)|加茂神社]] （滋賀県[[米原市]]醒井） * [[加茂神社 (阪南市)|加茂神社]] （[[大阪府]][[阪南市]]箱作） * [[加茂神社 (姫路市飾磨)|加茂神社]] （[[兵庫県]][[姫路市]][[飾磨]]区加茂） * [[加茂神社 (姫路市安富町)|加茂神社]] （兵庫県姫路市安富町） – 安志加茂神社 * [[加茂神社 (加古川市)|加茂神社]] （兵庫県[[加古川市]]加古川町） * [[加茂神社 (丹波篠山市)|加茂神社]] （兵庫県[[丹波篠山市]]西木之部） * [[加茂神社 (海南市)|加茂神社]] （[[和歌山県]][[海南市]]下津町下） * [[加茂神社 (紀の川市)|加茂神社]] （和歌山県[[紀の川市]]西川原）
=== 中国地方 === * [[加茂神社 (雲南市加茂町)|加茂神社]] （[[島根県]][[雲南市]]加茂町） * [[加茂神社 (雲南市木次町)|加茂神社]] （島根県雲南市木次町） * [[加茂神社 (岡山市)|加茂神社]] （[[岡山県]][[岡山市]][[北区 (岡山市)|北区]]加茂） * [[加茂神社 (津山市)|加茂神社]] （岡山県[[津山市]]加茂町） * [[加茂神社 (玉野市)|加茂神社]] （岡山県[[玉野市]]木目） * [[加茂神社 (総社市)|加茂神社]] （岡山県[[総社市]]山田） * [[加茂神社 (真庭市)|加茂神社]] （岡山県[[真庭市]][[蒜山 (地名)|蒜山]]） * [[加茂神社 (久米南町)|加茂神社]] （岡山県[[久米郡]][[久米南町]]里方）
=== 四国・九州地方 === * [[加茂神社 (徳島市南庄町)|加茂神社]] （[[徳島県]][[徳島市]][[南庄町]]） * [[加茂神社 (徳島市多家良町)|加茂神社]] （徳島県徳島市[[多家良町]]） * [[加茂神社 (高松市)|加茂神社]] （[[香川県]][[高松市]]亀水町） * [[加茂神社 (観音寺市)|加茂神社]] （香川県[[観音寺市]]植田町） * [[加茂神社 (多度津町)|加茂神社]] （香川県[[仲多度郡]][[多度津町]]南鴨） * [[加茂神社 (まんのう町)|加茂神社]] （香川県仲多度郡[[まんのう町]]佐文） * [[加茂神社 (今治市)|加茂神社]] （[[愛媛県]][[今治市]]菊間町） * [[加茂神社 (西条市)|加茂神社]] （愛媛県[[西条市]]福武） * [[加茂神社 (黒潮町)|加茂神社]] （[[高知県]][[幡多郡]][[黒潮町]]入野） * [[加茂神社 (宮若市)|加茂神社]] （[[福岡県]][[宮若市]]下） * [[加茂神社 (糸島市)|加茂神社]] （福岡県[[糸島市]]二丈福井） * [[加茂神社 (添田町)|加茂神社]] （福岡県[[田川郡]][[添田町]]野田） * [[加茂神社 (長崎市)|加茂神社]] （[[長崎県]][[長崎市]]大崎町） * [[加茂神社 (大分市)|加茂神社]] （[[大分県]][[大分市]]細） * [[加茂神社 (中津市田尻)|加茂神社]] （大分県[[中津市]]田尻） * [[加茂神社 (津久見市)|加茂神社]] （大分県[[津久見市]]保戸島） – [[保戸島]]内 * [[加茂神社 (錦町)|加茂神社]] （[[熊本県]][[球磨郡]][[錦町]]木上） – 木上加茂神社
== 関連項目 ==
* [[賀茂別雷神社]]、[[賀茂御祖神社]] * [[賀茂神社 (曖昧さ回避)|賀茂神社]] * [[鴨神社]] {{曖昧さ回避}} {{デフォルトソート:かもしんしや}} [[Category:同名の神社]] [[Category:加茂神社|!かもしんしや]]

revision.text:
'''Kamo Shrine''' (加茂神社・賀茂神社) refers to Shinto shrines with the name "Kamo."
Most of these shrines were branches of the famous {{ill|Kamo Shrines|ja|賀茂神社}}, {{ill|Kamigamo Shrine|ja|賀茂別雷神社}} (Upper Kamo Shrine) and {{ill|Shimogamo Shrine|ja|賀茂御祖神社}} (Lower Kamo Shrine), located in Kyoto. Many shrines are also historically tied to the ancient {{ill|Kamo clan|ja|賀茂県主氏|Kamo clan=Kamo clan|lt=Kamo-no-agatanushi}} clan (賀茂県主氏).
There are multiple spellings so this article will cover all of them
== Kamo Shrine ([[:ja:加茂神社|加茂神社]]) == === Tohoku === * {{ill|Kamo Shrine (Osaki)|ja|加茂神社 (大崎市)}} (Osaki City, Miyagi Prefecture) * {{ill|Kamo Shrine (Yamagata)|ja|加茂神社 (山形市)}} (Yamagata City, Yamagata Prefecture)
=== Kanto === * {{ill|Kamo Shrine (Nasukarasuyama)|ja|加茂神社 (那須烏山市)}} (Nasukarasuyama, Tochigi Prefecture) * {{ill|Kamo Shrine (Saitama)|ja|加茂神社 (さいたま市)}} (Saitama City, Saitama Prefecture) * {{ill|Kamo Shrine (Kamogawa)|ja|加茂神社 (鴨川市)}} (Kamogawa City, Chiba Prefecture) * Kamo Shrine (Ichihara City, Chiba Prefecture, Kamo 2-5-2) * {{ill|Kamo Shrine (Odawara)|ja|加茂神社 (小田原市)}} (Odawara City, Kanagawa Prefecture)
=== Chubu === * {{ill|Kamo Shrine (Sado)|ja|加茂神社 (佐渡市)}} (Sado City, Niigata Prefecture) * {{ill|Kamo Shrine (Myoko)|ja|加茂神社 (妙高市)}} (Myoko City, Niigata Prefecture) * {{ill|Kamo Shrine (Toyama)|ja|加茂神社 (富山市)}} (Toyama City, Toyama Prefecture) * {{ill|Kamo Shrine (Imizu)|ja|加茂神社 (射水市)}} (Imizu City, Toyama Prefecture) * {{ill|Kamo Shrine (Fukui)|ja|加茂神社 (福井市)}} (Fukui City, Fukui Prefecture) * {{ill|Kamo Shrine (Obama)|ja|加茂神社 (小浜市)}} (Obama City, Fukui Prefecture) * {{ill|Kamo Shrine (Tabayama)|ja|加茂神社 (丹波山村)}} (Tabayama Village, Yamanashi Prefecture) * {{ill|Kamo Shrine (Nagano Nishinagano)|ja|加茂神社 (長野市西長野)}} (Nishinagano, Nagano City, Nagano Prefecture) * {{ill|Kamo Shrine (Nagano Kinasa)|ja|加茂神社 (長野市鬼無里)}} (Kinasa, Nagano City, Nagano Prefecture) * {{ill|Kamo Shrine (Takayama)|ja|加茂神社 (高山市)}} (Takayama City, Gifu Prefecture) * {{ill|Kamo Shrine (Minokamo)|ja|加茂神社 (美濃加茂市)}} (Minokamo City, Gifu Prefecture)
=== Kinki === * {{ill|Kamo Shrine (Toba)|ja|加茂神社 (鳥羽市)}} (Toba City, Mie Prefecture) * {{ill|Kamo Shrine (Koka)|ja|加茂神社 (甲賀市)}} (Koka City, Shiga Prefecture) * {{ill|Kamo Shrine (Takashima)|ja|加茂神社 (高島市)}} (Takashima City, Shiga Prefecture) * {{ill|Kamo Shrine (Maibara)|ja|加茂神社 (米原市)}} (Maibara City, Shiga Prefecture) * {{ill|Kamo Shrine (Hannan)|ja|加茂神社 (阪南市)}} (Hannan City, Osaka Prefecture) * {{ill|Kamo Shrine (Himeji)|ja|加茂神社 (姫路市)}} (Himeji City, Hyogo Prefecture) * {{ill|Kamo Shrine (Kakogawa)|ja|加茂神社 (加古川市)}} (Kakogawa City, Hyogo Prefecture) * {{ill|Kamo Shrine (Tanbasasayama)|ja|加茂神社 (丹波篠山市)}} (Tanbasasayama City, Hyogo Prefecture) * {{ill|Kamo Shrine (Kainan)|ja|加茂神社 (海南市)}} (Kainan City, Wakayama Prefecture) * {{ill|Kamo Shrine (Kinokawa)|ja|加茂神社 (紀の川市)}} (Kinokawa City, Wakayama Prefecture)
=== Chugoku === * {{ill|Kamo Shrine (Unnan)|ja|加茂神社 (雲南市)}} (Unnan City, Shimane Prefecture) * {{ill|Kamo Shrine (Okayama)|ja|加茂神社 (岡山市)}} (Okayama City, Okayama Prefecture) * {{ill|Kamo Shrine (Tamano)|ja|加茂神社 (玉野市)}} (Tamano City, Okayama Prefecture) * {{ill|Kamo Shrine (Soja)|ja|加茂神社 (総社市)}} (Soja City, Okayama Prefecture) * {{ill|Kamo Shrine (Tsuyama)|ja|加茂神社 (津山市)}} (Tsuyama City, Okayama Prefecture) * {{ill|Kamo Shrine (Maniwa)|ja|加茂神社 (真庭市)}} (Maniwa City, Okayama Prefecture) * {{ill|Kamo Shrine (Kumenan)|ja|加茂神社 (久米南町)}} (Kumenan Town, Okayama Prefecture)
=== Shikoku === * {{ill|Kamo Shrine (Kanonji)|ja|加茂神社 (観音寺市)}} (Kanonji City, Kagawa Prefecture) * {{ill|Kamo Shrine (Manno)|ja|加茂神社 (まんのう町)}} (Manno Town, Kagawa Prefecture) * {{ill|Kamo Shrine (Imabari)|ja|加茂神社 (今治市)}} (Imabari City, Ehime Prefecture)
=== Kyushu === * {{ill|Kamo Shrine (Tsukumi)|ja|加茂神社 (津久見市)}} (Tsukumi City, Oita Prefecture, Hodoshima)

=== Kanto === * {{ill|Kamo Shrine (Kiryu)|ja|賀茂神社 (桐生市)}} (Kiryu City, Gunma Prefecture) * {{ill|Kamo Shrine (Sanmu)|ja|賀茂神社 (山武市)}} (Sanmu City, Chiba Prefecture) * {{ill|Kamo Shrine (Minamiboso)|ja|賀茂神社 (南房総市)}} (Minamiboso City, Chiba Prefecture) * {{ill|Kamo Shrine (Odawara)|ja|賀茂神社 (小田原市)}} (Odawara City, Kanagawa Prefecture)
=== Chubu === * {{ill|Kamo Shrine (Kahoku)|ja|賀茂神社 (かほく市)}} (Kahoku City, Ishikawa Prefecture) * {{ill|Kamo Shrine (Fukui)|ja|賀茂神社 (福井市)}} (Fukui City, Fukui Prefecture) * {{ill|Kamo Shrine (Mori Town)|ja|賀茂神社 (周智郡森町)}} (Mori Town, Shuchi District, Shizuoka Prefecture) * {{ill|Kamo Shrine (Minamiizu)|ja|賀茂神社 (南伊豆町)}} (Minamiizu Town, Shizuoka Prefecture) - Kabata Kamo Shrine, a Shikinai Shrine in Kamo District, Shizuoka Prefecture. * {{ill|Kamo Shrine (Toyohashi)|ja|賀茂神社 (豊橋市)}} (Toyohashi City, Aichi Prefecture) * {{ill|Kamo Shrine (Ichinomiya)|ja|賀茂神社 (一宮市)}} (Ichinomiya City, Aichi Prefecture)
=== Kinki === * {{ill|Kamo Shrine (Omi-Hachiman)|ja|賀茂神社 (近江八幡市)}} (Omi-Hachiman City, Shiga Prefecture) * {{ill|Kamo Shrine (Joyo)|ja|賀茂神社 (城陽市)}} (Joyo City, Kyoto Prefecture) * {{ill|Kamo Shrine (Tatsuno)|ja|賀茂神社 (たつの市)}} (Tatsuno City, Hyogo Prefecture)
=== Chugoku === * {{ill|Kamo Shrine (Kurayoshi)|ja|賀茂神社 (倉吉市)}} (Kurayoshi City, Tottori Prefecture) * {{ill|Kamo Shrine (Daisen)|ja|賀茂神社 (大山町)}} (Daisen Town, Saihaku District, Tottori Prefecture) * {{ill|Kamo Shrine (Nanbu, Tottori)|ja|賀茂神社 (鳥取県南部町)}} (Nanbu Town, Saihaku District, Tottori Prefecture) * {{ill|Kamo Shrine (Yazu Town)|ja|賀茂神社 (鳥取県八頭町)}} (Yazu Town, Yazu District, Tottori Prefecture) * {{ill|Kamo Shrine (Fukuyama)|ja|賀茂神社 (福山市)}} (Fukuyama City, Hiroshima Prefecture, Kamocho Ashihara) * {{ill|Kamo Shrine (Yanai)|ja|賀茂神社 (柳井市)}} (Yanai City, Yamaguchi Prefecture)
=== Shikoku === * {{ill|Kamo Shrine (Awa)|ja|賀茂神社 (阿波市)}} (Awa City, Tokushima Prefecture) * {{ill|Kamo Shrine (Susaki)|ja|賀茂神社 (須崎市)}} (Susaki City, Kochi Prefecture) * {{ill|Kamo Shrine (Ino)|ja|賀茂神社 (いの町)}} (Ino Town, Agawa District, Kochi Prefecture) * {{ill|Kamo Shrine (Sakawa)|ja|賀茂神社 (佐川町)}} (Sakawa Town, Takaoka District, Kochi Prefecture) * {{ill|Kamo Shrine (Kuroshio)|ja|賀茂神社 (黒潮町)}} (Kuroshio Town, Hata District, Kochi Prefecture)

== Kamo Shrine ([[:ja:鴨神社|鴨神社]]) == <!--for some reason this has an independent Japanese article [[:ja:鴨神社]]--> '''Kamo Shrine''' (鴨神社) is a Shinto shrine whose name includes the character "Kamo" (鴨).
* [[鴨神社_(玉城町)]] * {{ill|Kamo Shrine (Kamo-sha)|ja|賀茂神社|lt=Kamo Shrine (Kamo-sha)|lt_ja=鴨社}} ({{ill|Kyoto|ja|京都府}}, {{ill|Kyoto City|ja|京都市}}) - Another name for {{ill|Kamo Shrines|ja|賀茂神社}}, which collectively refers to {{ill|Kamigamo Shrine|ja|賀茂別雷神社}} and {{ill|Shimogamo Shrine|ja|賀茂御祖神社}}. * {{ill|Kamo Shrine (Tamaki)|ja|鴨神社 (玉城町)|lt=Kamo Shrine|lt_ja=鴨神社}} ({{ill|Mie Prefecture|ja|三重県}}, {{ill|Tamaki|ja|玉城町}}, Yamagami) * {{ill|Kamo Shrine (Takatuki)|ja|鴨神社 (高槻市)|lt=Kamo Shrine|lt_ja=鴨神社}} ({{ill|Osaka Prefecture|ja|大阪府}}, {{ill|Takatuki|ja|高槻市}}, Akaōji-chō) * {{ill|Kamo Shrine (Kawanishi)|ja|鴨神社 (川西市)|lt=Kamo Shrine|lt_ja=鴨神社}} ({{ill|Hyogo Prefecture|ja|兵庫県}}, {{ill|Kawanishi|ja|川西市}}, Kamo) * {{ill|Kamo Shrine (Tamano)|ja|鴨神社 (玉野市)|lt=Kamo Shrine|lt_ja=鴨神社}} ({{ill|Okayama Prefecture|ja|岡山県}}, {{ill|Tamano|ja|玉野市}}, Nagao) * {{ill|Kamo Shrine (Akaiwa)|ja|鴨神社 (赤磐市)|lt=Kamo Shrine|lt_ja=鴨神社}} (Okayama Prefecture, {{ill|Akaiwa|ja|赤磐市}}, Nibori-nishi) * {{ill|Kamo Shrine (Asakuchi)|ja|鴨神社 (浅口市)|lt=Kamo Shrine|lt_ja=鴨神社}} (Okayama Prefecture, {{ill|Asakuchi|ja|浅口市}}, Kamogata-chō Kamo) * {{ill|Kamo Shrine (Kibichuo)|ja|鴨神社 (吉備中央町)|lt=Kamo Shrine|lt_ja=鴨神社}} (Okayama Prefecture, {{ill|Kibichuo|ja|吉備中央町}}, Kamikamo) * {{ill|Kamo Shrine (Sanyo-Onoda)|ja|鴨神社 (山陽小野田市)|lt=Kamo Shrine|lt_ja=鴨神社}} ({{ill|Yamaguchi Prefecture|ja|山口県}}, {{ill|Sanyo-Onoda|ja|山陽小野田市}}, Asa) * {{ill|Kamo Shrine (Higashimiyoshi)|ja|鴨神社 (東みよし町)|lt=Kamo Shrine|lt_ja=鴨神社}} ({{ill|Tokushima Prefecture|ja|徳島県}}, {{ill|Higashimiyoshi|ja|東みよし町}}, Kamo) * {{ill|Kamo Shrine (Sakaide)|ja|鴨神社 (坂出市)|lt=Kamo Shrine|lt_ja=鴨神社}} - An {{ill|Ancient shrine of Sanuki Province|ja|讃岐国|阿野郡}}. Theories of its location include {{ill|Higashi Kamo Shrine|ja|鴨神社 (坂出市)#東鴨神社|lt=Higashi Kamo Shrine}} and {{ill|Nishi Kamo Shrine|ja|鴨神社 (坂出市)#西鴨神社|lt=Nishi Kamo Shrine}} (both in {{ill|Sakaide|ja|坂出市}}, Kamochō).

'''Kamo Shrine''' (加茂神社・賀茂神社) refers to Shinto shrines with the name "Kamo."
Most of these shrines were branches of the famous {{ill|Kamo Shrines|ja|賀茂神社}}, {{ill|Kamigamo Shrine|ja|賀茂別雷神社}} (Upper Kamo Shrine) and {{ill|Shimogamo Shrine|ja|賀茂御祖神社}} (Lower Kamo Shrine), located in Kyoto. Many shrines are also historically tied to the ancient {{ill|Kamo clan|ja|賀茂県主氏|Kamo clan=Kamo clan|lt=Kamo-no-agatanushi}} clan (賀茂県主氏).
There are multiple spellings so this article will cover all of them

== Kamo Shrine ([[:ja:鴨神社|鴨神社]]) == <!--for some reason this has an independent Japanese article [[:ja:鴨神社]]--> '''Kamo Shrine''' (鴨神社) is a Shinto shrine whose name includes the character "Kamo" (鴨).
* [[鴨神社_(玉城町)]] * {{ill|Kamo Shrine (Kamo-sha)|ja|賀茂神社|lt=Kamo Shrine (Kamo-sha)|lt_ja=鴨社}} ({{ill|Kyoto|ja|京都府}}, {{ill|Kyoto City|ja|京都市}}) - Another name for {{ill|Kamo Shrines|ja|賀茂神社}}, which collectively refers to {{ill|Kamigamo Shrine|ja|賀茂別雷神社}} and {{ill|Shimogamo Shrine|ja|賀茂御祖神社}}. * {{ill|Kamo Shrine (Tamaki)|ja|鴨神社 (玉城町)|lt=Kamo Shrine|lt_ja=鴨神社}} ({{ill|Mie Prefecture|ja|三重県}}, {{ill|Tamaki|ja|玉城町}}, Yamagami) * {{ill|Kamo Shrine (Takatuki)|ja|鴨神社 (高槻市)|lt=Kamo Shrine|lt_ja=鴨神社}} ({{ill|Osaka Prefecture|ja|大阪府}}, {{ill|Takatuki|ja|高槻市}}, Akaōji-chō) * {{ill|Kamo Shrine (Kawanishi)|ja|鴨神社 (川西市)|lt=Kamo Shrine|lt_ja=鴨神社}} ({{ill|Hyogo Prefecture|ja|兵庫県}}, {{ill|Kawanishi|ja|川西市}}, Kamo) * {{ill|Kamo Shrine (Tamano)|ja|鴨神社 (玉野市)|lt=Kamo Shrine|lt_ja=鴨神社}} ({{ill|Okayama Prefecture|ja|岡山県}}, {{ill|Tamano|ja|玉野市}}, Nagao) * {{ill|Kamo Shrine (Akaiwa)|ja|鴨神社 (赤磐市)|lt=Kamo Shrine|lt_ja=鴨神社}} (Okayama Prefecture, {{ill|Akaiwa|ja|赤磐市}}, Nibori-nishi) * {{ill|Kamo Shrine (Asakuchi)|ja|鴨神社 (浅口市)|lt=Kamo Shrine|lt_ja=鴨神社}} (Okayama Prefecture, {{ill|Asakuchi|ja|浅口市}}, Kamogata-chō Kamo) * {{ill|Kamo Shrine (Kibichuo)|ja|鴨神社 (吉備中央町)|lt=Kamo Shrine|lt_ja=鴨神社}} (Okayama Prefecture, {{ill|Kibichuo|ja|吉備中央町}}, Kamikamo) * {{ill|Kamo Shrine (Sanyo-Onoda)|ja|鴨神社 (山陽小野田市)|lt=Kamo Shrine|lt_ja=鴨神社}} ({{ill|Yamaguchi Prefecture|ja|山口県}}, {{ill|Sanyo-Onoda|ja|山陽小野田市}}, Asa) * {{ill|Kamo Shrine (Higashimiyoshi)|ja|鴨神社 (東みよし町)|lt=Kamo Shrine|lt_ja=鴨神社}} ({{ill|Tokushima Prefecture|ja|徳島県}}, {{ill|Higashimiyoshi|ja|東みよし町}}, Kamo) * {{ill|Kamo Shrine (Sakaide)|ja|鴨神社 (坂出市)|lt=Kamo Shrine|lt_ja=鴨神社}} - An {{ill|Ancient shrine of Sanuki Province|ja|讃岐国|阿野郡}}. Theories of its location include {{ill|Higashi Kamo Shrine|ja|鴨神社 (坂出市)#東鴨神社|lt=Higashi Kamo Shrine}} and {{ill|Nishi Kamo Shrine|ja|鴨神社 (坂出市)#西鴨神社|lt=Nishi Kamo Shrine}} (both in {{ill|Sakaide|ja|坂出市}}, Kamochō).

'''Kamo Shrine''' (加茂神社・賀茂神社) refers to Shinto shrines with the name "Kamo."
Most of these shrines were branches of the famous {{ill|Kamo Shrines|ja|賀茂神社}}, {{ill|Kamigamo Shrine|ja|賀茂別雷神社}} (Upper Kamo Shrine) and {{ill|Shimogamo Shrine|ja|賀茂御祖神社}} (Lower Kamo Shrine), located in Kyoto. Many shrines are also historically tied to the ancient {{ill|Kamo clan|ja|賀茂県主氏|Kamo clan=Kamo clan|lt=Kamo-no-agatanushi}} clan (賀茂県主氏).
There are multiple spellings so this article will cover all of them

'''Kamo Shrine''' (加茂神社・賀茂神社) refers to Shinto shrines with the name "Kamo."
Most of these shrines were branches of the famous {{ill|Kamo Shrines|ja|賀茂神社}}, {{ill|Kamigamo Shrine|ja|賀茂別雷神社}} (Upper Kamo Shrine) and {{ill|Shimogamo Shrine|ja|賀茂御祖神社}} (Lower Kamo Shrine), located in Kyoto. Many shrines are also historically tied to the ancient {{ill|Kamo clan|ja|賀茂県主氏|Kamo clan=Kamo clan|lt=Kamo-no-agatanushi}} clan (賀茂県主氏).
There are multiple spellings so this article will cover all of them

'''Kamo Shrine''' (加茂神社・賀茂神社) refers to Shinto shrines with the name "Kamo."
Most of these shrines were branches of the famous {{ill|Kamo Shrines|ja|賀茂神社}}, {{ill|Kamigamo Shrine|ja|賀茂別雷神社}} (Upper Kamo Shrine) and {{ill|Shimogamo Shrine|ja|賀茂御祖神社}} (Lower Kamo Shrine), located in Kyoto. Many shrines are also historically tied to the ancient {{ill|Kamo clan|ja|賀茂県主氏|Kamo clan=Kamo clan|lt=Kamo-no-agatanushi}} clan (賀茂県主氏).
There are multiple spellings so this article will cover all of them

== Kamo Shrine ([[:ja:鴨神社|鴨神社]]) == <!--for some reason this has an independent Japanese article [[:ja:鴨神社]]--> '''Kamo Shrine''' (鴨神社) is a Shinto shrine whose name includes the character "Kamo" (鴨).
* [[鴨神社_(玉城町)]] * {{ill|Kamo Shrine (Kamo-sha)|ja|賀茂神社|lt=Kamo Shrine (Kamo-sha)|lt_ja=鴨社}} ({{ill|Kyoto|ja|京都府}}, {{ill|Kyoto City|ja|京都市}}) - Another name for {{ill|Kamo Shrines|ja|賀茂神社}}, which collectively refers to {{ill|Kamigamo Shrine|ja|賀茂別雷神社}} and {{ill|Shimogamo Shrine|ja|賀茂御祖神社}}. * {{ill|Kamo Shrine (Tamaki)|ja|鴨神社 (玉城町)|lt=Kamo Shrine|lt_ja=鴨神社}} ({{ill|Mie Prefecture|ja|三重県}}, {{ill|Tamaki|ja|玉城町}}, Yamagami) * {{ill|Kamo Shrine (Takatuki)|ja|鴨神社 (高槻市)|lt=Kamo Shrine|lt_ja=鴨神社}} ({{ill|Osaka Prefecture|ja|大阪府}}, {{ill|Takatuki|ja|高槻市}}, Akaōji-chō) * {{ill|Kamo Shrine (Kawanishi)|ja|鴨神社 (川西市)|lt=Kamo Shrine|lt_ja=鴨神社}} ({{ill|Hyogo Prefecture|ja|兵庫県}}, {{ill|Kawanishi|ja|川西市}}, Kamo) * {{ill|Kamo Shrine (Tamano)|ja|鴨神社 (玉野市)|lt=Kamo Shrine|lt_ja=鴨神社}} ({{ill|Okayama Prefecture|ja|岡山県}}, {{ill|Tamano|ja|玉野市}}, Nagao) * {{ill|Kamo Shrine (Akaiwa)|ja|鴨神社 (赤磐市)|lt=Kamo Shrine|lt_ja=鴨神社}} (Okayama Prefecture, {{ill|Akaiwa|ja|赤磐市}}, Nibori-nishi) * {{ill|Kamo Shrine (Asakuchi)|ja|鴨神社 (浅口市)|lt=Kamo Shrine|lt_ja=鴨神社}} (Okayama Prefecture, {{ill|Asakuchi|ja|浅口市}}, Kamogata-chō Kamo) * {{ill|Kamo Shrine (Kibichuo)|ja|鴨神社 (吉備中央町)|lt=Kamo Shrine|lt_ja=鴨神社}} (Okayama Prefecture, {{ill|Kibichuo|ja|吉備中央町}}, Kamikamo) * {{ill|Kamo Shrine (Sanyo-Onoda)|ja|鴨神社 (山陽小野田市)|lt=Kamo Shrine|lt_ja=鴨神社}} ({{ill|Yamaguchi Prefecture|ja|山口県}}, {{ill|Sanyo-Onoda|ja|山陽小野田市}}, Asa) * {{ill|Kamo Shrine (Higashimiyoshi)|ja|鴨神社 (東みよし町)|lt=Kamo Shrine|lt_ja=鴨神社}} ({{ill|Tokushima Prefecture|ja|徳島県}}, {{ill|Higashimiyoshi|ja|東みよし町}}, Kamo) * {{ill|Kamo Shrine (Sakaide)|ja|鴨神社 (坂出市)|lt=Kamo Shrine|lt_ja=鴨神社}} - An {{ill|Ancient shrine of Sanuki Province|ja|讃岐国|阿野郡}}. Theories of its location include {{ill|Higashi Kamo Shrine|ja|鴨神社 (坂出市)#東鴨神社|lt=Higashi Kamo Shrine}} and {{ill|Nishi Kamo Shrine|ja|鴨神社 (坂出市)#西鴨神社|lt=Nishi Kamo Shrine}} (both in {{ill|Sakaide|ja|坂出市}}, Kamochō).

Many shrines are also historically tied to the ancient {{ill|Kamo clan|ja|賀茂県主氏|Kamo clan=Kamo clan|lt=Kamo-no-agatanushi}} clan (賀茂県主氏).

== Kamo Shrine ([[:ja:鴨神社|鴨神社]]) == <!--for some reason this has an independent Japanese article [[:ja:鴨神社]]--> '''Kamo Shrine''' (鴨神社) is a Shinto shrine whose name includes the character "Kamo" (鴨).
* [[鴨神社_(玉城町)]] * {{ill|Kamo Shrine (Kamo-sha)|ja|賀茂神社|lt=Kamo Shrine (Kamo-sha)|lt_ja=鴨社}} ({{ill|Kyoto|ja|京都府}}, {{ill|Kyoto City|ja|京都市}}) - Another name for {{ill|Kamo Shrines|ja|賀茂神社}}, which collectively refers to {{ill|Kamigamo Shrine|ja|賀茂別雷神社}} and {{ill|Shimogamo Shrine|ja|賀茂御祖神社}}. * {{ill|Kamo Shrine (Tamaki)|ja|鴨神社 (玉城町)|lt=Kamo Shrine|lt_ja=鴨神社}} ({{ill|Mie Prefecture|ja|三重県}}, {{ill|Tamaki|ja|玉城町}}, Yamagami) * {{ill|Kamo Shrine (Takatuki)|ja|鴨神社 (高槻市)|lt=Kamo Shrine|lt_ja=鴨神社}} ({{ill|Osaka Prefecture|ja|大阪府}}, {{ill|Takatuki|ja|高槻市}}, Akaōji-chō) * {{ill|Kamo Shrine (Kawanishi)|ja|鴨神社 (川西市)|lt=Kamo Shrine|lt_ja=鴨神社}} ({{ill|Hyogo Prefecture|ja|兵庫県}}, {{ill|Kawanishi|ja|川西市}}, Kamo) * {{ill|Kamo Shrine (Tamano)|ja|鴨神社 (玉野市)|lt=Kamo Shrine|lt_ja=鴨神社}} ({{ill|Okayama Prefecture|ja|岡山県}}, {{ill|Tamano|ja|玉野市}}, Nagao) * {{ill|Kamo Shrine (Akaiwa)|ja|鴨神社 (赤磐市)|lt=Kamo Shrine|lt_ja=鴨神社}} (Okayama Prefecture, {{ill|Akaiwa|ja|赤磐市}}, Nibori-nishi) * {{ill|Kamo Shrine (Asakuchi)|ja|鴨神社 (浅口市)|lt=Kamo Shrine|lt_ja=鴨神社}} (Okayama Prefecture, {{ill|Asakuchi|ja|浅口市}}, Kamogata-chō Kamo) * {{ill|Kamo Shrine (Kibichuo)|ja|鴨神社 (吉備中央町)|lt=Kamo Shrine|lt_ja=鴨神社}} (Okayama Prefecture, {{ill|Kibichuo|ja|吉備中央町}}, Kamikamo) * {{ill|Kamo Shrine (Sanyo-Onoda)|ja|鴨神社 (山陽小野田市)|lt=Kamo Shrine|lt_ja=鴨神社}} ({{ill|Yamaguchi Prefecture|ja|山口県}}, {{ill|Sanyo-Onoda|ja|山陽小野田市}}, Asa) * {{ill|Kamo Shrine (Higashimiyoshi)|ja|鴨神社 (東みよし町)|lt=Kamo Shrine|lt_ja=鴨神社}} ({{ill|Tokushima Prefecture|ja|徳島県}}, {{ill|Higashimiyoshi|ja|東みよし町}}, Kamo) * {{ill|Kamo Shrine (Sakaide)|ja|鴨神社 (坂出市)|lt=Kamo Shrine|lt_ja=鴨神社}} - An {{ill|Ancient shrine of Sanuki Province|ja|讃岐国|阿野郡}}. Theories of its location include {{ill|Higashi Kamo Shrine|ja|鴨神社 (坂出市)#東鴨神社|lt=Higashi Kamo Shrine}} and {{ill|Nishi Kamo Shrine|ja|鴨神社 (坂出市)#西鴨神社|lt=Nishi Kamo Shrine}} (both in {{ill|Sakaide|ja|坂出市}}, Kamochō).

'''Kamo Shrine''' (加茂神社・賀茂神社) refers to Shinto shrines with the name "Kamo."
Most of these shrines were branches of the famous {{ill|Kamo Shrines|ja|賀茂神社}}, {{ill|Kamigamo Shrine|ja|賀茂別雷神社}} (Upper Kamo Shrine) and {{ill|Shimogamo Shrine|ja|賀茂御祖神社}} (Lower Kamo Shrine), located in Kyoto. Many shrines are also historically tied to the ancient {{ill|Kamo clan|ja|賀茂県主氏|Kamo clan=Kamo clan|lt=Kamo-no-agatanushi}} clan (賀茂県主氏).
There are multiple spellings so this article will cover all of them

'''Kamo Shrine''' (加茂神社・賀茂神社) refers to Shinto shrines with the name "Kamo."
Most of these shrines were branches of the famous {{ill|Kamo Shrines|ja|賀茂神社}}, {{ill|Kamigamo Shrine|ja|賀茂別雷神社}} (Upper Kamo Shrine) and {{ill|Shimogamo Shrine|ja|賀茂御祖神社}} (Lower Kamo Shrine), located in Kyoto. Many shrines are also historically tied to the ancient {{ill|Kamo clan|ja|賀茂県主氏|Kamo clan=Kamo clan|lt=Kamo-no-agatanushi}} clan (賀茂県主氏).
There are multiple spellings so this article will cover all of them

== Kamo Shrine ([[:ja:鴨神社|鴨神社]][[Category:Shrine disambiguations]]) == <!--for some reason this has an independent Japanese article [[:ja:鴨神社]][[Category:Shrine disambiguations]]--> '''Kamo Shrine''' (鴨神社) is a Shinto shrine whose name includes the character "Kamo" (鴨).
* [[鴨神社_(玉城町)]] * {{ill|Kamo Shrine (Kamo-sha)|ja|賀茂神社|lt=Kamo Shrine (Kamo-sha)|lt_ja=鴨社}} ({{ill|Kyoto|ja|京都府}}, {{ill|Kyoto City|ja|京都市}}) - Another name for {{ill|Kamo Shrines|ja|賀茂神社}}, which collectively refers to {{ill|Kamigamo Shrine|ja|賀茂別雷神社}} and {{ill|Shimogamo Shrine|ja|賀茂御祖神社}}. * {{ill|Kamo Shrine (Tamaki)|ja|鴨神社 (玉城町)|lt=Kamo Shrine|lt_ja=鴨神社}} ({{ill|Mie Prefecture|ja|三重県}}, {{ill|Tamaki|ja|玉城町}}, Yamagami) * {{ill|Kamo Shrine (Takatuki)|ja|鴨神社 (高槻市)|lt=Kamo Shrine|lt_ja=鴨神社}} ({{ill|Osaka Prefecture|ja|大阪府}}, {{ill|Takatuki|ja|高槻市}}, Akaōji-chō) * {{ill|Kamo Shrine (Kawanishi)|ja|鴨神社 (川西市)|lt=Kamo Shrine|lt_ja=鴨神社}} ({{ill|Hyogo Prefecture|ja|兵庫県}}, {{ill|Kawanishi|ja|川西市}}, Kamo) * {{ill|Kamo Shrine (Tamano)|ja|鴨神社 (玉野市)|lt=Kamo Shrine|lt_ja=鴨神社}} ({{ill|Okayama Prefecture|ja|岡山県}}, {{ill|Tamano|ja|玉野市}}, Nagao) * {{ill|Kamo Shrine (Akaiwa)|ja|鴨神社 (赤磐市)|lt=Kamo Shrine|lt_ja=鴨神社}} (Okayama Prefecture, {{ill|Akaiwa|ja|赤磐市}}, Nibori-nishi) * {{ill|Kamo Shrine (Asakuchi)|ja|鴨神社 (浅口市)|lt=Kamo Shrine|lt_ja=鴨神社}} (Okayama Prefecture, {{ill|Asakuchi|ja|浅口市}}, Kamogata-chō Kamo) * {{ill|Kamo Shrine (Kibichuo)|ja|鴨神社 (吉備中央町)|lt=Kamo Shrine|lt_ja=鴨神社}} (Okayama Prefecture, {{ill|Kibichuo|ja|吉備中央町}}, Kamikamo) * {{ill|Kamo Shrine (Sanyo-Onoda)|ja|鴨神社 (山陽小野田市)|lt=Kamo Shrine|lt_ja=鴨神社}} ({{ill|Yamaguchi Prefecture|ja|山口県}}, {{ill|Sanyo-Onoda|ja|山陽小野田市}}, Asa) * {{ill|Kamo Shrine (Higashimiyoshi)|ja|鴨神社 (東みよし町)|lt=Kamo Shrine|lt_ja=鴨神社}} ({{ill|Tokushima Prefecture|ja|徳島県}}, {{ill|Higashimiyoshi|ja|東みよし町}}, Kamo) * {{ill|Kamo Shrine (Sakaide)|ja|鴨神社 (坂出市)|lt=Kamo Shrine|lt_ja=鴨神社}} - An {{ill|Ancient shrine of Sanuki Province|ja|讃岐国|阿野郡}}. Theories of its location include {{ill|Higashi Kamo Shrine|ja|鴨神社 (坂出市)#東鴨神社|lt=Higashi Kamo Shrine}} and {{ill|Nishi Kamo Shrine|ja|鴨神社 (坂出市)#西鴨神社|lt=Nishi Kamo Shrine}} (both in {{ill|Sakaide|ja|坂出市}}, Kamochō).

== Kamo Shrine ([[:ja:鴨神社|鴨神社]][[Category:Shrine disambiguations]]) == <!--for some reason this has an independent Japanese article [[:ja:鴨神社]][[Category:Shrine disambiguations]]--> '''Kamo Shrine''' (鴨神社) is a Shinto shrine whose name includes the character "Kamo" (鴨).
* [[鴨神社_(玉城町)]] * {{ill|Kamo Shrine (Kamo-sha)|ja|賀茂神社|lt=Kamo Shrine (Kamo-sha)|lt_ja=鴨社}} ({{ill|Kyoto|ja|京都府}}, {{ill|Kyoto City|ja|京都市}}) - Another name for {{ill|Kamo Shrines|ja|賀茂神社}}, which collectively refers to {{ill|Kamigamo Shrine|ja|賀茂別雷神社}} and {{ill|Shimogamo Shrine|ja|賀茂御祖神社}}. * {{ill|Kamo Shrine (Tamaki)|ja|鴨神社 (玉城町)|lt=Kamo Shrine|lt_ja=鴨神社}} ({{ill|Mie Prefecture|ja|三重県}}, {{ill|Tamaki|ja|玉城町}}, Yamagami) * {{ill|Kamo Shrine (Takatuki)|ja|鴨神社 (高槻市)|lt=Kamo Shrine|lt_ja=鴨神社}} ({{ill|Osaka Prefecture|ja|大阪府}}, {{ill|Takatuki|ja|高槻市}}, Akaōji-chō) * {{ill|Kamo Shrine (Kawanishi)|ja|鴨神社 (川西市)|lt=Kamo Shrine|lt_ja=鴨神社}} ({{ill|Hyogo Prefecture|ja|兵庫県}}, {{ill|Kawanishi|ja|川西市}}, Kamo) * {{ill|Kamo Shrine (Tamano)|ja|鴨神社 (玉野市)|lt=Kamo Shrine|lt_ja=鴨神社}} ({{ill|Okayama Prefecture|ja|岡山県}}, {{ill|Tamano|ja|玉野市}}, Nagao) * {{ill|Kamo Shrine (Akaiwa)|ja|鴨神社 (赤磐市)|lt=Kamo Shrine|lt_ja=鴨神社}} (Okayama Prefecture, {{ill|Akaiwa|ja|赤磐市}}, Nibori-nishi) * {{ill|Kamo Shrine (Asakuchi)|ja|鴨神社 (浅口市)|lt=Kamo Shrine|lt_ja=鴨神社}} (Okayama Prefecture, {{ill|Asakuchi|ja|浅口市}}, Kamogata-chō Kamo) * {{ill|Kamo Shrine (Kibichuo)|ja|鴨神社 (吉備中央町)|lt=Kamo Shrine|lt_ja=鴨神社}} (Okayama Prefecture, {{ill|Kibichuo|ja|吉備中央町}}, Kamikamo) * {{ill|Kamo Shrine (Sanyo-Onoda)|ja|鴨神社 (山陽小野田市)|lt=Kamo Shrine|lt_ja=鴨神社}} ({{ill|Yamaguchi Prefecture|ja|山口県}}, {{ill|Sanyo-Onoda|ja|山陽小野田市}}, Asa) * {{ill|Kamo Shrine (Higashimiyoshi)|ja|鴨神社 (東みよし町)|lt=Kamo Shrine|lt_ja=鴨神社}} ({{ill|Tokushima Prefecture|ja|徳島県}}, {{ill|Higashimiyoshi|ja|東みよし町}}, Kamo) * {{ill|Kamo Shrine (Sakaide)|ja|鴨神社 (坂出市)|lt=Kamo Shrine|lt_ja=鴨神社}} - An {{ill|Ancient shrine of Sanuki Province|ja|讃岐国|阿野郡}}. Theories of its location include {{ill|Higashi Kamo Shrine|ja|鴨神社 (坂出市)#東鴨神社|lt=Higashi Kamo Shrine}} and {{ill|Nishi Kamo Shrine|ja|鴨神社 (坂出市)#西鴨神社|lt=Nishi Kamo Shrine}} (both in {{ill|Sakaide|ja|坂出市}}, Kamochō).

'''Kamo Shrine''' (加茂神社・賀茂神社) refers to Shinto shrines with the name "Kamo."
Most of these shrines were branches of the famous {{ill|Kamo Shrines|ja|賀茂神社}}, {{ill|Kamigamo Shrine|ja|賀茂別雷神社}} (Upper Kamo Shrine) and {{ill|Shimogamo Shrine|ja|賀茂御祖神社}} (Lower Kamo Shrine), located in Kyoto. Many shrines are also historically tied to the ancient {{ill|Kamo clan|ja|賀茂県主氏|Kamo clan=Kamo clan|lt=Kamo-no-agatanushi}} clan (賀茂県主氏).
There are multiple spellings so this article will cover all of them

revision.text:
'''Kamo Shrine''' (加茂神社・賀茂神社) refers to Shinto shrines with the name "Kamo."
Most of these shrines were branches of the famous {{ill|Kamo Shrines|ja|賀茂神社}}, {{ill|Kamigamo Shrine|ja|賀茂別雷神社}} (Upper Kamo Shrine) and {{ill|Shimogamo Shrine|ja|賀茂御祖神社}} (Lower Kamo Shrine), located in Kyoto. Many shrines are also historically tied to the ancient {{ill|Kamo clan|ja|賀茂県主氏|Kamo clan=Kamo clan|lt=Kamo-no-agatanushi}} clan (賀茂県主氏).
There are multiple spellings so this article will cover all of them
== Kamo Shrine ([[:ja:加茂神社|加茂神社]][[Category:Shrine disambiguations]]) == === Tohoku === * {{ill|Kamo Shrine (Osaki)|ja|加茂神社 (大崎市)}} (Osaki City, Miyagi Prefecture) * {{ill|Kamo Shrine (Yamagata)|ja|加茂神社 (山形市)}} (Yamagata City, Yamagata Prefecture)

=== Chugoku === * {{ill|Kamo Shrine (Unnan)|ja|加茂神社 (雲南市)}} (Unnan City, Shimane Prefecture) * {{ill|Kamo Shrine (Okayama)|ja|加茂神社 (岡山市)}} (Okayama City, Okayama Prefecture) * {{ill|Kamo Shrine (Tamano)|ja|加茂神社 (玉野市)}} (Tamano City, Okayama Prefecture) * {{ill|Kamo Shrine (Soja)|ja|加茂神社 (総社市)}} (Soja City, Okayama Prefecture) * {{ill|Kamo Shrine (Tsuyama)|ja|加茂神社 (津山市)}} (Tsuyama City, Okayama Prefecture) * {{ill|Kamo Shrine (Maniwa)|ja|加茂神社 (真庭市)}} (Maniwa City, Okayama Prefecture) * {{ill|Kamo Shrine (Kumenan)|ja|加茂神社 (久米南町)}} (Kumenan Town, Okayama Prefecture)

=== Kinki === * {{ill|Kamo Shrine (Omi-Hachiman)|ja|賀茂神社 (近江八幡市)}} (Omi-Hachiman City, Shiga Prefecture) * {{ill|Kamo Shrine (Joyo)|ja|賀茂神社 (城陽市)}} (Joyo City, Kyoto Prefecture) * {{ill|Kamo Shrine (Tatsuno)|ja|賀茂神社 (たつの市)}} (Tatsuno City, Hyogo Prefecture)

== Kamo Shrine ([[:ja:鴨神社|鴨神社]][[Category:Shrine disambiguations]]) == <!--for some reason this has an independent Japanese article [[:ja:鴨神社]][[Category:Shrine disambiguations]]--> '''Kamo Shrine''' (鴨神社) is a Shinto shrine whose name includes the character "Kamo" (鴨).
* [[鴨神社_(玉城町)]] * {{ill|Kamo Shrine (Kamo-sha)|ja|賀茂神社|lt=Kamo Shrine (Kamo-sha)|lt_ja=鴨社}} ({{ill|Kyoto|ja|京都府}}, {{ill|Kyoto City|ja|京都市}}) - Another name for {{ill|Kamo Shrines|ja|賀茂神社}}, which collectively refers to {{ill|Kamigamo Shrine|ja|賀茂別雷神社}} and {{ill|Shimogamo Shrine|ja|賀茂御祖神社}}. * {{ill|Kamo Shrine (Tamaki)|ja|鴨神社 (玉城町)|lt=Kamo Shrine|lt_ja=鴨神社}} ({{ill|Mie Prefecture|ja|三重県}}, {{ill|Tamaki|ja|玉城町}}, Yamagami) * {{ill|Kamo Shrine (Takatuki)|ja|鴨神社 (高槻市)|lt=Kamo Shrine|lt_ja=鴨神社}} ({{ill|Osaka Prefecture|ja|大阪府}}, {{ill|Takatuki|ja|高槻市}}, Akaōji-chō) * {{ill|Kamo Shrine (Kawanishi)|ja|鴨神社 (川西市)|lt=Kamo Shrine|lt_ja=鴨神社}} ({{ill|Hyogo Prefecture|ja|兵庫県}}, {{ill|Kawanishi|ja|川西市}}, Kamo) * {{ill|Kamo Shrine (Tamano)|ja|鴨神社 (玉野市)|lt=Kamo Shrine|lt_ja=鴨神社}} ({{ill|Okayama Prefecture|ja|岡山県}}, {{ill|Tamano|ja|玉野市}}, Nagao) * {{ill|Kamo Shrine (Akaiwa)|ja|鴨神社 (赤磐市)|lt=Kamo Shrine|lt_ja=鴨神社}} (Okayama Prefecture, {{ill|Akaiwa|ja|赤磐市}}, Nibori-nishi) * {{ill|Kamo Shrine (Asakuchi)|ja|鴨神社 (浅口市)|lt=Kamo Shrine|lt_ja=鴨神社}} (Okayama Prefecture, {{ill|Asakuchi|ja|浅口市}}, Kamogata-chō Kamo) * {{ill|Kamo Shrine (Kibichuo)|ja|鴨神社 (吉備中央町)|lt=Kamo Shrine|lt_ja=鴨神社}} (Okayama Prefecture, {{ill|Kibichuo|ja|吉備中央町}}, Kamikamo) * {{ill|Kamo Shrine (Sanyo-Onoda)|ja|鴨神社 (山陽小野田市)|lt=Kamo Shrine|lt_ja=鴨神社}} ({{ill|Yamaguchi Prefecture|ja|山口県}}, {{ill|Sanyo-Onoda|ja|山陽小野田市}}, Asa) * {{ill|Kamo Shrine (Higashimiyoshi)|ja|鴨神社 (東みよし町)|lt=Kamo Shrine|lt_ja=鴨神社}} ({{ill|Tokushima Prefecture|ja|徳島県}}, {{ill|Higashimiyoshi|ja|東みよし町}}, Kamo) * {{ill|Kamo Shrine (Sakaide)|ja|鴨神社 (坂出市)|lt=Kamo Shrine|lt_ja=鴨神社}} - An {{ill|Ancient shrine of Sanuki Province|ja|讃岐国|阿野郡}}. Theories of its location include {{ill|Higashi Kamo Shrine|ja|鴨神社 (坂出市)#東鴨神社|lt=Higashi Kamo Shrine}} and {{ill|Nishi Kamo Shrine|ja|鴨神社 (坂出市)#西鴨神社|lt=Nishi Kamo Shrine}} (both in {{ill|Sakaide|ja|坂出市}}, Kamochō).
== Related Articles ==

Most of these shrines were branches of the famous {{ill|Kamo Shrines|ja|賀茂神社}}, {{ill|Kamigamo Shrine|ja|賀茂別雷神社}} (Upper Kamo Shrine) and {{ill|Shimogamo Shrine|ja|賀茂御祖神社}} (Lower Kamo Shrine), located in Kyoto. Many shrines are also historically tied to the ancient {{ill|Kamo clan|ja|賀茂県主氏|Kamo clan=Kamo clan|lt=Kamo-no-agatanushi}} clan (賀茂県主氏).

== Kamo Shrine ([[:ja:鴨神社|鴨神社]][[Category:Shrine disambiguations]]) == <!--for some reason this has an independent Japanese article [[:ja:鴨神社]][[Category:Shrine disambiguations]]--> '''Kamo Shrine''' (鴨神社) is a Shinto shrine whose name includes the character "Kamo" (鴨).
* [[鴨神社_(玉城町)]] * {{ill|Kamo Shrine (Kamo-sha)|ja|賀茂神社|lt=Kamo Shrine (Kamo-sha)|lt_ja=鴨社}} ({{ill|Kyoto|ja|京都府}}, {{ill|Kyoto City|ja|京都市}}) - Another name for {{ill|Kamo Shrines|ja|賀茂神社}}, which collectively refers to {{ill|Kamigamo Shrine|ja|賀茂別雷神社}} and {{ill|Shimogamo Shrine|ja|賀茂御祖神社}}. * {{ill|Kamo Shrine (Tamaki)|ja|鴨神社 (玉城町)|lt=Kamo Shrine|lt_ja=鴨神社}} ({{ill|Mie Prefecture|ja|三重県}}, {{ill|Tamaki|ja|玉城町}}, Yamagami) * {{ill|Kamo Shrine (Takatuki)|ja|鴨神社 (高槻市)|lt=Kamo Shrine|lt_ja=鴨神社}} ({{ill|Osaka Prefecture|ja|大阪府}}, {{ill|Takatuki|ja|高槻市}}, Akaōji-chō) * {{ill|Kamo Shrine (Kawanishi)|ja|鴨神社 (川西市)|lt=Kamo Shrine|lt_ja=鴨神社}} ({{ill|Hyogo Prefecture|ja|兵庫県}}, {{ill|Kawanishi|ja|川西市}}, Kamo) * {{ill|Kamo Shrine (Tamano)|ja|鴨神社 (玉野市)|lt=Kamo Shrine|lt_ja=鴨神社}} ({{ill|Okayama Prefecture|ja|岡山県}}, {{ill|Tamano|ja|玉野市}}, Nagao) * {{ill|Kamo Shrine (Akaiwa)|ja|鴨神社 (赤磐市)|lt=Kamo Shrine|lt_ja=鴨神社}} (Okayama Prefecture, {{ill|Akaiwa|ja|赤磐市}}, Nibori-nishi) * {{ill|Kamo Shrine (Asakuchi)|ja|鴨神社 (浅口市)|lt=Kamo Shrine|lt_ja=鴨神社}} (Okayama Prefecture, {{ill|Asakuchi|ja|浅口市}}, Kamogata-chō Kamo) * {{ill|Kamo Shrine (Kibichuo)|ja|鴨神社 (吉備中央町)|lt=Kamo Shrine|lt_ja=鴨神社}} (Okayama Prefecture, {{ill|Kibichuo|ja|吉備中央町}}, Kamikamo) * {{ill|Kamo Shrine (Sanyo-Onoda)|ja|鴨神社 (山陽小野田市)|lt=Kamo Shrine|lt_ja=鴨神社}} ({{ill|Yamaguchi Prefecture|ja|山口県}}, {{ill|Sanyo-Onoda|ja|山陽小野田市}}, Asa) * {{ill|Kamo Shrine (Higashimiyoshi)|ja|鴨神社 (東みよし町)|lt=Kamo Shrine|lt_ja=鴨神社}} ({{ill|Tokushima Prefecture|ja|徳島県}}, {{ill|Higashimiyoshi|ja|東みよし町}}, Kamo) * {{ill|Kamo Shrine (Sakaide)|ja|鴨神社 (坂出市)|lt=Kamo Shrine|lt_ja=鴨神社}} - An {{ill|Ancient shrine of Sanuki Province|ja|讃岐国|阿野郡}}. Theories of its location include {{ill|Higashi Kamo Shrine|ja|鴨神社 (坂出市)#東鴨神社|lt=Higashi Kamo Shrine}} and {{ill|Nishi Kamo Shrine|ja|鴨神社 (坂出市)#西鴨神社|lt=Nishi Kamo Shrine}} (both in {{ill|Sakaide|ja|坂出市}}, Kamochō).

'''Kamo Shrine''' (加茂神社・賀茂神社) refers to Shinto shrines with the name "Kamo."
Most of these shrines were branches of the famous {{ill|Kamo Shrines|ja|賀茂神社}}, {{ill|Kamigamo Shrine|ja|賀茂別雷神社}} (Upper Kamo Shrine) and {{ill|Shimogamo Shrine|ja|賀茂御祖神社}} (Lower Kamo Shrine), located in Kyoto. Many shrines are also historically tied to the ancient {{ill|Kamo clan|ja|賀茂県主氏|Kamo clan=Kamo clan|lt=Kamo-no-agatanushi}} clan (賀茂県主氏).
There are multiple spellings so this article will cover all of them

revision.text:
'''Kamo Shrine''' (加茂神社・賀茂神社) refers to Shinto shrines with the name "Kamo."
Most of these shrines were branches of the famous {{ill|Kamo Shrines|ja|賀茂神社}}, {{ill|Kamigamo Shrine|ja|賀茂別雷神社}} (Upper Kamo Shrine) and {{ill|Shimogamo Shrine|ja|賀茂御祖神社}} (Lower Kamo Shrine), located in Kyoto. Many shrines are also historically tied to the ancient {{ill|Kamo clan|ja|賀茂県主氏|Kamo clan=Kamo clan|lt=Kamo-no-agatanushi}} clan (賀茂県主氏).
There are multiple spellings so this article will cover all of them
== Kamo Shrine ([[:ja:加茂神社|加茂神社]][[Category:Shrine disambiguations]]) == === Tohoku === * {{ill|Kamo Shrine (Osaki)|ja|加茂神社 (大崎市)}} (Osaki City, Miyagi Prefecture) * {{ill|Kamo Shrine (Yamagata)|ja|加茂神社 (山形市)}} (Yamagata City, Yamagata Prefecture)

=== Kinki === * {{ill|Kamo Shrine (Toba)|ja|加茂神社 (鳥羽市)}} (Toba City, Mie Prefecture) * {{ill|Kamo Shrine (Koka)|ja|加茂神社 (甲賀市)}} (Koka City, Shiga Prefecture) * {{ill|Kamo Shrine (Takashima)|ja|加茂神社 (高島市)}} (Takashima City, Shiga Prefecture) * {{ill|Kamo Shrine (Maibara)|ja|加茂神社 (米原市)}} (Maibara City, Shiga Prefecture) * {{ill|Kamo Shrine (Hannan)|ja|加茂神社 (阪南市)}} (Hannan City, Osaka Prefecture) * {{ill|Kamo Shrine (Himeji)|ja|加茂神社 (姫路市)}} (Himeji City, Hyogo Prefecture) * {{ill|Kamo Shrine (Kakogawa)|ja|加茂神社 (加古川市)}} (Kakogawa City, Hyogo Prefecture) * {{ill|Kamo Shrine (Tanbasasayama)|ja|加茂神社 (丹波篠山市)}} (Tanbasasayama City, Hyogo Prefecture) * {{ill|Kamo Shrine (Kainan)|ja|加茂神社 (海南市)}} (Kainan City, Wakayama Prefecture) * {{ill|Kamo Shrine (Kinokawa)|ja|加茂神社 (紀の川市)}} (Kinokawa City, Wakayama Prefecture)
=== Chugoku === * {{ill|Kamo Shrine (Unnan)|ja|加茂神社 (雲南市)}} (Unnan City, Shimane Prefecture) * {{ill|Kamo Shrine (Okayama)|ja|加茂神社 (岡山市)}} (Okayama City, Okayama Prefecture) * {{ill|Kamo Shrine (Tamano)|ja|加茂神社 (玉野市)}} (Tamano City, Okayama Prefecture) * {{ill|Kamo Shrine (Soja)|ja|加茂神社 (総社市)}} (Soja City, Okayama Prefecture) * {{ill|Kamo Shrine (Tsuyama)|ja|加茂神社 (津山市)}} (Tsuyama City, Okayama Prefecture) * {{ill|Kamo Shrine (Maniwa)|ja|加茂神社 (真庭市)}} (Maniwa City, Okayama Prefecture) * {{ill|Kamo Shrine (Kumenan)|ja|加茂神社 (久米南町)}} (Kumenan Town, Okayama Prefecture)

=== Chubu === * {{ill|Kamo Shrine (Kahoku)|ja|賀茂神社 (かほく市)}} (Kahoku City, Ishikawa Prefecture) * {{ill|Kamo Shrine (Fukui)|ja|賀茂神社 (福井市)}} (Fukui City, Fukui Prefecture) * {{ill|Kamo Shrine (Mori Town)|ja|賀茂神社 (周智郡森町)}} (Mori Town, Shuchi District, Shizuoka Prefecture) * {{ill|Kamo Shrine (Minamiizu)|ja|賀茂神社 (南伊豆町)}} (Minamiizu Town, Shizuoka Prefecture) - Kabata Kamo Shrine, a Shikinai Shrine in Kamo District, Shizuoka Prefecture. * {{ill|Kamo Shrine (Toyohashi)|ja|賀茂神社 (豊橋市)}} (Toyohashi City, Aichi Prefecture) * {{ill|Kamo Shrine (Ichinomiya)|ja|賀茂神社 (一宮市)}} (Ichinomiya City, Aichi Prefecture)
=== Kinki === * {{ill|Kamo Shrine (Omi-Hachiman)|ja|賀茂神社 (近江八幡市)}} (Omi-Hachiman City, Shiga Prefecture) * {{ill|Kamo Shrine (Joyo)|ja|賀茂神社 (城陽市)}} (Joyo City, Kyoto Prefecture) * {{ill|Kamo Shrine (Tatsuno)|ja|賀茂神社 (たつの市)}} (Tatsuno City, Hyogo Prefecture)

== Kamo Shrine ([[:ja:鴨神社|鴨神社]][[Category:Shrine disambiguations]]) == <!--for some reason this has an independent Japanese article [[:ja:鴨神社]][[Category:Shrine disambiguations]]--> '''Kamo Shrine''' (鴨神社) is a Shinto shrine whose name includes the character "Kamo" (鴨).
* [[鴨神社_(玉城町)]] * {{ill|Kamo Shrine (Kamo-sha)|ja|賀茂神社|lt=Kamo Shrine (Kamo-sha)|lt_ja=鴨社}} ({{ill|Kyoto|ja|京都府}}, {{ill|Kyoto City|ja|京都市}}) - Another name for {{ill|Kamo Shrines|ja|賀茂神社}}, which collectively refers to {{ill|Kamigamo Shrine|ja|賀茂別雷神社}} and {{ill|Shimogamo Shrine|ja|賀茂御祖神社}}. * {{ill|Kamo Shrine (Tamaki)|ja|鴨神社 (玉城町)|lt=Kamo Shrine|lt_ja=鴨神社}} ({{ill|Mie Prefecture|ja|三重県}}, {{ill|Tamaki|ja|玉城町}}, Yamagami) * {{ill|Kamo Shrine (Takatuki)|ja|鴨神社 (高槻市)|lt=Kamo Shrine|lt_ja=鴨神社}} ({{ill|Osaka Prefecture|ja|大阪府}}, {{ill|Takatuki|ja|高槻市}}, Akaōji-chō) * {{ill|Kamo Shrine (Kawanishi)|ja|鴨神社 (川西市)|lt=Kamo Shrine|lt_ja=鴨神社}} ({{ill|Hyogo Prefecture|ja|兵庫県}}, {{ill|Kawanishi|ja|川西市}}, Kamo) * {{ill|Kamo Shrine (Tamano)|ja|鴨神社 (玉野市)|lt=Kamo Shrine|lt_ja=鴨神社}} ({{ill|Okayama Prefecture|ja|岡山県}}, {{ill|Tamano|ja|玉野市}}, Nagao) * {{ill|Kamo Shrine (Akaiwa)|ja|鴨神社 (赤磐市)|lt=Kamo Shrine|lt_ja=鴨神社}} (Okayama Prefecture, {{ill|Akaiwa|ja|赤磐市}}, Nibori-nishi) * {{ill|Kamo Shrine (Asakuchi)|ja|鴨神社 (浅口市)|lt=Kamo Shrine|lt_ja=鴨神社}} (Okayama Prefecture, {{ill|Asakuchi|ja|浅口市}}, Kamogata-chō Kamo) * {{ill|Kamo Shrine (Kibichuo)|ja|鴨神社 (吉備中央町)|lt=Kamo Shrine|lt_ja=鴨神社}} (Okayama Prefecture, {{ill|Kibichuo|ja|吉備中央町}}, Kamikamo) * {{ill|Kamo Shrine (Sanyo-Onoda)|ja|鴨神社 (山陽小野田市)|lt=Kamo Shrine|lt_ja=鴨神社}} ({{ill|Yamaguchi Prefecture|ja|山口県}}, {{ill|Sanyo-Onoda|ja|山陽小野田市}}, Asa) * {{ill|Kamo Shrine (Higashimiyoshi)|ja|鴨神社 (東みよし町)|lt=Kamo Shrine|lt_ja=鴨神社}} ({{ill|Tokushima Prefecture|ja|徳島県}}, {{ill|Higashimiyoshi|ja|東みよし町}}, Kamo) * {{ill|Kamo Shrine (Sakaide)|ja|鴨神社 (坂出市)|lt=Kamo Shrine|lt_ja=鴨神社}} - An {{ill|Ancient shrine of Sanuki Province|ja|讃岐国|阿野郡}}. Theories of its location include {{ill|Higashi Kamo Shrine|ja|鴨神社 (坂出市)#東鴨神社|lt=Higashi Kamo Shrine}} and {{ill|Nishi Kamo Shrine|ja|鴨神社 (坂出市)#西鴨神社|lt=Nishi Kamo Shrine}} (both in {{ill|Sakaide|ja|坂出市}}, Kamochō).
== Related Articles ==

'''Kamo Shrine''' (加茂神社・賀茂神社) refers to Shinto shrines with the name "Kamo."
Most of these shrines were branches of the famous {{ill|Kamo Shrines|ja|賀茂神社}}, {{ill|Kamigamo Shrine|ja|賀茂別雷神社}} (Upper Kamo Shrine) and {{ill|Shimogamo Shrine|ja|賀茂御祖神社}} (Lower Kamo Shrine), located in Kyoto. Many shrines are also historically tied to the ancient {{ill|Kamo clan|ja|賀茂県主氏|Kamo clan=Kamo clan|lt=Kamo-no-agatanushi}} clan (賀茂県主氏).
There are multiple spellings so this article will cover all of them

== Kamo Shrine ([[:ja:鴨神社|鴨神社]][[Category:Shrine disambiguations]]) == <!--for some reason this has an independent Japanese article [[:ja:鴨神社]][[Category:Shrine disambiguations]]--> '''Kamo Shrine''' (鴨神社) is a Shinto shrine whose name includes the character "Kamo" (鴨).
* [[鴨神社_(玉城町)]] * {{ill|Kamo Shrine (Kamo-sha)|ja|賀茂神社|lt=Kamo Shrine (Kamo-sha)|lt_ja=鴨社}} ({{ill|Kyoto|ja|京都府}}, {{ill|Kyoto City|ja|京都市}}) - Another name for {{ill|Kamo Shrines|ja|賀茂神社}}, which collectively refers to {{ill|Kamigamo Shrine|ja|賀茂別雷神社}} and {{ill|Shimogamo Shrine|ja|賀茂御祖神社}}. * {{ill|Kamo Shrine (Tamaki)|ja|鴨神社 (玉城町)|lt=Kamo Shrine|lt_ja=鴨神社}} ({{ill|Mie Prefecture|ja|三重県}}, {{ill|Tamaki|ja|玉城町}}, Yamagami) * {{ill|Kamo Shrine (Takatuki)|ja|鴨神社 (高槻市)|lt=Kamo Shrine|lt_ja=鴨神社}} ({{ill|Osaka Prefecture|ja|大阪府}}, {{ill|Takatuki|ja|高槻市}}, Akaōji-chō) * {{ill|Kamo Shrine (Kawanishi)|ja|鴨神社 (川西市)|lt=Kamo Shrine|lt_ja=鴨神社}} ({{ill|Hyogo Prefecture|ja|兵庫県}}, {{ill|Kawanishi|ja|川西市}}, Kamo) * {{ill|Kamo Shrine (Tamano)|ja|鴨神社 (玉野市)|lt=Kamo Shrine|lt_ja=鴨神社}} ({{ill|Okayama Prefecture|ja|岡山県}}, {{ill|Tamano|ja|玉野市}}, Nagao) * {{ill|Kamo Shrine (Akaiwa)|ja|鴨神社 (赤磐市)|lt=Kamo Shrine|lt_ja=鴨神社}} (Okayama Prefecture, {{ill|Akaiwa|ja|赤磐市}}, Nibori-nishi) * {{ill|Kamo Shrine (Asakuchi)|ja|鴨神社 (浅口市)|lt=Kamo Shrine|lt_ja=鴨神社}} (Okayama Prefecture, {{ill|Asakuchi|ja|浅口市}}, Kamogata-chō Kamo) * {{ill|Kamo Shrine (Kibichuo)|ja|鴨神社 (吉備中央町)|lt=Kamo Shrine|lt_ja=鴨神社}} (Okayama Prefecture, {{ill|Kibichuo|ja|吉備中央町}}, Kamikamo) * {{ill|Kamo Shrine (Sanyo-Onoda)|ja|鴨神社 (山陽小野田市)|lt=Kamo Shrine|lt_ja=鴨神社}} ({{ill|Yamaguchi Prefecture|ja|山口県}}, {{ill|Sanyo-Onoda|ja|山陽小野田市}}, Asa) * {{ill|Kamo Shrine (Higashimiyoshi)|ja|鴨神社 (東みよし町)|lt=Kamo Shrine|lt_ja=鴨神社}} ({{ill|Tokushima Prefecture|ja|徳島県}}, {{ill|Higashimiyoshi|ja|東みよし町}}, Kamo) * {{ill|Kamo Shrine (Sakaide)|ja|鴨神社 (坂出市)|lt=Kamo Shrine|lt_ja=鴨神社}} - An {{ill|Ancient shrine of Sanuki Province|ja|讃岐国|阿野郡}}. Theories of its location include {{ill|Higashi Kamo Shrine|ja|鴨神社 (坂出市)#東鴨神社|lt=Higashi Kamo Shrine}} and {{ill|Nishi Kamo Shrine|ja|鴨神社 (坂出市)#西鴨神社|lt=Nishi Kamo Shrine}} (both in {{ill|Sakaide|ja|坂出市}}, Kamochō).

'''Kamo Shrine''' (加茂神社・賀茂神社) refers to Shinto shrines with the name "Kamo."
Most of these shrines were branches of the famous {{ill|Kamo Shrines|ja|賀茂神社}}, {{ill|Kamigamo Shrine|ja|賀茂別雷神社}} (Upper Kamo Shrine) and {{ill|Shimogamo Shrine|ja|賀茂御祖神社}} (Lower Kamo Shrine), located in Kyoto. Many shrines are also historically tied to the ancient {{ill|Kamo clan|ja|賀茂県主氏|Kamo clan=Kamo clan|lt=Kamo-no-agatanushi}} clan (賀茂県主氏).
There are multiple spellings so this article will cover all of them

'''Kamo Shrine''' (加茂神社・賀茂神社) refers to Shinto shrines with the name "Kamo."
Most of these shrines were branches of the famous {{ill|Kamo Shrines|ja|賀茂神社}}, {{ill|Kamigamo Shrine|ja|賀茂別雷神社}} (Upper Kamo Shrine) and {{ill|Shimogamo Shrine|ja|賀茂御祖神社}} (Lower Kamo Shrine), located in Kyoto. Many shrines are also historically tied to the ancient {{ill|Kamo clan|ja|賀茂県主氏|Kamo clan=Kamo clan|lt=Kamo-no-agatanushi}} clan (賀茂県主氏).
There are multiple spellings so this article will cover all of them

== Kamo Shrine ([[:ja:鴨神社|鴨神社]][[Category:Shrine disambiguations]]) == <!--for some reason this has an independent Japanese article [[:ja:鴨神社]][[Category:Shrine disambiguations]]--> '''Kamo Shrine''' (鴨神社) is a Shinto shrine whose name includes the character "Kamo" (鴨).
* [[鴨神社_(玉城町)]] * {{ill|Kamo Shrine (Kamo-sha)|ja|賀茂神社|lt=Kamo Shrine (Kamo-sha)|lt_ja=鴨社}} ({{ill|Kyoto|ja|京都府}}, {{ill|Kyoto City|ja|京都市}}) - Another name for {{ill|Kamo Shrines|ja|賀茂神社}}, which collectively refers to {{ill|Kamigamo Shrine|ja|賀茂別雷神社}} and {{ill|Shimogamo Shrine|ja|賀茂御祖神社}}. * {{ill|Kamo Shrine (Tamaki)|ja|鴨神社 (玉城町)|lt=Kamo Shrine|lt_ja=鴨神社}} ({{ill|Mie Prefecture|ja|三重県}}, {{ill|Tamaki|ja|玉城町}}, Yamagami) * {{ill|Kamo Shrine (Takatuki)|ja|鴨神社 (高槻市)|lt=Kamo Shrine|lt_ja=鴨神社}} ({{ill|Osaka Prefecture|ja|大阪府}}, {{ill|Takatuki|ja|高槻市}}, Akaōji-chō) * {{ill|Kamo Shrine (Kawanishi)|ja|鴨神社 (川西市)|lt=Kamo Shrine|lt_ja=鴨神社}} ({{ill|Hyogo Prefecture|ja|兵庫県}}, {{ill|Kawanishi|ja|川西市}}, Kamo) * {{ill|Kamo Shrine (Tamano)|ja|鴨神社 (玉野市)|lt=Kamo Shrine|lt_ja=鴨神社}} ({{ill|Okayama Prefecture|ja|岡山県}}, {{ill|Tamano|ja|玉野市}}, Nagao) * {{ill|Kamo Shrine (Akaiwa)|ja|鴨神社 (赤磐市)|lt=Kamo Shrine|lt_ja=鴨神社}} (Okayama Prefecture, {{ill|Akaiwa|ja|赤磐市}}, Nibori-nishi) * {{ill|Kamo Shrine (Asakuchi)|ja|鴨神社 (浅口市)|lt=Kamo Shrine|lt_ja=鴨神社}} (Okayama Prefecture, {{ill|Asakuchi|ja|浅口市}}, Kamogata-chō Kamo) * {{ill|Kamo Shrine (Kibichuo)|ja|鴨神社 (吉備中央町)|lt=Kamo Shrine|lt_ja=鴨神社}} (Okayama Prefecture, {{ill|Kibichuo|ja|吉備中央町}}, Kamikamo) * {{ill|Kamo Shrine (Sanyo-Onoda)|ja|鴨神社 (山陽小野田市)|lt=Kamo Shrine|lt_ja=鴨神社}} ({{ill|Yamaguchi Prefecture|ja|山口県}}, {{ill|Sanyo-Onoda|ja|山陽小野田市}}, Asa) * {{ill|Kamo Shrine (Higashimiyoshi)|ja|鴨神社 (東みよし町)|lt=Kamo Shrine|lt_ja=鴨神社}} ({{ill|Tokushima Prefecture|ja|徳島県}}, {{ill|Higashimiyoshi|ja|東みよし町}}, Kamo) * {{ill|Kamo Shrine (Sakaide)|ja|鴨神社 (坂出市)|lt=Kamo Shrine|lt_ja=鴨神社}} - An {{ill|Ancient shrine of Sanuki Province|ja|讃岐国|阿野郡}}. Theories of its location include {{ill|Higashi Kamo Shrine|ja|鴨神社 (坂出市)#東鴨神社|lt=Higashi Kamo Shrine}} and {{ill|Nishi Kamo Shrine|ja|鴨神社 (坂出市)#西鴨神社|lt=Nishi Kamo Shrine}} (both in {{ill|Sakaide|ja|坂出市}}, Kamochō).

Most of these shrines were branches of the famous {{ill|Kamo Shrines|ja|賀茂神社}}, {{ill|Kamigamo Shrine|ja|賀茂別雷神社}} (Upper Kamo Shrine) and {{ill|Shimogamo Shrine|ja|賀茂御祖神社}} (Lower Kamo Shrine), located in Kyoto. Many shrines are also historically tied to the ancient {{ill|Kamo clan|ja|賀茂県主氏|Kamo clan=Kamo clan|lt=Kamo-no-agatanushi}} clan (賀茂県主氏).
There are multiple spellings so this article will cover all of them

'''Kamo Shrine''' (加茂神社・賀茂神社) refers to Shinto shrines with the name "Kamo."
Most of these shrines were branches of the famous {{ill|Kamo Shrines|ja|賀茂神社}}, {{ill|Kamigamo Shrine|ja|賀茂別雷神社}} (Upper Kamo Shrine) and {{ill|Shimogamo Shrine|ja|賀茂御祖神社}} (Lower Kamo Shrine), located in Kyoto. Many shrines are also historically tied to the ancient {{ill|Kamo clan|ja|賀茂県主氏|Kamo clan=Kamo clan|lt=Kamo-no-agatanushi}} clan (賀茂県主氏).
There are multiple spellings so this article will cover all of them

'''Kamo Shrine''' (加茂神社・賀茂神社) refers to Shinto shrines with the name "Kamo."
Most of these shrines were branches of the famous {{ill|Kamo Shrines|ja|賀茂神社}}, {{ill|Kamigamo Shrine|ja|賀茂別雷神社}} (Upper Kamo Shrine) and {{ill|Shimogamo Shrine|ja|賀茂御祖神社}} (Lower Kamo Shrine), located in Kyoto. Many shrines are also historically tied to the ancient {{ill|Kamo clan|ja|賀茂県主氏|Kamo clan=Kamo clan|lt=Kamo-no-agatanushi}} clan (賀茂県主氏).
There are multiple spellings so this article will cover all of them

== Kamo Shrine ([[:ja:鴨神社|鴨神社]][[Category:Shrine disambiguations]]) == <!--for some reason this has an independent Japanese article [[:ja:鴨神社]][[Category:Shrine disambiguations]]--> '''Kamo Shrine''' (鴨神社) is a Shinto shrine whose name includes the character "Kamo" (鴨).
* [[鴨神社_(玉城町)]] * {{ill|Kamo Shrine (Kamo-sha)|ja|賀茂神社|lt=Kamo Shrine (Kamo-sha)|lt_ja=鴨社}} ({{ill|Kyoto|ja|京都府}}, {{ill|Kyoto City|ja|京都市}}) - Another name for {{ill|Kamo Shrines|ja|賀茂神社}}, which collectively refers to {{ill|Kamigamo Shrine|ja|賀茂別雷神社}} and {{ill|Shimogamo Shrine|ja|賀茂御祖神社}}. * {{ill|Kamo Shrine (Tamaki)|ja|鴨神社 (玉城町)|lt=Kamo Shrine|lt_ja=鴨神社}} ({{ill|Mie Prefecture|ja|三重県}}, {{ill|Tamaki|ja|玉城町}}, Yamagami) * {{ill|Kamo Shrine (Takatuki)|ja|鴨神社 (高槻市)|lt=Kamo Shrine|lt_ja=鴨神社}} ({{ill|Osaka Prefecture|ja|大阪府}}, {{ill|Takatuki|ja|高槻市}}, Akaōji-chō) * {{ill|Kamo Shrine (Kawanishi)|ja|鴨神社 (川西市)|lt=Kamo Shrine|lt_ja=鴨神社}} ({{ill|Hyogo Prefecture|ja|兵庫県}}, {{ill|Kawanishi|ja|川西市}}, Kamo) * {{ill|Kamo Shrine (Tamano)|ja|鴨神社 (玉野市)|lt=Kamo Shrine|lt_ja=鴨神社}} ({{ill|Okayama Prefecture|ja|岡山県}}, {{ill|Tamano|ja|玉野市}}, Nagao) * {{ill|Kamo Shrine (Akaiwa)|ja|鴨神社 (赤磐市)|lt=Kamo Shrine|lt_ja=鴨神社}} (Okayama Prefecture, {{ill|Akaiwa|ja|赤磐市}}, Nibori-nishi) * {{ill|Kamo Shrine (Asakuchi)|ja|鴨神社 (浅口市)|lt=Kamo Shrine|lt_ja=鴨神社}} (Okayama Prefecture, {{ill|Asakuchi|ja|浅口市}}, Kamogata-chō Kamo) * {{ill|Kamo Shrine (Kibichuo)|ja|鴨神社 (吉備中央町)|lt=Kamo Shrine|lt_ja=鴨神社}} (Okayama Prefecture, {{ill|Kibichuo|ja|吉備中央町}}, Kamikamo) * {{ill|Kamo Shrine (Sanyo-Onoda)|ja|鴨神社 (山陽小野田市)|lt=Kamo Shrine|lt_ja=鴨神社}} ({{ill|Yamaguchi Prefecture|ja|山口県}}, {{ill|Sanyo-Onoda|ja|山陽小野田市}}, Asa) * {{ill|Kamo Shrine (Higashimiyoshi)|ja|鴨神社 (東みよし町)|lt=Kamo Shrine|lt_ja=鴨神社}} ({{ill|Tokushima Prefecture|ja|徳島県}}, {{ill|Higashimiyoshi|ja|東みよし町}}, Kamo) * {{ill|Kamo Shrine (Sakaide)|ja|鴨神社 (坂出市)|lt=Kamo Shrine|lt_ja=鴨神社}} - An {{ill|Ancient shrine of Sanuki Province|ja|讃岐国|阿野郡}}. Theories of its location include {{ill|Higashi Kamo Shrine|ja|鴨神社 (坂出市)#東鴨神社|lt=Higashi Kamo Shrine}} and {{ill|Nishi Kamo Shrine|ja|鴨神社 (坂出市)#西鴨神社|lt=Nishi Kamo Shrine}} (both in {{ill|Sakaide|ja|坂出市}}, Kamochō).

Most of these shrines were branches of the famous {{ill|Kamo Shrines|ja|賀茂神社}}, {{ill|Kamigamo Shrine|ja|賀茂別雷神社}} (Upper Kamo Shrine) and {{ill|Shimogamo Shrine|ja|賀茂御祖神社}} (Lower Kamo Shrine), located in Kyoto. Many shrines are also historically tied to the ancient {{ill|Kamo clan|ja|賀茂県主氏|Kamo clan=Kamo clan|lt=Kamo-no-agatanushi}} clan (賀茂県主氏).
There are multiple spellings so this article will cover all of them

== Kamo Shrine ([[:ja:鴨神社|鴨神社]][[Category:Shrine disambiguations]]) == <!--for some reason this has an independent Japanese article [[:ja:鴨神社]][[Category:Shrine disambiguations]]--> '''Kamo Shrine''' (鴨神社) is a Shinto shrine whose name includes the character "Kamo" (鴨).
* [[鴨神社_(玉城町)]] * {{ill|Kamo Shrine (Kamo-sha)|ja|賀茂神社|lt=Kamo Shrine (Kamo-sha)|lt_ja=鴨社}} ({{ill|Kyoto|ja|京都府}}, {{ill|Kyoto City|ja|京都市}}) - Another name for {{ill|Kamo Shrines|ja|賀茂神社}}, which collectively refers to {{ill|Kamigamo Shrine|ja|賀茂別雷神社}} and {{ill|Shimogamo Shrine|ja|賀茂御祖神社}}. * {{ill|Kamo Shrine (Tamaki)|ja|鴨神社 (玉城町)|lt=Kamo Shrine|lt_ja=鴨神社}} ({{ill|Mie Prefecture|ja|三重県}}, {{ill|Tamaki|ja|玉城町}}, Yamagami) * {{ill|Kamo Shrine (Takatuki)|ja|鴨神社 (高槻市)|lt=Kamo Shrine|lt_ja=鴨神社}} ({{ill|Osaka Prefecture|ja|大阪府}}, {{ill|Takatuki|ja|高槻市}}, Akaōji-chō) * {{ill|Kamo Shrine (Kawanishi)|ja|鴨神社 (川西市)|lt=Kamo Shrine|lt_ja=鴨神社}} ({{ill|Hyogo Prefecture|ja|兵庫県}}, {{ill|Kawanishi|ja|川西市}}, Kamo) * {{ill|Kamo Shrine (Tamano)|ja|鴨神社 (玉野市)|lt=Kamo Shrine|lt_ja=鴨神社}} ({{ill|Okayama Prefecture|ja|岡山県}}, {{ill|Tamano|ja|玉野市}}, Nagao) * {{ill|Kamo Shrine (Akaiwa)|ja|鴨神社 (赤磐市)|lt=Kamo Shrine|lt_ja=鴨神社}} (Okayama Prefecture, {{ill|Akaiwa|ja|赤磐市}}, Nibori-nishi) * {{ill|Kamo Shrine (Asakuchi)|ja|鴨神社 (浅口市)|lt=Kamo Shrine|lt_ja=鴨神社}} (Okayama Prefecture, {{ill|Asakuchi|ja|浅口市}}, Kamogata-chō Kamo) * {{ill|Kamo Shrine (Kibichuo)|ja|鴨神社 (吉備中央町)|lt=Kamo Shrine|lt_ja=鴨神社}} (Okayama Prefecture, {{ill|Kibichuo|ja|吉備中央町}}, Kamikamo) * {{ill|Kamo Shrine (Sanyo-Onoda)|ja|鴨神社 (山陽小野田市)|lt=Kamo Shrine|lt_ja=鴨神社}} ({{ill|Yamaguchi Prefecture|ja|山口県}}, {{ill|Sanyo-Onoda|ja|山陽小野田市}}, Asa) * {{ill|Kamo Shrine (Higashimiyoshi)|ja|鴨神社 (東みよし町)|lt=Kamo Shrine|lt_ja=鴨神社}} ({{ill|Tokushima Prefecture|ja|徳島県}}, {{ill|Higashimiyoshi|ja|東みよし町}}, Kamo) * {{ill|Kamo Shrine (Sakaide)|ja|鴨神社 (坂出市)|lt=Kamo Shrine|lt_ja=鴨神社}} - An {{ill|Ancient shrine of Sanuki Province|ja|讃岐国|阿野郡}}. Theories of its location include {{ill|Higashi Kamo Shrine|ja|鴨神社 (坂出市)#東鴨神社|lt=Higashi Kamo Shrine}} and {{ill|Nishi Kamo Shrine|ja|鴨神社 (坂出市)#西鴨神社|lt=Nishi Kamo Shrine}} (both in {{ill|Sakaide|ja|坂出市}}, Kamochō).

revision.text:
'''Kamo Shrine''' (加茂神社・賀茂神社) refers to Shinto shrines with the name "Kamo."
Most of these shrines were branches of the famous {{ill|Kamo Shrines|ja|賀茂神社}}, {{ill|Kamigamo Shrine|ja|賀茂別雷神社}} (Upper Kamo Shrine) and {{ill|Shimogamo Shrine|ja|賀茂御祖神社}} (Lower Kamo Shrine), located in Kyoto. Many shrines are also historically tied to the ancient {{ill|Kamo clan|ja|賀茂県主氏|Kamo clan=Kamo clan|lt=Kamo-no-agatanushi}} clan (賀茂県主氏).
There are multiple spellings so this article will cover all of them
== Kamo Shrine ([[:ja:加茂神社|加茂神社]][[Category:Shrine disambiguations]]) == === Tohoku === * {{ill|Kamo Shrine (Osaki)|ja|加茂神社 (大崎市)}} (Osaki City, Miyagi Prefecture) * {{ill|Kamo Shrine (Yamagata)|ja|加茂神社 (山形市)}} (Yamagata City, Yamagata Prefecture)

'''Kamo Shrine''' (加茂神社・賀茂神社) refers to Shinto shrines with the name "Kamo."
Most of these shrines were branches of the famous {{ill|Kamo Shrines|ja|賀茂神社}}, {{ill|Kamigamo Shrine|ja|賀茂別雷神社}} (Upper Kamo Shrine) and {{ill|Shimogamo Shrine|ja|賀茂御祖神社}} (Lower Kamo Shrine), located in Kyoto. Many shrines are also historically tied to the ancient {{ill|Kamo clan|ja|賀茂県主氏|Kamo clan=Kamo clan|lt=Kamo-no-agatanushi}} clan (賀茂県主氏).
There are multiple spellings so this article will cover all of them

== Kamo Shrine ([[:ja:鴨神社|鴨神社]][[Category:Shrine disambiguations]]) == <!--for some reason this has an independent Japanese article [[:ja:鴨神社]][[Category:Shrine disambiguations]]--> '''Kamo Shrine''' (鴨神社) is a Shinto shrine whose name includes the character "Kamo" (鴨).
* [[鴨神社_(玉城町)]] * {{ill|Kamo Shrine (Kamo-sha)|ja|賀茂神社|lt=Kamo Shrine (Kamo-sha)|lt_ja=鴨社}} ({{ill|Kyoto|ja|京都府}}, {{ill|Kyoto City|ja|京都市}}) - Another name for {{ill|Kamo Shrines|ja|賀茂神社}}, which collectively refers to {{ill|Kamigamo Shrine|ja|賀茂別雷神社}} and {{ill|Shimogamo Shrine|ja|賀茂御祖神社}}. * {{ill|Kamo Shrine (Tamaki)|ja|鴨神社 (玉城町)|lt=Kamo Shrine|lt_ja=鴨神社}} ({{ill|Mie Prefecture|ja|三重県}}, {{ill|Tamaki|ja|玉城町}}, Yamagami) * {{ill|Kamo Shrine (Takatuki)|ja|鴨神社 (高槻市)|lt=Kamo Shrine|lt_ja=鴨神社}} ({{ill|Osaka Prefecture|ja|大阪府}}, {{ill|Takatuki|ja|高槻市}}, Akaōji-chō) * {{ill|Kamo Shrine (Kawanishi)|ja|鴨神社 (川西市)|lt=Kamo Shrine|lt_ja=鴨神社}} ({{ill|Hyogo Prefecture|ja|兵庫県}}, {{ill|Kawanishi|ja|川西市}}, Kamo) * {{ill|Kamo Shrine (Tamano)|ja|鴨神社 (玉野市)|lt=Kamo Shrine|lt_ja=鴨神社}} ({{ill|Okayama Prefecture|ja|岡山県}}, {{ill|Tamano|ja|玉野市}}, Nagao) * {{ill|Kamo Shrine (Akaiwa)|ja|鴨神社 (赤磐市)|lt=Kamo Shrine|lt_ja=鴨神社}} (Okayama Prefecture, {{ill|Akaiwa|ja|赤磐市}}, Nibori-nishi) * {{ill|Kamo Shrine (Asakuchi)|ja|鴨神社 (浅口市)|lt=Kamo Shrine|lt_ja=鴨神社}} (Okayama Prefecture, {{ill|Asakuchi|ja|浅口市}}, Kamogata-chō Kamo) * {{ill|Kamo Shrine (Kibichuo)|ja|鴨神社 (吉備中央町)|lt=Kamo Shrine|lt_ja=鴨神社}} (Okayama Prefecture, {{ill|Kibichuo|ja|吉備中央町}}, Kamikamo) * {{ill|Kamo Shrine (Sanyo-Onoda)|ja|鴨神社 (山陽小野田市)|lt=Kamo Shrine|lt_ja=鴨神社}} ({{ill|Yamaguchi Prefecture|ja|山口県}}, {{ill|Sanyo-Onoda|ja|山陽小野田市}}, Asa) * {{ill|Kamo Shrine (Higashimiyoshi)|ja|鴨神社 (東みよし町)|lt=Kamo Shrine|lt_ja=鴨神社}} ({{ill|Tokushima Prefecture|ja|徳島県}}, {{ill|Higashimiyoshi|ja|東みよし町}}, Kamo) * {{ill|Kamo Shrine (Sakaide)|ja|鴨神社 (坂出市)|lt=Kamo Shrine|lt_ja=鴨神社}} - An {{ill|Ancient shrine of Sanuki Province|ja|讃岐国|阿野郡}}. Theories of its location include {{ill|Higashi Kamo Shrine|ja|鴨神社 (坂出市)#東鴨神社|lt=Higashi Kamo Shrine}} and {{ill|Nishi Kamo Shrine|ja|鴨神社 (坂出市)#西鴨神社|lt=Nishi Kamo Shrine}} (both in {{ill|Sakaide|ja|坂出市}}, Kamochō).

'''Kamo Shrine''' (加茂神社・賀茂神社) refers to Shinto shrines with the name "Kamo."
Most of these shrines were branches of the famous {{ill|Kamo Shrines|ja|賀茂神社}}, {{ill|Kamigamo Shrine|ja|賀茂別雷神社}} (Upper Kamo Shrine) and {{ill|Shimogamo Shrine|ja|賀茂御祖神社}} (Lower Kamo Shrine), located in Kyoto. Many shrines are also historically tied to the ancient {{ill|Kamo clan|ja|賀茂県主氏|Kamo clan=Kamo clan|lt=Kamo-no-agatanushi}} clan (賀茂県主氏).
There are multiple spellings so this article will cover all of them

'''Kamo Shrine''' (加茂神社・賀茂神社) refers to Shinto shrines with the name "Kamo."
Most of these shrines were branches of the famous {{ill|Kamo Shrines|ja|賀茂神社}}, {{ill|Kamigamo Shrine|ja|賀茂別雷神社}} (Upper Kamo Shrine) and {{ill|Shimogamo Shrine|ja|賀茂御祖神社}} (Lower Kamo Shrine), located in Kyoto. Many shrines are also historically tied to the ancient {{ill|Kamo clan|ja|賀茂県主氏|Kamo clan=Kamo clan|lt=Kamo-no-agatanushi}} clan (賀茂県主氏).
There are multiple spellings so this article will cover all of them

'''Kamo Shrine''' (加茂神社・賀茂神社) refers to Shinto shrines with the name "Kamo."
Most of these shrines were branches of the famous {{ill|Kamo Shrines|ja|賀茂神社}}, {{ill|Kamigamo Shrine|ja|賀茂別雷神社}} (Upper Kamo Shrine) and {{ill|Shimogamo Shrine|ja|賀茂御祖神社}} (Lower Kamo Shrine), located in Kyoto. Many shrines are also historically tied to the ancient {{ill|Kamo clan|ja|賀茂県主氏|Kamo clan=Kamo clan|lt=Kamo-no-agatanushi}} clan (賀茂県主氏).
There are multiple spellings so this article will cover all of them
== Kamo Shrine ([[:ja:加茂神社|加茂神社]][[Category:Shrine disambiguations]]) == === Tohoku === * {{ill|Kamo Shrine (Osaki)|ja|加茂神社 (大崎市)}} (Osaki City, Miyagi Prefecture) * {{ill|Kamo Shrine (Yamagata)|ja|加茂神社 (山形市)}} (Yamagata City, Yamagata Prefecture)

== Kamo Shrine ([[:ja:鴨神社|鴨神社]][[Category:Shrine disambiguations]]) == <!--for some reason this has an independent Japanese article [[:ja:鴨神社]][[Category:Shrine disambiguations]]--> '''Kamo Shrine''' (鴨神社) is a Shinto shrine whose name includes the character "Kamo" (鴨).
* [[鴨神社_(玉城町)]] * {{ill|Kamo Shrine (Kamo-sha)|ja|賀茂神社|lt=Kamo Shrine (Kamo-sha)|lt_ja=鴨社}} ({{ill|Kyoto|ja|京都府}}, {{ill|Kyoto City|ja|京都市}}) - Another name for {{ill|Kamo Shrines|ja|賀茂神社}}, which collectively refers to {{ill|Kamigamo Shrine|ja|賀茂別雷神社}} and {{ill|Shimogamo Shrine|ja|賀茂御祖神社}}. * {{ill|Kamo Shrine (Tamaki)|ja|鴨神社 (玉城町)|lt=Kamo Shrine|lt_ja=鴨神社}} ({{ill|Mie Prefecture|ja|三重県}}, {{ill|Tamaki|ja|玉城町}}, Yamagami) * {{ill|Kamo Shrine (Takatuki)|ja|鴨神社 (高槻市)|lt=Kamo Shrine|lt_ja=鴨神社}} ({{ill|Osaka Prefecture|ja|大阪府}}, {{ill|Takatuki|ja|高槻市}}, Akaōji-chō) * {{ill|Kamo Shrine (Kawanishi)|ja|鴨神社 (川西市)|lt=Kamo Shrine|lt_ja=鴨神社}} ({{ill|Hyogo Prefecture|ja|兵庫県}}, {{ill|Kawanishi|ja|川西市}}, Kamo) * {{ill|Kamo Shrine (Tamano)|ja|鴨神社 (玉野市)|lt=Kamo Shrine|lt_ja=鴨神社}} ({{ill|Okayama Prefecture|ja|岡山県}}, {{ill|Tamano|ja|玉野市}}, Nagao) * {{ill|Kamo Shrine (Akaiwa)|ja|鴨神社 (赤磐市)|lt=Kamo Shrine|lt_ja=鴨神社}} (Okayama Prefecture, {{ill|Akaiwa|ja|赤磐市}}, Nibori-nishi) * {{ill|Kamo Shrine (Asakuchi)|ja|鴨神社 (浅口市)|lt=Kamo Shrine|lt_ja=鴨神社}} (Okayama Prefecture, {{ill|Asakuchi|ja|浅口市}}, Kamogata-chō Kamo) * {{ill|Kamo Shrine (Kibichuo)|ja|鴨神社 (吉備中央町)|lt=Kamo Shrine|lt_ja=鴨神社}} (Okayama Prefecture, {{ill|Kibichuo|ja|吉備中央町}}, Kamikamo) * {{ill|Kamo Shrine (Sanyo-Onoda)|ja|鴨神社 (山陽小野田市)|lt=Kamo Shrine|lt_ja=鴨神社}} ({{ill|Yamaguchi Prefecture|ja|山口県}}, {{ill|Sanyo-Onoda|ja|山陽小野田市}}, Asa) * {{ill|Kamo Shrine (Higashimiyoshi)|ja|鴨神社 (東みよし町)|lt=Kamo Shrine|lt_ja=鴨神社}} ({{ill|Tokushima Prefecture|ja|徳島県}}, {{ill|Higashimiyoshi|ja|東みよし町}}, Kamo) * {{ill|Kamo Shrine (Sakaide)|ja|鴨神社 (坂出市)|lt=Kamo Shrine|lt_ja=鴨神社}} - An {{ill|Ancient shrine of Sanuki Province|ja|讃岐国|阿野郡}}. Theories of its location include {{ill|Higashi Kamo Shrine|ja|鴨神社 (坂出市)#東鴨神社|lt=Higashi Kamo Shrine}} and {{ill|Nishi Kamo Shrine|ja|鴨神社 (坂出市)#西鴨神社|lt=Nishi Kamo Shrine}} (both in {{ill|Sakaide|ja|坂出市}}, Kamochō).

== Kamo Shrine ([[:ja:鴨神社|鴨神社]][[Category:Shrine disambiguations]]) == <!--for some reason this has an independent Japanese article [[:ja:鴨神社]][[Category:Shrine disambiguations]]--> '''Kamo Shrine''' (鴨神社) is a Shinto shrine whose name includes the character "Kamo" (鴨).
* [[鴨神社_(玉城町)]] * {{ill|Kamo Shrine (Kamo-sha)|ja|賀茂神社|lt=Kamo Shrine (Kamo-sha)|lt_ja=鴨社}} ({{ill|Kyoto|ja|京都府}}, {{ill|Kyoto City|ja|京都市}}) - Another name for {{ill|Kamo Shrines|ja|賀茂神社}}, which collectively refers to {{ill|Kamigamo Shrine|ja|賀茂別雷神社}} and {{ill|Shimogamo Shrine|ja|賀茂御祖神社}}. * {{ill|Kamo Shrine (Tamaki)|ja|鴨神社 (玉城町)|lt=Kamo Shrine|lt_ja=鴨神社}} ({{ill|Mie Prefecture|ja|三重県}}, {{ill|Tamaki|ja|玉城町}}, Yamagami) * {{ill|Kamo Shrine (Takatuki)|ja|鴨神社 (高槻市)|lt=Kamo Shrine|lt_ja=鴨神社}} ({{ill|Osaka Prefecture|ja|大阪府}}, {{ill|Takatuki|ja|高槻市}}, Akaōji-chō) * {{ill|Kamo Shrine (Kawanishi)|ja|鴨神社 (川西市)|lt=Kamo Shrine|lt_ja=鴨神社}} ({{ill|Hyogo Prefecture|ja|兵庫県}}, {{ill|Kawanishi|ja|川西市}}, Kamo) * {{ill|Kamo Shrine (Tamano)|ja|鴨神社 (玉野市)|lt=Kamo Shrine|lt_ja=鴨神社}} ({{ill|Okayama Prefecture|ja|岡山県}}, {{ill|Tamano|ja|玉野市}}, Nagao) * {{ill|Kamo Shrine (Akaiwa)|ja|鴨神社 (赤磐市)|lt=Kamo Shrine|lt_ja=鴨神社}} (Okayama Prefecture, {{ill|Akaiwa|ja|赤磐市}}, Nibori-nishi) * {{ill|Kamo Shrine (Asakuchi)|ja|鴨神社 (浅口市)|lt=Kamo Shrine|lt_ja=鴨神社}} (Okayama Prefecture, {{ill|Asakuchi|ja|浅口市}}, Kamogata-chō Kamo) * {{ill|Kamo Shrine (Kibichuo)|ja|鴨神社 (吉備中央町)|lt=Kamo Shrine|lt_ja=鴨神社}} (Okayama Prefecture, {{ill|Kibichuo|ja|吉備中央町}}, Kamikamo) * {{ill|Kamo Shrine (Sanyo-Onoda)|ja|鴨神社 (山陽小野田市)|lt=Kamo Shrine|lt_ja=鴨神社}} ({{ill|Yamaguchi Prefecture|ja|山口県}}, {{ill|Sanyo-Onoda|ja|山陽小野田市}}, Asa) * {{ill|Kamo Shrine (Higashimiyoshi)|ja|鴨神社 (東みよし町)|lt=Kamo Shrine|lt_ja=鴨神社}} ({{ill|Tokushima Prefecture|ja|徳島県}}, {{ill|Higashimiyoshi|ja|東みよし町}}, Kamo) * {{ill|Kamo Shrine (Sakaide)|ja|鴨神社 (坂出市)|lt=Kamo Shrine|lt_ja=鴨神社}} - An {{ill|Ancient shrine of Sanuki Province|ja|讃岐国|阿野郡}}. Theories of its location include {{ill|Higashi Kamo Shrine|ja|鴨神社 (坂出市)#東鴨神社|lt=Higashi Kamo Shrine}} and {{ill|Nishi Kamo Shrine|ja|鴨神社 (坂出市)#西鴨神社|lt=Nishi Kamo Shrine}} (both in {{ill|Sakaide|ja|坂出市}}, Kamochō).

revision.text:
'''Kamo Shrine''' (加茂神社・賀茂神社) refers to Shinto shrines with the name "Kamo."
Most of these shrines were branches of the famous {{ill|Kamo Shrines|ja|賀茂神社}}, {{ill|Kamigamo Shrine|ja|賀茂別雷神社}} (Upper Kamo Shrine) and {{ill|Shimogamo Shrine|ja|賀茂御祖神社}} (Lower Kamo Shrine), located in Kyoto. Many shrines are also historically tied to the ancient {{ill|Kamo clan|ja|賀茂県主氏|Kamo clan=Kamo clan|lt=Kamo-no-agatanushi}} clan (賀茂県主氏).
There are multiple spellings so this article will cover all of them

== Kamo Shrine ([[:ja:鴨神社|鴨神社]][[Category:Shrine disambiguations]]) == <!--for some reason this has an independent Japanese article [[:ja:鴨神社]][[Category:Shrine disambiguations]]--> '''Kamo Shrine''' (鴨神社) is a Shinto shrine whose name includes the character "Kamo" (鴨).
* [[鴨神社_(玉城町)]] * {{ill|Kamo Shrine (Kamo-sha)|ja|賀茂神社|lt=Kamo Shrine (Kamo-sha)|lt_ja=鴨社}} ({{ill|Kyoto|ja|京都府}}, {{ill|Kyoto City|ja|京都市}}) - Another name for {{ill|Kamo Shrines|ja|賀茂神社}}, which collectively refers to {{ill|Kamigamo Shrine|ja|賀茂別雷神社}} and {{ill|Shimogamo Shrine|ja|賀茂御祖神社}}. * {{ill|Kamo Shrine (Tamaki)|ja|鴨神社 (玉城町)|lt=Kamo Shrine|lt_ja=鴨神社}} ({{ill|Mie Prefecture|ja|三重県}}, {{ill|Tamaki|ja|玉城町}}, Yamagami) * {{ill|Kamo Shrine (Takatuki)|ja|鴨神社 (高槻市)|lt=Kamo Shrine|lt_ja=鴨神社}} ({{ill|Osaka Prefecture|ja|大阪府}}, {{ill|Takatuki|ja|高槻市}}, Akaōji-chō) * {{ill|Kamo Shrine (Kawanishi)|ja|鴨神社 (川西市)|lt=Kamo Shrine|lt_ja=鴨神社}} ({{ill|Hyogo Prefecture|ja|兵庫県}}, {{ill|Kawanishi|ja|川西市}}, Kamo) * {{ill|Kamo Shrine (Tamano)|ja|鴨神社 (玉野市)|lt=Kamo Shrine|lt_ja=鴨神社}} ({{ill|Okayama Prefecture|ja|岡山県}}, {{ill|Tamano|ja|玉野市}}, Nagao) * {{ill|Kamo Shrine (Akaiwa)|ja|鴨神社 (赤磐市)|lt=Kamo Shrine|lt_ja=鴨神社}} (Okayama Prefecture, {{ill|Akaiwa|ja|赤磐市}}, Nibori-nishi) * {{ill|Kamo Shrine (Asakuchi)|ja|鴨神社 (浅口市)|lt=Kamo Shrine|lt_ja=鴨神社}} (Okayama Prefecture, {{ill|Asakuchi|ja|浅口市}}, Kamogata-chō Kamo) * {{ill|Kamo Shrine (Kibichuo)|ja|鴨神社 (吉備中央町)|lt=Kamo Shrine|lt_ja=鴨神社}} (Okayama Prefecture, {{ill|Kibichuo|ja|吉備中央町}}, Kamikamo) * {{ill|Kamo Shrine (Sanyo-Onoda)|ja|鴨神社 (山陽小野田市)|lt=Kamo Shrine|lt_ja=鴨神社}} ({{ill|Yamaguchi Prefecture|ja|山口県}}, {{ill|Sanyo-Onoda|ja|山陽小野田市}}, Asa) * {{ill|Kamo Shrine (Higashimiyoshi)|ja|鴨神社 (東みよし町)|lt=Kamo Shrine|lt_ja=鴨神社}} ({{ill|Tokushima Prefecture|ja|徳島県}}, {{ill|Higashimiyoshi|ja|東みよし町}}, Kamo) * {{ill|Kamo Shrine (Sakaide)|ja|鴨神社 (坂出市)|lt=Kamo Shrine|lt_ja=鴨神社}} - An {{ill|Ancient shrine of Sanuki Province|ja|讃岐国|阿野郡}}. Theories of its location include {{ill|Higashi Kamo Shrine|ja|鴨神社 (坂出市)#東鴨神社|lt=Higashi Kamo Shrine}} and {{ill|Nishi Kamo Shrine|ja|鴨神社 (坂出市)#西鴨神社|lt=Nishi Kamo Shrine}} (both in {{ill|Sakaide|ja|坂出市}}, Kamochō).

revision.comment: Bot: Reformatted page - moved categories, interwikis, DEFAULTSORT, and removed duplicates.
revision.text:
'''Kamo Shrine''' (加茂神社・賀茂神社) refers to Shinto shrines with the name "Kamo."
Most of these shrines were branches of the famous {{ill|Kamo Shrines|ja|賀茂神社}}, {{ill|Kamigamo Shrine|ja|賀茂別雷神社}} (Upper Kamo Shrine) and {{ill|Shimogamo Shrine|ja|賀茂御祖神社}} (Lower Kamo Shrine), located in Kyoto. Many shrines are also historically tied to the ancient {{ill|Kamo clan|ja|賀茂県主氏|Kamo clan=Kamo clan|lt=Kamo-no-agatanushi}} clan (賀茂県主氏).
There are multiple spellings so this article will cover all of them
== Kamo Shrine ([[:ja:加茂神社|加茂神社]]) == === Tohoku === * {{ill|Kamo Shrine (Osaki)|ja|加茂神社 (大崎市)}} (Osaki City, Miyagi Prefecture) * {{ill|Kamo Shrine (Yamagata)|ja|加茂神社 (山形市)}} (Yamagata City, Yamagata Prefecture)

revision.text:
'''Kamo Shrine''' (加茂神社・賀茂神社) refers to Shinto shrines with the name "Kamo."
Most of these shrines were branches of the famous {{ill|Kamo Shrines|ja|賀茂神社|12=draft}}, {{ill|Kamigamo Shrine|ja|賀茂別雷神社|12=draft}} (Upper Kamo Shrine) and {{ill|Shimogamo Shrine|ja|賀茂御祖神社|12=draft}} (Lower Kamo Shrine), located in Kyoto. Many shrines are also historically tied to the ancient {{ill|Kamo clan|ja|賀茂県主氏|Kamo clan=Kamo clan|lt=Kamo-no-agatanushi|12=draft}} clan (賀茂県主氏).
There are multiple spellings so this article will cover all of them

revision.text:
'''Kamo Shrine''' (加茂神社・賀茂神社) refers to Shinto shrines with the name "Kamo."
Most of these shrines were branches of the famous {{ill|Kamo Shrines|ja|賀茂神社|12=draft|12=draft}}, {{ill|Kamigamo Shrine|ja|賀茂別雷神社|12=draft|12=draft}} (Upper Kamo Shrine) and {{ill|Shimogamo Shrine|ja|賀茂御祖神社|12=draft|12=draft}} (Lower Kamo Shrine), located in Kyoto. Many shrines are also historically tied to the ancient {{ill|Kamo clan|ja|賀茂県主氏|Kamo clan=Kamo clan|lt=Kamo-no-agatanushi|12=draft|12=draft}} clan (賀茂県主氏).
There are multiple spellings so this article will cover all of them

'''Kamo Shrine''' (加茂神社・賀茂神社) refers to Shinto shrines with the name "Kamo."
Most of these shrines were branches of the famous {{ill|Kamo Shrines|ja|賀茂神社|12=draft|12=draft}}, {{ill|Kamigamo Shrine|ja|賀茂別雷神社|12=draft|12=draft}} (Upper Kamo Shrine) and {{ill|Shimogamo Shrine|ja|賀茂御祖神社|12=draft|12=draft}} (Lower Kamo Shrine), located in Kyoto. Many shrines are also historically tied to the ancient {{ill|Kamo clan|ja|賀茂県主氏|Kamo clan=Kamo clan|lt=Kamo-no-agatanushi|12=draft|12=draft}} clan (賀茂県主氏).
There are multiple spellings so this article will cover all of them

'''Kamo Shrine''' (加茂神社・賀茂神社) refers to Shinto shrines with the name "Kamo."
Most of these shrines were branches of the famous {{ill|Kamo Shrines|ja|賀茂神社|12=draft|12=draft}}, {{ill|Kamigamo Shrine|ja|賀茂別雷神社|12=draft|12=draft}} (Upper Kamo Shrine) and {{ill|Shimogamo Shrine|ja|賀茂御祖神社|12=draft|12=draft}} (Lower Kamo Shrine), located in Kyoto. Many shrines are also historically tied to the ancient {{ill|Kamo clan|ja|賀茂県主氏|Kamo clan=Kamo clan|lt=Kamo-no-agatanushi|12=draft|12=draft}} clan (賀茂県主氏).
There are multiple spellings so this article will cover all of them

'''Kamo Shrine''' (加茂神社・賀茂神社) refers to Shinto shrines with the name "Kamo."
Most of these shrines were branches of the famous {{ill|Kamo Shrines|ja|賀茂神社|12=draft|12=draft}}, {{ill|Kamigamo Shrine|ja|賀茂別雷神社|12=draft|12=draft}} (Upper Kamo Shrine) and {{ill|Shimogamo Shrine|ja|賀茂御祖神社|12=draft|12=draft}} (Lower Kamo Shrine), located in Kyoto. Many shrines are also historically tied to the ancient {{ill|Kamo clan|ja|賀茂県主氏|Kamo clan=Kamo clan|lt=Kamo-no-agatanushi|12=draft|12=draft}} clan (賀茂県主氏).
There are multiple spellings so this article will cover all of them

'''Kamo Shrine''' (加茂神社・賀茂神社) refers to Shinto shrines with the name "Kamo."
Most of these shrines were branches of the famous {{ill|Kamo Shrines|ja|賀茂神社|12=draft|12=draft}}, {{ill|Kamigamo Shrine|ja|賀茂別雷神社|12=draft|12=draft}} (Upper Kamo Shrine) and {{ill|Shimogamo Shrine|ja|賀茂御祖神社|12=draft|12=draft}} (Lower Kamo Shrine), located in Kyoto. Many shrines are also historically tied to the ancient {{ill|Kamo clan|ja|賀茂県主氏|Kamo clan=Kamo clan|lt=Kamo-no-agatanushi|12=draft|12=draft}} clan (賀茂県主氏).
There are multiple spellings so this article will cover all of them

'''Kamo Shrine''' (加茂神社・賀茂神社) refers to Shinto shrines with the name "Kamo."
Most of these shrines were branches of the famous {{ill|Kamo Shrines|ja|賀茂神社|12=draft|12=draft}}, {{ill|Kamigamo Shrine|ja|賀茂別雷神社|12=draft|12=draft}} (Upper Kamo Shrine) and {{ill|Shimogamo Shrine|ja|賀茂御祖神社|12=draft|12=draft}} (Lower Kamo Shrine), located in Kyoto. Many shrines are also historically tied to the ancient {{ill|Kamo clan|ja|賀茂県主氏|Kamo clan=Kamo clan|lt=Kamo-no-agatanushi|12=draft|12=draft}} clan (賀茂県主氏).
There are multiple spellings so this article will cover all of them

'''Kamo Shrine''' (加茂神社・賀茂神社) refers to Shinto shrines with the name "Kamo."
Most of these shrines were branches of the famous {{ill|Kamo Shrines|ja|賀茂神社|12=draft|12=draft}}, {{ill|Kamigamo Shrine|ja|賀茂別雷神社|12=draft|12=draft}} (Upper Kamo Shrine) and {{ill|Shimogamo Shrine|ja|賀茂御祖神社|12=draft|12=draft}} (Lower Kamo Shrine), located in Kyoto. Many shrines are also historically tied to the ancient {{ill|Kamo clan|ja|賀茂県主氏|Kamo clan=Kamo clan|lt=Kamo-no-agatanushi|12=draft|12=draft}} clan (賀茂県主氏).
There are multiple spellings so this article will cover all of them

'''Kamo Shrine''' (加茂神社・賀茂神社) refers to Shinto shrines with the name "Kamo."
Most of these shrines were branches of the famous {{ill|Kamo Shrines|ja|賀茂神社|12=draft|12=draft}}, {{ill|Kamigamo Shrine|ja|賀茂別雷神社|12=draft|12=draft}} (Upper Kamo Shrine) and {{ill|Shimogamo Shrine|ja|賀茂御祖神社|12=draft|12=draft}} (Lower Kamo Shrine), located in Kyoto. Many shrines are also historically tied to the ancient {{ill|Kamo clan|ja|賀茂県主氏|Kamo clan=Kamo clan|lt=Kamo-no-agatanushi|12=draft|12=draft}} clan (賀茂県主氏).
There are multiple spellings so this article will cover all of them

'''Kamo Shrine''' (加茂神社・賀茂神社) refers to Shinto shrines with the name "Kamo."
Most of these shrines were branches of the famous {{ill|Kamo Shrines|ja|賀茂神社|12=draft|12=draft}}, {{ill|Kamigamo Shrine|ja|賀茂別雷神社|12=draft|12=draft}} (Upper Kamo Shrine) and {{ill|Shimogamo Shrine|ja|賀茂御祖神社|12=draft|12=draft}} (Lower Kamo Shrine), located in Kyoto. Many shrines are also historically tied to the ancient {{ill|Kamo clan|ja|賀茂県主氏|Kamo clan=Kamo clan|lt=Kamo-no-agatanushi|12=draft|12=draft}} clan (賀茂県主氏).
There are multiple spellings so this article will cover all of them

'''Kamo Shrine''' (加茂神社・賀茂神社) refers to Shinto shrines with the name "Kamo."
Most of these shrines were branches of the famous {{ill|Kamo Shrines|ja|賀茂神社|12=draft|12=draft}}, {{ill|Kamigamo Shrine|ja|賀茂別雷神社|12=draft|12=draft}} (Upper Kamo Shrine) and {{ill|Shimogamo Shrine|ja|賀茂御祖神社|12=draft|12=draft}} (Lower Kamo Shrine), located in Kyoto. Many shrines are also historically tied to the ancient {{ill|Kamo clan|ja|賀茂県主氏|Kamo clan=Kamo clan|lt=Kamo-no-agatanushi|12=draft|12=draft}} clan (賀茂県主氏).
There are multiple spellings so this article will cover all of them

'''Kamo Shrine''' (加茂神社・賀茂神社) refers to Shinto shrines with the name "Kamo."
Most of these shrines were branches of the famous {{ill|Kamo Shrines|ja|賀茂神社|12=draft|12=draft}}, {{ill|Kamigamo Shrine|ja|賀茂別雷神社|12=draft|12=draft}} (Upper Kamo Shrine) and {{ill|Shimogamo Shrine|ja|賀茂御祖神社|12=draft|12=draft}} (Lower Kamo Shrine), located in Kyoto. Many shrines are also historically tied to the ancient {{ill|Kamo clan|ja|賀茂県主氏|Kamo clan=Kamo clan|lt=Kamo-no-agatanushi|12=draft|12=draft}} clan (賀茂県主氏).
There are multiple spellings so this article will cover all of them

'''Kamo Shrine''' (加茂神社・賀茂神社) refers to Shinto shrines with the name "Kamo."
Most of these shrines were branches of the famous {{ill|Kamo Shrines|ja|賀茂神社|12=draft|12=draft}}, {{ill|Kamigamo Shrine|ja|賀茂別雷神社|12=draft|12=draft}} (Upper Kamo Shrine) and {{ill|Shimogamo Shrine|ja|賀茂御祖神社|12=draft|12=draft}} (Lower Kamo Shrine), located in Kyoto. Many shrines are also historically tied to the ancient {{ill|Kamo clan|ja|賀茂県主氏|Kamo clan=Kamo clan|lt=Kamo-no-agatanushi|12=draft|12=draft}} clan (賀茂県主氏).
There are multiple spellings so this article will cover all of them

'''Kamo Shrine''' (加茂神社・賀茂神社) refers to Shinto shrines with the name "Kamo."
Most of these shrines were branches of the famous {{ill|Kamo Shrines|ja|賀茂神社|12=draft|12=draft}}, {{ill|Kamigamo Shrine|ja|賀茂別雷神社|12=draft|12=draft}} (Upper Kamo Shrine) and {{ill|Shimogamo Shrine|ja|賀茂御祖神社|12=draft|12=draft}} (Lower Kamo Shrine), located in Kyoto. Many shrines are also historically tied to the ancient {{ill|Kamo clan|ja|賀茂県主氏|Kamo clan=Kamo clan|lt=Kamo-no-agatanushi|12=draft|12=draft}} clan (賀茂県主氏).
There are multiple spellings so this article will cover all of them

'''Kamo Shrine''' (加茂神社・賀茂神社) refers to Shinto shrines with the name "Kamo."
Most of these shrines were branches of the famous {{ill|Kamo Shrines|ja|賀茂神社|12=draft|12=draft}}, {{ill|Kamigamo Shrine|ja|賀茂別雷神社|12=draft|12=draft}} (Upper Kamo Shrine) and {{ill|Shimogamo Shrine|ja|賀茂御祖神社|12=draft|12=draft}} (Lower Kamo Shrine), located in Kyoto. Many shrines are also historically tied to the ancient {{ill|Kamo clan|ja|賀茂県主氏|Kamo clan=Kamo clan|lt=Kamo-no-agatanushi|12=draft|12=draft}} clan (賀茂県主氏).
There are multiple spellings so this article will cover all of them

revision.text:
'''Kamo Shrine''' (加茂神社・賀茂神社) refers to Shinto shrines with the name "Kamo."
Most of these shrines were branches of the famous {{ill|Kamo Shrines|ja|賀茂神社|12=draft|12=draft}}, {{ill|Kamigamo Shrine|ja|賀茂別雷神社|12=draft|12=draft}} (Upper Kamo Shrine) and {{ill|Shimogamo Shrine|ja|賀茂御祖神社|12=draft|12=draft}} (Lower Kamo Shrine), located in Kyoto. Many shrines are also historically tied to the ancient {{ill|Kamo clan|ja|賀茂県主氏|Kamo clan=Kamo clan|lt=Kamo-no-agatanushi|12=draft|12=draft}} clan (賀茂県主氏).
There are multiple spellings so this article will cover all of them

'''Kamo Shrine''' (加茂神社・賀茂神社) refers to Shinto shrines with the name "Kamo."
Most of these shrines were branches of the famous {{ill|Kamo Shrines|ja|賀茂神社|12=draft|12=draft}}, {{ill|Kamigamo Shrine|ja|賀茂別雷神社|12=draft|12=draft}} (Upper Kamo Shrine) and {{ill|Shimogamo Shrine|ja|賀茂御祖神社|12=draft|12=draft}} (Lower Kamo Shrine), located in Kyoto. Many shrines are also historically tied to the ancient {{ill|Kamo clan|ja|賀茂県主氏|Kamo clan=Kamo clan|lt=Kamo-no-agatanushi|12=draft|12=draft}} clan (賀茂県主氏).
There are multiple spellings so this article will cover all of them

revision.text:
'''Kamo Shrine''' (加茂神社・賀茂神社) refers to Shinto shrines with the name "Kamo."
Most of these shrines were branches of the famous {{ill|Kamo Shrines|ja|賀茂神社|12=draft|12=draft}}, {{ill|Kamigamo Shrine|ja|賀茂別雷神社|12=draft|12=draft}} (Upper Kamo Shrine) and {{ill|Shimogamo Shrine|ja|賀茂御祖神社|12=draft|12=draft}} (Lower Kamo Shrine), located in Kyoto. Many shrines are also historically tied to the ancient {{ill|Kamo clan|ja|賀茂県主氏|Kamo clan=Kamo clan|lt=Kamo-no-agatanushi|12=draft|12=draft}} clan (賀茂県主氏).
There are multiple spellings so this article will cover all of them

revision.text:
'''Kamo Shrine''' (加茂神社・賀茂神社) refers to Shinto shrines with the name "Kamo."
Most of these shrines were branches of the famous {{ill|Kamo Shrines|ja|賀茂神社|12=draft|12=draft}}, {{ill|Kamigamo Shrine|ja|賀茂別雷神社|12=draft|12=draft}} (Upper Kamo Shrine) and {{ill|Shimogamo Shrine|ja|賀茂御祖神社|12=draft|12=draft}} (Lower Kamo Shrine), located in Kyoto. Many shrines are also historically tied to the ancient {{ill|Kamo clan|ja|賀茂県主氏|Kamo clan=Kamo clan|lt=Kamo-no-agatanushi|12=draft|12=draft}} clan (賀茂県主氏).
There are multiple spellings so this article will cover all of them

'''Kamo Shrine''' (加茂神社・賀茂神社) refers to Shinto shrines with the name "Kamo."
Most of these shrines were branches of the famous {{ill|Kamo Shrines|ja|賀茂神社|12=draft|12=draft}}, {{ill|Kamigamo Shrine|ja|賀茂別雷神社|12=draft|12=draft}} (Upper Kamo Shrine) and {{ill|Shimogamo Shrine|ja|賀茂御祖神社|12=draft|12=draft}} (Lower Kamo Shrine), located in Kyoto. Many shrines are also historically tied to the ancient {{ill|Kamo clan|ja|賀茂県主氏|Kamo clan=Kamo clan|lt=Kamo-no-agatanushi|12=draft|12=draft}} clan (賀茂県主氏).
There are multiple spellings so this article will cover all of them

'''Kamo Shrine''' (加茂神社・賀茂神社) refers to Shinto shrines with the name "Kamo."
Most of these shrines were branches of the famous {{ill|Kamo Shrines|ja|賀茂神社|12=draft|12=draft}}, {{ill|Kamigamo Shrine|ja|賀茂別雷神社|12=draft|12=draft}} (Upper Kamo Shrine) and {{ill|Shimogamo Shrine|ja|賀茂御祖神社|12=draft|12=draft}} (Lower Kamo Shrine), located in Kyoto. Many shrines are also historically tied to the ancient {{ill|Kamo clan|ja|賀茂県主氏|Kamo clan=Kamo clan|lt=Kamo-no-agatanushi|12=draft|12=draft}} clan (賀茂県主氏).
There are multiple spellings so this article will cover all of them
