revision.text:
'''Kamo Shrine''' (加茂神社・賀茂神社) refers to Shinto shrines with the name "Kamo."
Most of these shrines were branches of the famous {{ill|Kamo Shrines|ja|賀茂神社|12=draft|12=draft}}, {{ill|Kamigamo Shrine|ja|賀茂別雷神社|12=draft|12=draft}} (Upper Kamo Shrine) and {{ill|Shimogamo Shrine|ja|賀茂御祖神社|12=draft|12=draft}} (Lower Kamo Shrine), located in Kyoto. Many shrines are also historically tied to the ancient {{ill|Kamo clan|ja|賀茂県主氏|Kamo clan=Kamo clan|lt=Kamo-no-agatanushi|12=draft|12=draft}} clan (賀茂県主氏).
There are multiple spellings so this article will cover all of them

'''Kamo Shrine''' (加茂神社・賀茂神社) refers to Shinto shrines with the name "Kamo."
Most of these shrines were branches of the famous {{ill|Kamo Shrines|ja|賀茂神社|12=draft|12=draft}}, {{ill|Kamigamo Shrine|ja|賀茂別雷神社|12=draft|12=draft}} (Upper Kamo Shrine) and {{ill|Shimogamo Shrine|ja|賀茂御祖神社|12=draft|12=draft}} (Lower Kamo Shrine), located in Kyoto. Many shrines are also historically tied to the ancient {{ill|Kamo clan|ja|賀茂県主氏|Kamo clan=Kamo clan|lt=Kamo-no-agatanushi|12=draft|12=draft}} clan (賀茂県主氏).
There are multiple spellings so this article will cover all of them

== Kamo Shrine ([[:ja:鴨神社|鴨神社]]) ==
'''Kamo Shrine''' (鴨神社) is a Shinto shrine whose name includes the character "Kamo" (鴨).

'''Kamo Shrine''' (加茂神社・賀茂神社) refers to Shinto shrines with the name "Kamo."
Most of these shrines were branches of the famous {{ill|Kamo Shrines|ja|賀茂神社|12=draft}}, {{ill|Kamigamo Shrine|ja|賀茂別雷神社|12=draft}} (Upper Kamo Shrine) and {{ill|Shimogamo Shrine|ja|賀茂御祖神社|12=draft}} (Lower Kamo Shrine), located in Kyoto. Many shrines are also historically tied to the ancient {{ill|Kamo clan|ja|賀茂県主氏|Kamo clan=Kamo clan|lt=Kamo-no-agatanushi|12=draft}} clan (賀茂県主氏).
There are multiple spellings so this article will cover all of them
== Kamo Shrine ([[:ja:加茂神社|加茂神社]]) == === Tohoku === * {{ill|Kamo Shrine (Osaki)|ja|加茂神社 (大崎市)|12=draft}} (Osaki City, Miyagi Prefecture) * {{ill|Kamo Shrine (Yamagata)|ja|加茂神社 (山形市)|12=draft}} (Yamagata City, Yamagata Prefecture)

== Kamo Shrine ([[:ja:鴨神社|鴨神社]]) ==
'''Kamo Shrine''' (鴨神社) is a Shinto shrine whose name includes the character "Kamo" (鴨).

Theories of its location include {{ill|Higashi Kamo Shrine|ja|鴨神社 (坂出市)#東鴨神社|lt=Higashi Kamo Shrine|12=draft}} and {{ill|Nishi Kamo Shrine|ja|鴨神社 (坂出市)#西鴨神社|lt=Nishi Kamo Shrine|12=draft}} (both in {{ill|Sakaide|ja|坂出市|12=draft}}, Kamochō).

<!--for some reason this has an independent Japanese article [[:ja:鴨神社]]--> <!--note do not remove this category during translation--> <!--Kamo Shrine (disambiguation)--> <!--Kamo Shrine (disambiguation)-->

'''Kamo Shrine''' (加茂神社・賀茂神社) refers to Shinto shrines with the name "Kamo."
Most of these shrines were branches of the famous {{ill|Kamo Shrines|ja|賀茂神社}}, {{ill|Kamigamo Shrine|ja|賀茂別雷神社}} (Upper Kamo Shrine) and {{ill|Shimogamo Shrine|ja|賀茂御祖神社}} (Lower Kamo Shrine), located in Kyoto. Many shrines are also historically tied to the ancient {{ill|Kamo clan|ja|賀茂県主氏|Kamo clan=Kamo clan|lt=Kamo-no-agatanushi}} clan (賀茂県主氏).
There are multiple spellings so this article will cover all of them

== Kamo Shrine ([[:ja:鴨神社|鴨神社]]) ==
'''Kamo Shrine''' (鴨神社) is a Shinto shrine whose name includes the character "Kamo" (鴨).
* [[鴨神社_(玉城町)]] * {{ill|Kamo Shrine (Kamo-sha)|ja|賀茂神社|lt=Kamo Shrine (Kamo-sha)|lt_ja=鴨社}} ({{ill|Kyoto|ja|京都府}}, {{ill|Kyoto City|ja|京都市}}) - Another name for {{ill|Kamo Shrines|ja|賀茂神社}}, which collectively refers to {{ill|Kamigamo Shrine|ja|賀茂別雷神社}} and {{ill|Shimogamo Shrine|ja|賀茂御祖神社}}. * {{ill|Kamo Shrine (Tamaki)|ja|鴨神社 (玉城町)|lt=Kamo Shrine|lt_ja=鴨神社}} ({{ill|Mie Prefecture|ja|三重県}}, {{ill|Tamaki|ja|玉城町}}, Yamagami) * {{ill|Kamo Shrine (Takatuki)|ja|鴨神社 (高槻市)|lt=Kamo Shrine|lt_ja=鴨神社}} ({{ill|Osaka Prefecture|ja|大阪府}}, {{ill|Takatuki|ja|高槻市}}, Akaōji-chō) * {{ill|Kamo Shrine (Kawanishi)|ja|鴨神社 (川西市)|lt=Kamo Shrine|lt_ja=鴨神社}} ({{ill|Hyogo Prefecture|ja|兵庫県}}, {{ill|Kawanishi|ja|川西市}}, Kamo) * {{ill|Kamo Shrine (Tamano)|ja|鴨神社 (玉野市)|lt=Kamo Shrine|lt_ja=鴨神社}} ({{ill|Okayama Prefecture|ja|岡山県}}, {{ill|Tamano|ja|玉野市}}, Nagao) * {{ill|Kamo Shrine (Akaiwa)|ja|鴨神社 (赤磐市)|lt=Kamo Shrine|lt_ja=鴨神社}} (Okayama Prefecture, {{ill|Akaiwa|ja|赤磐市}}, Nibori-nishi) * {{ill|Kamo Shrine (Asakuchi)|ja|鴨神社 (浅口市)|lt=Kamo Shrine|lt_ja=鴨神社}} (Okayama Prefecture, {{ill|Asakuchi|ja|浅口市}}, Kamogata-chō Kamo) * {{ill|Kamo Shrine (Kibichuo)|ja|鴨神社 (吉備中央町)|lt=Kamo Shrine|lt_ja=鴨神社}} (Okayama Prefecture, {{ill|Kibichuo|ja|吉備中央町}}, Kamikamo) * {{ill|Kamo Shrine (Sanyo-Onoda)|ja|鴨神社 (山陽小野田市)|lt=Kamo Shrine|lt_ja=鴨神社}} ({{ill|Yamaguchi Prefecture|ja|山口県}}, {{ill|Sanyo-Onoda|ja|山陽小野田市}}, Asa) * {{ill|Kamo Shrine (Higashimiyoshi)|ja|鴨神社 (東みよし町)|lt=Kamo Shrine|lt_ja=鴨神社}} ({{ill|Tokushima Prefecture|ja|徳島県}}, {{ill|Higashimiyoshi|ja|東みよし町}}, Kamo) * {{ill|Kamo Shrine (Sakaide)|ja|鴨神社 (坂出市)|lt=Kamo Shrine|lt_ja=鴨神社}} - An {{ill|Ancient shrine of Sanuki Province|ja|讃岐国|阿野郡}}. Theories of its location include {{ill|Higashi Kamo Shrine|ja|鴨神社 (坂出市)#東鴨神社|lt=Higashi Kamo Shrine}} and {{ill|Nishi Kamo Shrine|ja|鴨神社 (坂出市)#西鴨神社|lt=Nishi Kamo Shrine}} (both in {{ill|Sakaide|ja|坂出市}}, Kamochō).

'''Kamo Shrine''' (加茂神社・賀茂神社) refers to Shinto shrines with the name "Kamo."
Most of these shrines were branches of the famous {{ill|Kamo Shrines|ja|賀茂神社}}, {{ill|Kamigamo Shrine|ja|賀茂別雷神社}} (Upper Kamo Shrine) and {{ill|Shimogamo Shrine|ja|賀茂御祖神社}} (Lower Kamo Shrine), located in Kyoto. Many shrines are also historically tied to the ancient {{ill|Kamo clan|ja|賀茂県主氏|Kamo clan=Kamo clan|lt=Kamo-no-agatanushi}} clan (賀茂県主氏).
There are multiple spellings so this article will cover all of them

revision.text:
{{otheruses|日本各地の「加茂神社」|京都にある神社|賀茂神社|同音異字の賀茂神社|賀茂神社 (曖昧さ回避)|鴨神社|鴨神社}} '''加茂神社'''（かもじんじゃ）は、｢加茂」を社名に持つ[[神社]]。
その大半は、京都の[[賀茂神社]]と呼ばれる[[賀茂別雷神社]]（上賀茂神社）と[[賀茂御祖神社]]（下鴨神社）から勧請されたもので、古代氏族・[[賀茂県主氏]]に関係するものもある。
== 主な加茂神社 ==
=== 東北・関東地方 === * [[加茂神社 (大船渡市)|加茂神社]] （[[岩手県]][[大船渡市]]大船渡町） * [[加茂神社 (大崎市)|加茂神社]] （[[宮城県]][[大崎市]]田尻） * [[加茂神社 (大仙市)|加茂神社]] （[[秋田県]][[大仙市]]下深井） * [[加茂神社 (天童市)|加茂神社]] （[[山形県]][[天童市]]老野森） * [[加茂神社 (かすみがうら市)|加茂神社]] （[[茨城県]][[かすみがうら市]]加茂） * [[加茂神社 (那須烏山市)|加茂神社]] （[[栃木県]][[那須烏山市]]月次） * [[加茂神社 (さいたま市)|加茂神社]] （[[埼玉県]][[さいたま市]][[北区 (さいたま市)|北区]][[宮原町 (さいたま市)|宮原町]]） * [[加茂神社 (市原市)|加茂神社]] ([[千葉県]][[市原市]]加茂） * [[加茂神社 (鴨川市)|加茂神社]] （千葉県[[鴨川市]][[成川 (鴨川市)|成川]]） * [[加茂神社 (小田原市)|加茂神社]] （[[神奈川県]][[小田原市]]鴨宮）
=== 中部地方 === * [[加茂神社 (妙高市)|加茂神社]] （[[新潟県]][[妙高市]]小出雲） * [[加茂神社 (佐渡市)|加茂神社]] （新潟県[[佐渡市]]栗野江） * [[加茂神社 (富山市)|加茂神社]] （[[富山県]][[富山市]]任海） * [[加茂神社 (射水市)|加茂神社]] （富山県[[射水市]]加茂中部） – 下村加茂神社 * [[加茂神社 (射水市海老江)|加茂神社]] （富山県射水市海老江） * [[加茂神社 (福井市)|加茂神社]] （[[福井県]][[福井市]]加茂町） * [[加茂神社 (小浜市)|加茂神社]] （福井県[[小浜市]]矢代） * [[加茂神社 (丹波山村)|加茂神社]] （[[山梨県]][[北都留郡]][[丹波山村]]鴨沢） * [[加茂神社 (長野市西長野)|加茂神社]] （[[長野県]][[長野市]][[西長野]]） * [[加茂神社 (長野市鬼無里)|加茂神社]] （長野県長野市鬼無里） * [[加茂神社 (高山市)|加茂神社]] （[[岐阜県]][[高山市]]国府町） * [[加茂神社 (美濃加茂市蜂屋町)|加茂神社]] （岐阜県[[美濃加茂市]]蜂屋町） * [[加茂神社 (美濃加茂市加茂野町)|加茂神社]] （岐阜県美濃加茂市加茂野町）

revision.comment: Immanuelle moved page [[加茂神社]] to [[Kamo Shrine (disambiguation)]] without leaving a redirect: Bot: merge import
revision.text:
{{otheruses|日本各地の「加茂神社」|京都にある神社|賀茂神社|同音異字の賀茂神社|賀茂神社 (曖昧さ回避)|鴨神社|鴨神社}} '''加茂神社'''（かもじんじゃ）は、｢加茂」を社名に持つ[[神社]]。
その大半は、京都の[[賀茂神社]]と呼ばれる[[賀茂別雷神社]]（上賀茂神社）と[[賀茂御祖神社]]（下鴨神社）から勧請されたもので、古代氏族・[[賀茂県主氏]]に関係するものもある。

'''Kamo Shrine''' (加茂神社・賀茂神社) refers to Shinto shrines with the name "Kamo."
Most of these shrines were branches of the famous {{ill|Kamo Shrines|ja|賀茂神社}}, {{ill|Kamigamo Shrine|ja|賀茂別雷神社}} (Upper Kamo Shrine) and {{ill|Shimogamo Shrine|ja|賀茂御祖神社}} (Lower Kamo Shrine), located in Kyoto. Many shrines are also historically tied to the ancient {{ill|Kamo clan|ja|賀茂県主氏|Kamo clan=Kamo clan|lt=Kamo-no-agatanushi}} clan (賀茂県主氏).
There are multiple spellings so this article will cover all of them
== Kamo Shrine ([[:ja:加茂神社|加茂神社]]) == === Tohoku === * {{ill|Kamo Shrine (Osaki)|ja|加茂神社 (大崎市)}} (Osaki City, Miyagi Prefecture) * {{ill|Kamo Shrine (Yamagata)|ja|加茂神社 (山形市)}} (Yamagata City, Yamagata Prefecture)

== Kamo Shrine ([[:ja:鴨神社|鴨神社]]) ==
'''Kamo Shrine''' (鴨神社) is a Shinto shrine whose name includes the character "Kamo" (鴨).

Theories of its location include {{ill|Higashi Kamo Shrine|ja|鴨神社 (坂出市)#東鴨神社|lt=Higashi Kamo Shrine}} and {{ill|Nishi Kamo Shrine|ja|鴨神社 (坂出市)#西鴨神社|lt=Nishi Kamo Shrine}} (both in {{ill|Sakaide|ja|坂出市}}, Kamochō).

<!--for some reason this has an independent Japanese article [[:ja:鴨神社]]--> <!--note do not remove this category during translation--> <!--Kamo Shrine (disambiguation)--> <!--Kamo Shrine (disambiguation)--> {{translated page|ja|加茂神社|version=103715123|comment=Imported full ja history}}

'''Kamo Shrine''' (加茂神社・賀茂神社) refers to Shinto shrines with the name "Kamo."
Most of these shrines were branches of the famous {{ill|Kamo Shrines|ja|賀茂神社|12=draft}}, {{ill|Kamigamo Shrine|ja|賀茂別雷神社|12=draft}} (Upper Kamo Shrine) and {{ill|Shimogamo Shrine|ja|賀茂御祖神社|12=draft}} (Lower Kamo Shrine), located in Kyoto. Many shrines are also historically tied to the ancient {{ill|Kamo clan|ja|賀茂県主氏|Kamo clan=Kamo clan|lt=Kamo-no-agatanushi|12=draft}} clan (賀茂県主氏).
There are multiple spellings so this article will cover all of them

== Kamo Shrine ([[:ja:鴨神社|鴨神社]]) ==
'''Kamo Shrine''' (鴨神社) is a Shinto shrine whose name includes the character "Kamo" (鴨).

[[ja:加茂神社]] [[ja:賀茂神社]] [[ja:鴨神社]] [[en:Kamo Shrines]] [[sn:Kamo Shrine (disambiguation)]]

'''Kamo Shrine''' (加茂神社・賀茂神社) refers to Shinto shrines with the name "Kamo."
Most of these shrines were branches of the famous {{ill|Kamo Shrines|ja|賀茂神社|12=draft|12=draft}}, {{ill|Kamigamo Shrine|ja|賀茂別雷神社|12=draft|12=draft}} (Upper Kamo Shrine) and {{ill|Shimogamo Shrine|ja|賀茂御祖神社|12=draft|12=draft}} (Lower Kamo Shrine), located in Kyoto. Many shrines are also historically tied to the ancient {{ill|Kamo clan|ja|賀茂県主氏|Kamo clan=Kamo clan|lt=Kamo-no-agatanushi|12=draft|12=draft}} clan (賀茂県主氏).
There are multiple spellings so this article will cover all of them

'''Kamo Shrine''' (加茂神社・賀茂神社) refers to Shinto shrines with the name "Kamo."
Most of these shrines were branches of the famous {{ill|Kamo Shrines|ja|賀茂神社|12=draft|12=draft}}, {{ill|Kamigamo Shrine|ja|賀茂別雷神社|12=draft|12=draft}} (Upper Kamo Shrine) and {{ill|Shimogamo Shrine|ja|賀茂御祖神社|12=draft|12=draft}} (Lower Kamo Shrine), located in Kyoto. Many shrines are also historically tied to the ancient {{ill|Kamo clan|ja|賀茂県主氏|Kamo clan=Kamo clan|lt=Kamo-no-agatanushi|12=draft|12=draft}} clan (賀茂県主氏).
There are multiple spellings so this article will cover all of them

== Kamo Shrine ([[:ja:鴨神社|鴨神社]]) ==
'''Kamo Shrine''' (鴨神社) is a Shinto shrine whose name includes the character "Kamo" (鴨).

Another name for {{ill|Kamo Shrines|ja|賀茂神社|12=draft|12=draft}}, which collectively refers to {{ill|Kamigamo Shrine|ja|賀茂別雷神社|12=draft|12=draft}} and {{ill|Shimogamo '''Kamo Shrine''' (加茂神社・賀茂神社) refers to Shinto shrines with the name "Kamo."
Most of these shrines were branches of the famous {{ill|Kamo Shrines|ja|賀茂神社|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, {{ill|Kamigamo Shrine|ja|賀茂別雷神社|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (Upper Kamo Shrine) and {{ill|Shimogamo Shrine|ja|賀茂御祖神社|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (Lower Kamo Shrine), located in Kyoto. Many shrines are also historically tied to the ancient {{ill|Kamo clan|ja|賀茂県主氏|Kamo clan=Kamo clan|lt=Kamo-no-agatanushi|qq=draft|qq=draft}} clan (賀茂県主氏).
There are multiple spellings so this article will cover all of them

<!--for some reason this has an independent Japanese article [[:ja:鴨神社]]--><!--note do not remove this category during translation--><!--Kamo Shrine (disambiguation)--><!--Kamo Shrine (disambiguation)-->

'''Kamo Shrine''' (加茂神社・賀茂神社) refers to Shinto shrines with the name "Kamo."
Most of these shrines were branches of the famous {{ill|Kamo Shrines|ja|賀茂神社|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, {{ill|Kamigamo Shrine|ja|賀茂別雷神社|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (Upper Kamo Shrine) and {{ill|Shimogamo Shrine|ja|賀茂御祖神社|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (Lower Kamo Shrine), located in Kyoto. Many shrines are also historically tied to the ancient {{ill|Kamo clan|ja|賀茂県主氏|Kamo clan=Kamo clan|lt=Kamo-no-agatanushi|qq=draft|qq=draft}} clan (賀茂県主氏).
There are multiple spellings so this article will cover all of them

'''Kamo Shrine''' (加茂神社・賀茂神社) refers to Shinto shrines with the name "Kamo."
Most of these shrines were branches of the famous {{ill|Kamo Shrines|ja|賀茂神社|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, {{ill|Kamigamo Shrine|ja|賀茂別雷神社|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (Upper Kamo Shrine) and {{ill|Shimogamo Shrine|ja|賀茂御祖神社|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (Lower Kamo Shrine), located in Kyoto. Many shrines are also historically tied to the ancient {{ill|Kamo clan|ja|賀茂県主氏|Kamo clan=Kamo clan|lt=Kamo-no-agatanushi|qq=draft|qq=draft}} clan (賀茂県主氏).
There are multiple spellings so this article will cover all of them

== Kamo Shrine ([[:ja:鴨神社|鴨神社]]) ==
'''Kamo Shrine''' (鴨神社) is a Shinto shrine whose name includes the character "Kamo" (鴨).
* [[鴨神社_(玉城町)]] * {{ill|Kamo Shrine (Kamo-sha)|ja|賀茂神社|lt=Kamo Shrine (Kamo-sha)|lt_ja=鴨社|qq=draft|qq=draft}} ({{ill|Kyoto|ja|京都府|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, {{ill|Kyoto City|ja|京都市|qq=draft|qq=draft}}) - Another name for {{ill|Kamo Shrines|ja|賀茂神社|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, which collectively refers to {{ill|Kamigamo Shrine|ja|賀茂別雷神社|qq=draft|qq=draft}} and {{ill|Shimogamo Shrine|ja|賀茂御祖神社|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. * {{ill|Kamo Shrine (Tamaki)|ja|鴨神社 (玉城町)|lt=Kamo Shrine|lt_ja=鴨神社|qq=draft|qq=draft}} ({{ill|Mie Prefecture|ja|三重県|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, {{ill|Tamaki|ja|玉城町|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, Yamagami) * {{ill|Kamo Shrine (Takatuki)|ja|鴨神社 (高槻市)|lt=Kamo Shrine|lt_ja=鴨神社|qq=draft|qq=draft}} ({{ill|Osaka Prefecture|ja|大阪府|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, {{ill|Kawanishi|ja|川西市|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, Kamo) * {{ill|Kamo Shrine (Tamano)|ja|鴨神社 (玉野市)|lt=Kamo Shrine|lt_ja=鴨神社|qq=draft|qq=draft}} ({{ill|Okayama Prefecture|ja|岡山県|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, {{ill|Tamano|ja|玉野市|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, Nagao) * {{ill|Kamo Shrine (Akaiwa)|ja|鴨神社 (赤磐市)|lt=Kamo Shrine|lt_ja=鴨神社|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (Okayama Prefecture, {{ill|Akaiwa|ja|赤磐市|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, Nibori-nishi) * {{ill|Kamo Shrine (Asakuchi)|ja|鴨神社 (浅口市)|lt=Kamo Shrine|lt_ja=鴨神社|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (Okayama Prefecture, {{ill|Asakuchi|ja|浅口市|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, Kamogata-chō Kamo) * {{ill|Kamo Shrine (Kibichuo)|ja|鴨神社 (吉備中央町)|lt=Kamo Shrine|lt_ja=鴨神社|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (Okayama Prefecture, {{ill|Kibichuo|ja|吉備中央町|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, Kamikamo) * {{ill|Kamo Shrine (Sanyo-Onoda)|ja|鴨神社 (山陽小野田市)|lt=Kamo Shrine|lt_ja=鴨神社|qq=draft|qq=draft}} ({{ill|Yamaguchi Prefecture|ja|山口県|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, {{ill|Sanyo-Onoda|ja|山陽小野田市|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, Asa) * {{ill|Kamo Shrine (Higashimiyoshi)|ja|鴨神社 (東みよし町)|lt=Kamo Shrine|lt_ja=鴨神社|qq=draft|qq=draft}} ({{ill|Tokushima Prefecture|ja|徳島県|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, {{ill|Higashimiyoshi|ja|東みよし町|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, Kamo) * {{ill|Kamo Shrine (Sakaide)|ja|鴨神社 (坂出市)|lt=Kamo Shrine|lt_ja=鴨神社|qq=draft|qq=draft}} - An {{ill|Ancient shrine of Sanuki Province|ja|讃岐国|阿野郡|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. Theories of its location include {{ill|Higashi Kamo Shrine|ja|鴨神社 (坂出市)#東鴨神社|lt=Higashi Kamo Shrine|qq=draft|qq=draft}} and {{ill|Nishi Kamo Shrine|ja|鴨神社 (坂出市)#西鴨神社|lt=Nishi Kamo Shrine|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (both in {{ill|Sakaide|ja|坂出市|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, Kamochō).

revision.text:
'''Kamo Shrine''' (加茂神社・賀茂神社) refers to Shinto shrines with the name "Kamo."
Most of these shrines were branches of the famous {{ill|Kamo Shrines|ja|賀茂神社|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, {{ill|Kamigamo Shrine|ja|賀茂別雷神社|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (Upper Kamo Shrine) and {{ill|Shimogamo Shrine|ja|賀茂御祖神社|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (Lower Kamo Shrine), located in Kyoto. Many shrines are also historically tied to the ancient {{ill|Kamo clan|ja|賀茂県主氏|Kamo clan=Kamo clan|lt=Kamo-no-agatanushi|qq=draft|qq=draft}} clan (賀茂県主氏).
There are multiple spellings so this article will cover all of them

== Kamo Shrine ([[:ja:鴨神社|鴨神社]]) ==
'''Kamo Shrine''' (鴨神社) is a Shinto shrine whose name includes the character "Kamo" (鴨).
* [[鴨神社_(玉城町)]] * {{ill|Kamo Shrine (Kamo-sha)|ja|賀茂神社|lt=Kamo Shrine (Kamo-sha)|lt_ja=鴨社|qq=draft|qq=draft}} ({{ill|Kyoto|ja|京都府|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, {{ill|Kyoto City|ja|京都市|qq=draft|qq=draft}}) - Another name for {{ill|Kamo Shrines|ja|賀茂神社|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, which collectively refers to {{ill|Kamigamo Shrine|ja|賀茂別雷神社|qq=draft|qq=draft}} and {{ill|Shimogamo Shrine|ja|賀茂御祖神社|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. * {{ill|Kamo Shrine (Tamaki)|ja|鴨神社 (玉城町)|lt=Kamo Shrine|lt_ja=鴨神社|qq=draft|qq=draft}} ({{ill|Mie Prefecture|ja|三重県|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, {{ill|Tamaki|ja|玉城町|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, Yamagami) * {{ill|Kamo Shrine (Takatuki)|ja|鴨神社 (高槻市)|lt=Kamo Shrine|lt_ja=鴨神社|qq=draft|qq=draft}} ({{ill|Osaka Prefecture|ja|大阪府|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, {{ill|Takatuki|ja|高槻市|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, Akaōji-chō) * {{ill|Kamo Shrine (Kawanishi)|ja|鴨神社 (川西市)|lt=Kamo Shrine|lt_ja=鴨神社|qq=draft|qq=draft}} ({{ill|Hyogo Prefecture|ja|兵庫県|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, {{ill|Kawanishi|ja|川西市|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, Kamo) * {{ill|Kamo Shrine (Tamano)|ja|鴨神社 (玉野市)|lt=Kamo Shrine|lt_ja=鴨神社|qq=draft|qq=draft}} ({{ill|Okayama Prefecture|ja|岡山県|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, {{ill|Tamano|ja|玉野市|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, Nagao) * {{ill|Kamo Shrine (Akaiwa)|ja|鴨神社 (赤磐市)|lt=Kamo Shrine|lt_ja=鴨神社|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (Okayama Prefecture, {{ill|Akaiwa|ja|赤磐市|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, Nibori-nishi) * {{ill|Kamo Shrine (Asakuchi)|ja|鴨神社 (浅口市)|lt=Kamo Shrine|lt_ja=鴨神社|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (Okayama Prefecture, {{ill|Asakuchi|ja|浅口市|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, Kamogata-chō Kamo) * {{ill|Kamo Shrine (Kibichuo)|ja|鴨神社 (吉備中央町)|lt=Kamo Shrine|lt_ja=鴨神社|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (Okayama Prefecture, {{ill|Kibichuo|ja|吉備中央町|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, Kamikamo) * {{ill|Kamo Shrine (Sanyo-Onoda)|ja|鴨神社 (山陽小野田市)|lt=Kamo Shrine|lt_ja=鴨神社|qq=draft|qq=draft}} ({{ill|Yamaguchi Prefecture|ja|山口県|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, {{ill|Sanyo-Onoda|ja|山陽小野田市|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, Asa) * {{ill|Kamo Shrine (Higashimiyoshi)|ja|鴨神社 (東みよし町)|lt=Kamo Shrine|lt_ja=鴨神社|qq=draft|qq=draft}} ({{ill|Tokushima Prefecture|ja|徳島県|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, {{ill|Higashimiyoshi|ja|東みよし町|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, Kamo) * {{ill|Kamo Shrine (Sakaide)|ja|鴨神社 (坂出市)|lt=Kamo Shrine|lt_ja=鴨神社|qq=draft|qq=draft}} - An {{ill|Ancient shrine of Sanuki Province|ja|讃岐国|阿野郡|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. Theories of its location include {{ill|Higashi Kamo Shrine|ja|鴨神社 (坂出市)#東鴨神社|lt=Higashi Kamo Shrine|qq=draft|qq=draft}} and {{ill|Nishi Kamo Shrine|ja|鴨神社 (坂出市)#西鴨神社|lt=Nishi Kamo Shrine|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (both in {{ill|Sakaide|ja|坂出市|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, Kamochō).

revision.text:
'''Kamo Shrine''' (加茂神社・賀茂神社) refers to Shinto shrines with the name "Kamo."
Most of these shrines were branches of the famous {{ill|Kamo Shrines|ja|賀茂神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamo Shrines|qq=}}, {{ill|Kamigamo Shrine|ja|賀茂別雷神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamigamo Shrine|qq=}} (Upper Kamo Shrine) and {{ill|Shimogamo Shrine|ja|賀茂御祖神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shimogamo Shrine|qq=}} (Lower Kamo Shrine), located in Kyoto. Many shrines are also historically tied to the ancient {{ill|Kamo clan|ja|賀茂県主氏|Kamo clan=Kamo clan|lt=Kamo-no-agatanushi|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamo clan|qq=}} clan (賀茂県主氏).
There are multiple spellings so this article will cover all of them

<!--for some reason this has an independent Japanese article [[:ja:鴨神社]]--><!--note do not remove this category during translation--><!--Kamo Shrine (disambiguation)--><!--Kamo Shrine (disambiguation)-->

'''Kamo Shrine''' (加茂神社・賀茂神社) refers to Shinto shrines with the name "Kamo."
Most of these shrines were branches of the famous {{ill|Kamo Shrines|ja|賀茂神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamo Shrines|qq=}}, {{ill|Kamigamo Shrine|ja|賀茂別雷神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamigamo Shrine|qq=}} (Upper Kamo Shrine) and {{ill|Shimogamo Shrine|ja|賀茂御祖神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shimogamo Shrine|qq=}} (Lower Kamo Shrine), located in Kyoto. Many shrines are also historically tied to the ancient {{ill|Kamo clan|ja|賀茂県主氏|Kamo clan=Kamo clan|lt=Kamo-no-agatanushi|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamo clan|qq=}} clan (賀茂県主氏).
There are multiple spellings so this article will cover all of them

== Kamo Shrine ([[:ja:鴨神社|鴨神社]]) ==

revision.text:
'''Kamo Shrine''' (加茂神社・賀茂神社) refers to Shinto shrines with the name "Kamo."
Most of these shrines were branches of the famous {{ill|Kamo Shrines|ja|賀茂神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamo Shrines|qq=}}, {{ill|Kamigamo Shrine|ja|賀茂別雷神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamigamo Shrine|qq=}} (Upper Kamo Shrine) and {{ill|Shimogamo Shrine|ja|賀茂御祖神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shimogamo Shrine|qq=}} (Lower Kamo Shrine), located in Kyoto. Many shrines are also historically tied to the ancient {{ill|Kamo clan|ja|賀茂県主氏|Kamo clan=Kamo clan|lt=Kamo-no-agatanushi|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamo clan|qq=}} clan (賀茂県主氏).
There are multiple spellings so this article will cover all of them

revision.text:
'''Kamo Shrine''' (加茂神社・賀茂神社) refers to Shinto shrines with the name "Kamo."
Most of these shrines were branches of the famous [[:en:Kamo shrines|Kamo Shrines]], [[:en:Kamigamo Shrine|Kamigamo Shrine]] (Upper Kamo Shrine) and [[:en:Shimogamo Shrine|Shimogamo Shrine]] (Lower Kamo Shrine), located in Kyoto. Many shrines are also historically tied to the ancient [[:en:Kamo clan|Kamo-no-agatanushi]] clan (賀茂県主氏).
There are multiple spellings so this article will cover all of them

== Kamo Shrine ([[:ja:鴨神社|鴨神社]]) ==
'''Kamo Shrine''' (鴨神社) is a Shinto shrine whose name includes the character "Kamo" (鴨).

revision.comment: Bot: add category Category:加茂神社
revision.text:
'''Kamo Shrine''' (加茂神社・賀茂神社) refers to Shinto shrines with the name "Kamo."
Most of these shrines were branches of the famous [[:en:Kamo shrines|Kamo Shrines]], [[:en:Kamigamo Shrine|Kamigamo Shrine]] (Upper Kamo Shrine) and [[:en:Shimogamo Shrine|Shimogamo Shrine]] (Lower Kamo Shrine), located in Kyoto. Many shrines are also historically tied to the ancient [[:en:Kamo clan|Kamo-no-agatanushi]] clan (賀茂県主氏).
There are multiple spellings so this article will cover all of them

== Kamo Shrine ([[:ja:鴨神社|鴨神社]]) ==
'''Kamo Shrine''' (鴨神社) is a Shinto shrine whose name includes the character "Kamo" (鴨).
* [[鴨神社_(玉城町)]] * [[:en:Kamo shrines|Kamo Shrine (Kamo-sha)]] ([[:en:Kyoto Prefecture|Kyoto]], [[:en:Kyoto|Kyoto City]]) - Another name for [[:en:Kamo shrines|Kamo Shrines]], which collectively refers to [[:en:Kamigamo Shrine|Kamigamo Shrine]] and [[:en:Shimogamo Shrine|Shimogamo Shrine]]. * {{ill|Kamo Shrine (Tamaki)|ja|Kamo Shrine (Ise)|lt=Kamo Shrine (Ise)|lt=鴨神社 (玉城町)|lt=Kamo ([[:en:Okayama Prefecture|Okayama Prefecture]], [[:en:Tamano|Tamano]], Nagao) * {{ill|Kamo Shrine (Akaiwa)|ja|鴨神社 (赤磐市)|lt=Kamo Shrine|lt_ja=鴨神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamo revision.text:
'''Kamo Shrine''' (加茂神社・賀茂神社) refers to Shinto shrines with the name "Kamo."
Most of these shrines were branches of the famous [[:en:Kamo shrines|Kamo Shrines]], [[:en:Kamigamo Shrine|Kamigamo Shrine]] (Upper Kamo Shrine) and [[:en:Shimogamo Shrine|Shimogamo Shrine]] (Lower Kamo Shrine), located in Kyoto. Many shrines are also historically tied to the ancient [[:en:Kamo clan|Kamo-no-agatanushi]] clan (賀茂県主氏).
There are multiple spellings so this article will cover all of them

== Kamo Shrine ([[:ja:鴨神社|鴨神社]]) ==
'''Kamo Shrine''' (鴨神社) is a Shinto shrine whose name includes the character "Kamo" (鴨).

<!--for some reason this has an independent Japanese article [[:ja:鴨神社]]--><!--note do not remove this category during translation--><!--Kamo Shrine (disambiguation)--><!--Kamo Shrine (disambiguation)--> [[Category:加茂神社]] [[Category:同名の神社]]

'''Kamo Shrine''' (加茂神社・賀茂神社) refers to Shinto shrines with the name "Kamo."
Most of these shrines were branches of the famous [[:en:Kamo shrines|Kamo Shrines]], [[:en:Kamigamo Shrine|Kamigamo Shrine]] (Upper Kamo Shrine) and [[:en:Shimogamo Shrine|Shimogamo Shrine]] (Lower Kamo Shrine), located in Kyoto. Many shrines are also historically tied to the ancient [[:en:Kamo clan|Kamo-no-agatanushi]] clan (賀茂県主氏).
There are multiple spellings so this article will cover all of them

== Kamo Shrine ([[:ja:鴨神社|鴨神社]]) ==
'''Kamo Shrine''' (鴨神社) is a Shinto shrine whose name includes the character "Kamo" (鴨).
* [[鴨神社_(玉城町)]] * [[:en:Kamo shrines|Kamo Shrine (Kamo-sha)]] ([[:en:Kyoto Prefecture|Kyoto]], [[:en:Kyoto|Kyoto City]]) - Another name for [[:en:Kamo shrines|Kamo Shrines]], which collectively refers to [[:en:Kamigamo Shrine|Kamigamo Shrine]] and [[:en:Shimogamo Shrine|Shimogamo Shrine]]. * {{ill|Kamo Shrine (Tamaki)|ja|Kamo Shrine (Ise)|lt=Kamo Shrine (Ise)|lt=鴨神社 (玉城町)|lt=Kamo Shrine|lt_ja=鴨神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamo Shrine (Tamaki)|qq=}} ([[:en:Mie Prefecture|Mie Prefecture]], [[:en:Tamaki, Mie|Tamaki]], Yamagami) * {{ill|Kamo Shrine ([[:en:Okayama Prefecture|Okayama Prefecture]], [[:en:Tamano|Tamano]], Nagao) * {{ill|Kamo Shrine (Akaiwa)|ja|鴨神社 (赤磐市)|lt=Kamo Shrine|lt_ja=鴨神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamo revision.text:
'''Kamo Shrine''' (加茂神社・賀茂神社) refers to Shinto shrines with the name "Kamo."
Most of these shrines were branches of the famous [[:en:Kamo shrines|Kamo Shrines]], [[:en:Kamigamo Shrine|Kamigamo Shrine]] (Upper Kamo Shrine) and [[:en:Shimogamo Shrine|Shimogamo Shrine]] (Lower Kamo Shrine), located in Kyoto. Many shrines are also historically tied to the ancient [[:en:Kamo clan|Kamo-no-agatanushi]] clan (賀茂県主氏).
There are multiple spellings so this article will cover all of them

== Kamo Shrine ([[:ja:鴨神社|鴨神社]]) ==

<!--for some reason this has an independent Japanese article [[:ja:鴨神社]]--><!--note do not remove this category during translation--><!--Kamo Shrine (disambiguation)--><!--Kamo Shrine (disambiguation)--> [[Category:加茂神社]] [[Category:Shrine disambiguations]] [[Category:Disambiguation pages]]

'''Kamo Shrine''' (加茂神社・賀茂神社) refers to Shinto shrines with the name "Kamo."
Most of these shrines were branches of the famous [[:en:Kamo shrines|Kamo Shrines]], [[:en:Kamigamo Shrine|Kamigamo Shrine]] (Upper Kamo Shrine) and [[:en:Shimogamo Shrine|Shimogamo Shrine]] (Lower Kamo Shrine), located in Kyoto. Many shrines are also historically tied to the ancient [[:en:Kamo clan|Kamo-no-agatanushi]] clan (賀茂県主氏).
There are multiple spellings so this article will cover all of them

== Kamo Shrine ([[:ja:鴨神社|鴨神社]]) ==
'''Kamo Shrine''' (鴨神社) is a Shinto shrine whose name includes the character "Kamo" (鴨).
* [[鴨神社_(玉城町)]] * [[:en:Kamo shrines|Kamo Shrine (Kamo-sha)]] ([[:en:Kyoto Prefecture|Kyoto]], [[:en:Kyoto|Kyoto City]]) - Another name for [[:en:Kamo shrines|Kamo Shrines]], which collectively refers to [[:en:Kamigamo Shrine|Kamigamo Shrine]] and [[:en:Shimogamo Shrine|Shimogamo Shrine]]. * {{ill|Kamo Shrine (Tamaki)|ja|Kamo Shrine (Ise)|lt=Kamo Shrine (Ise)|lt=鴨神社 (玉城町)|lt=Kamo revision.text:
'''Kamo Shrine''' (加茂神社・賀茂神社) refers to Shinto shrines with the name "Kamo."
Most of these shrines were branches of the famous [[:en:Kamo shrines|Kamo Shrines]], [[:en:Kamigamo Shrine|Kamigamo Shrine]] (Upper Kamo Shrine) and [[:en:Shimogamo Shrine|Shimogamo Shrine]] (Lower Kamo Shrine), located in Kyoto. Many shrines are also historically tied to the ancient [[:en:Kamo clan|Kamo-no-agatanushi]] clan (賀茂県主氏).
There are multiple spellings so this article will cover all of them

== Kamo Shrine ([[:ja:鴨神社|鴨神社]]) ==
'''Kamo Shrine''' (鴨神社) is a Shinto shrine whose name includes the character "Kamo" (鴨).

<!--for some reason this has an independent Japanese article [[:ja:鴨神社]]--><!--note do not remove this category during translation--><!--Kamo Shrine (disambiguation)--><!--Kamo Shrine (disambiguation)--> [[Category:加茂神社]] [[Category:Shrine disambiguations]] [[Category:Disambiguation pages]] <!--jawiki derived wikidata interwikis--> [[Category:translated pages with valid en interwikis]]

'''Kamo Shrine''' (加茂神社・賀茂神社) refers to Shinto shrines with the name "Kamo."
Most of these shrines were branches of the famous [[:en:Kamo shrines|Kamo Shrines]], [[:en:Kamigamo Shrine|Kamigamo Shrine]] (Upper Kamo Shrine) and [[:en:Shimogamo Shrine|Shimogamo Shrine]] (Lower Kamo Shrine), located in Kyoto. Many shrines are also historically tied to the ancient [[:en:Kamo clan|Kamo-no-agatanushi]] clan (賀茂県主氏).
There are multiple spellings so this article will cover all of them

== Kamo Shrine ([[:ja:鴨神社|鴨神社]]) ==
'''Kamo Shrine''' (鴨神社) is a Shinto shrine whose name includes the character "Kamo" (鴨).

to [[:en:Kamigamo Shrine|Kamigamo Shrine]] and [[:en:Shimogamo Shrine|Shimogamo Shrine]]. * {{ill|Kamo Shrine (Tamaki)|ja|Kamo Shrine (Ise)|lt=Kamo Shrine (Ise)|lt=鴨神社 (玉城町)|lt=Kamo revision.text:
'''Kamo Shrine''' (加茂神社・賀茂神社) refers to Shinto shrines with the name "Kamo."
Most of these shrines were branches of the famous [[:en:Kamo shrines|Kamo Shrines]], [[:en:Kamigamo Shrine|Kamigamo Shrine]] (Upper Kamo Shrine) and [[:en:Shimogamo Shrine|Shimogamo Shrine]] (Lower Kamo Shrine), located in Kyoto. Many shrines are also historically tied to the ancient [[:en:Kamo clan|Kamo-no-agatanushi]] clan (賀茂県主氏).
There are multiple spellings so this article will cover all of them

== Kamo Shrine ([[:ja:鴨神社|鴨神社]]) ==
'''Kamo Shrine''' (鴨神社) is a Shinto shrine whose name includes the character "Kamo" (鴨).
* [[鴨神社_(玉城町)]] * [[:en:Kamo shrines|Kamo Shrine (Kamo-sha)]] ([[:en:Kyoto Prefecture|Kyoto]], [[:en:Kyoto|Kyoto City]]) - Another name for [[:en:Kamo shrines|Kamo Shrines]], which collectively refers to [[:en:Kamigamo Shrine|Kamigamo Shrine]] and [[:en:Shimogamo Shrine|Shimogamo Shrine]]. * {{ill|Kamo Shrine (Tamaki)|ja|Kamo Shrine (Ise)|lt=Kamo Shrine (Ise)|lt=鴨神社 (玉城町)|lt=Kamo revision.comment: Bot: remove deleted category Kamo Shrine (disambiguation)
revision.text:
'''Kamo Shrine''' (加茂神社・賀茂神社) refers to Shinto shrines with the name "Kamo."
Most of these shrines were branches of the famous [[:en:Kamo shrines|Kamo Shrines]], [[:en:Kamigamo Shrine|Kamigamo Shrine]] (Upper Kamo Shrine) and [[:en:Shimogamo Shrine|Shimogamo Shrine]] (Lower Kamo Shrine), located in Kyoto. Many shrines are also historically tied to the ancient [[:en:Kamo clan|Kamo-no-agatanushi]] clan (賀茂県主氏).
There are multiple spellings so this article will cover all of them
== Kamo Shrine ([[:ja:加茂神社|加茂神社]]) == === Tohoku === * {{ill|Kamo Shrine (Osaki)|ja|加茂神社 (大崎市)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamo Shrine (Osaki)|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (Osaki City, Miyagi Prefecture) * {{ill|Kamo Shrine (Yamagata)|ja|加茂神社 (山形市)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamo Shrine (Yamagata)|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (Yamagata City, Yamagata Prefecture)

== Kamo Shrine ([[:ja:鴨神社|鴨神社]]) ==
'''Kamo Shrine''' (鴨神社) is a Shinto shrine whose name includes the character "Kamo" (鴨).
* [[鴨神社_(玉城町)]] * [[:en:Kamo shrines|Kamo Shrine (Kamo-sha)]] ([[:en:Kyoto Prefecture|Kyoto]], [[:en:Kyoto|Kyoto City]]) - Another name for [[:en:Kamo shrines|Kamo Shrines]], which collectively refers to [[:en:Kamigamo Shrine|Kamigamo Shrine]] and [[:en:Shimogamo Shrine|Shimogamo Shrine]]. * {{ill|Kamo Shrine (Tamaki)|ja|Kamo Shrine (Ise)|lt=Kamo Shrine (Ise)|lt=鴨神社 (玉城町)|lt=Kamo revision.comment: Bot: remove deleted category Kamo Shrines
revision.text:
'''Kamo Shrine''' (加茂神社・賀茂神社) refers to Shinto shrines with the name "Kamo."
Most of these shrines were branches of the famous [[:en:Kamo shrines|Kamo Shrines]], [[:en:Kamigamo Shrine|Kamigamo Shrine]] (Upper Kamo Shrine) and [[:en:Shimogamo Shrine|Shimogamo Shrine]] (Lower Kamo Shrine), located in Kyoto. Many shrines are also historically tied to the ancient [[:en:Kamo clan|Kamo-no-agatanushi]] clan (賀茂県主氏).
There are multiple spellings so this article will cover all of them
== Kamo Shrine ([[:ja:加茂神社|加茂神社]]) == === Tohoku === * {{ill|Kamo Shrine (Osaki)|ja|加茂神社 (大崎市)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamo Shrine (Osaki)|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (Osaki City, Miyagi Prefecture) * {{ill|Kamo Shrine (Yamagata)|ja|加茂神社 (山形市)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamo Shrine (Yamagata)|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (Yamagata City, Yamagata Prefecture)

== Kamo Shrine ([[:ja:鴨神社|鴨神社]]) ==
'''Kamo Shrine''' (鴨神社) is a Shinto shrine whose name includes the character "Kamo" (鴨).

to [[:en:Kamigamo Shrine|Kamigamo Shrine]] and [[:en:Shimogamo Shrine|Shimogamo Shrine]]. * {{ill|Kamo Shrine (Tamaki)|ja|Kamo Shrine (Ise)|lt=Kamo Shrine (Ise)|lt=鴨神社 (玉城町)|lt=Kamo revision.comment: Merge [[Category:加茂神社]] into [[Category:Kamo Shrines]] via bot
revision.text:
'''Kamo Shrine''' (加茂神社・賀茂神社) refers to Shinto shrines with the name "Kamo."
Most of these shrines were branches of the famous [[:en:Kamo shrines|Kamo Shrines]], [[:en:Kamigamo Shrine|Kamigamo Shrine]] (Upper Kamo Shrine) and [[:en:Shimogamo Shrine|Shimogamo Shrine]] (Lower Kamo Shrine), located in Kyoto. Many shrines are also historically tied to the ancient [[:en:Kamo clan|Kamo-no-agatanushi]] clan (賀茂県主氏).
There are multiple spellings so this article will cover all of them
== Kamo Shrine ([[:ja:加茂神社|加茂神社]]) == === Tohoku === * {{ill|Kamo Shrine (Osaki)|ja|加茂神社 (大崎市)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamo Shrine (Osaki)|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (Osaki City, Miyagi Prefecture) * {{ill|Kamo Shrine (Yamagata)|ja|加茂神社 (山形市)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamo Shrine (Yamagata)|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (Yamagata City, Yamagata Prefecture)

== Kamo Shrine ([[:ja:鴨神社|鴨神社]]) ==
'''Kamo Shrine''' (鴨神社) is a Shinto shrine whose name includes the character "Kamo" (鴨).

to [[:en:Kamigamo Shrine|Kamigamo Shrine]] and [[:en:Shimogamo Shrine|Shimogamo Shrine]]. * {{ill|Kamo Shrine (Tamaki)|ja|Kamo Shrine (Ise)|lt=Kamo Shrine (Ise)|lt=鴨神社 (玉城町)|lt=Kamo revision.text:
{{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q11398885]] Wikidata name: (none) [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 03:59, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} '''Kamo Shrine''' (加茂神社・賀茂神社) refers to Shinto shrines with the name "Kamo."
Most of these shrines were branches of the famous [[:en:Kamo shrines|Kamo Shrines]], [[:en:Kamigamo Shrine|Kamigamo Shrine]] (Upper Kamo Shrine) and [[:en:Shimogamo Shrine|Shimogamo Shrine]] (Lower Kamo Shrine), located in Kyoto. Many shrines are also historically tied to the ancient [[:en:Kamo clan|Kamo-no-agatanushi]] clan (賀茂県主氏).
There are multiple spellings so this article will cover all of them

== Kamo Shrine ([[:ja:鴨神社|鴨神社]]) ==
'''Kamo Shrine''' (鴨神社) is a Shinto shrine whose name includes the character "Kamo" (鴨).
* [[鴨神社_(玉城町)]] * [[:en:Kamo shrines|Kamo Shrine (Kamo-sha)]] ([[:en:Kyoto Prefecture|Kyoto]], [[:en:Kyoto|Kyoto City]]) - Another name for [[:en:Kamo shrines|Kamo Shrines]], which collectively refers to [[:en:Kamigamo Shrine|Kamigamo Shrine]] and [[:en:Shimogamo Shrine|Shimogamo Shrine]]. * {{ill|Kamo Shrine (Tamaki)|ja|Kamo Shrine (Ise)|lt=Kamo Shrine (Ise)|lt=鴨神社 (玉城町)|lt=Kamo Shrine|lt_ja=鴨神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamo Shrine (Tamaki)|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} ([[:en:Mie Prefecture|Mie Prefecture]], [[:en:Tamaki, Mie|Tamaki]], Yamagami) revision.text:
{{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q11398885]] Wikidata name: (none) [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 07:19, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q11398885]] Wikidata name: (none) [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 03:59, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} '''Kamo Shrine''' (加茂神社・賀茂神社) refers to Shinto shrines with the name "Kamo."
Most of these shrines were branches of the famous [[:en:Kamo shrines|Kamo Shrines]], [[:en:Kamigamo Shrine|Kamigamo Shrine]] (Upper Kamo Shrine) and [[:en:Shimogamo Shrine|Shimogamo Shrine]] (Lower Kamo Shrine), located in Kyoto. Many shrines are also historically tied to the ancient [[:en:Kamo clan|Kamo-no-agatanushi]] clan (賀茂県主氏).
There are multiple spellings so this article will cover all of them

== Kamo Shrine ([[:ja:鴨神社|鴨神社]]) ==
'''Kamo Shrine''' (鴨神社) is a Shinto shrine whose name includes the character "Kamo" (鴨).
* [[鴨神社_(玉城町)]] * [[:en:Kamo shrines|Kamo Shrine (Kamo-sha)]] ([[:en:Kyoto Prefecture|Kyoto]], [[:en:Kyoto|Kyoto City]]) - Another name for [[:en:Kamo shrines|Kamo Shrines]], which collectively refers to [[:en:Kamigamo Shrine|Kamigamo Shrine]] and [[:en:Shimogamo Shrine|Shimogamo Shrine]]. * {{ill|Kamo Shrine (Tamaki)|ja|Kamo Shrine (Ise)|lt=Kamo Shrine (Ise)|lt=鴨神社 (玉城町)|lt=Kamo <!--for some reason this has an independent Japanese article [[:ja:鴨神社]]--><!--note do not remove this category during translation--><!--Kamo Shrine (disambiguation)--><!--Kamo Shrine (disambiguation)--> [[Category:Kamo Shrines]] [[Category:Shrine disambiguations]] [[Category:Disambiguation pages]] <!--jawiki derived wikidata interwikis--> [[Category:translated pages with valid en interwikis]] [[Category:pages without english wikidata name]] [[Category:pages without english wikidata name]]

{{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q11398885]] Wikidata name: (none) [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 07:19, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q11398885]] Wikidata name: (none) [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 03:59, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} '''Kamo Shrine''' (加茂神社・賀茂神社) refers to Shinto shrines with the name "Kamo."
Most of these shrines were branches of the famous [[:en:Kamo shrines|Kamo Shrines]], [[:en:Kamigamo Shrine|Kamigamo Shrine]] (Upper Kamo Shrine) and [[:en:Shimogamo Shrine|Shimogamo Shrine]] (Lower Kamo Shrine), located in Kyoto. Many shrines are also historically tied to the ancient [[:en:Kamo clan|Kamo-no-agatanushi]] clan (賀茂県主氏).
There are multiple spellings so this article will cover all of them

== Kamo Shrine ([[:ja:鴨神社|鴨神社]]) ==
'''Kamo Shrine''' (鴨神社) is a Shinto shrine whose name includes the character "Kamo" (鴨).

{{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q11398885]] Wikidata name: (none) [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 07:19, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q11398885]] Wikidata name: (none) [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 03:59, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} '''Kamo Shrine''' (加茂神社・賀茂神社) refers to Shinto shrines with the name "Kamo."
Most of these shrines were branches of the famous [[:en:Kamo shrines|Kamo Shrines]], [[:en:Kamigamo Shrine|Kamigamo Shrine]] (Upper Kamo Shrine) and [[:en:Shimogamo Shrine|Shimogamo Shrine]] (Lower Kamo Shrine), located in Kyoto. Many shrines are also historically tied to the ancient [[:en:Kamo clan|Kamo-no-agatanushi]] clan (賀茂県主氏).
There are multiple spellings so this article will cover all of them

== Kamo Shrine ([[:ja:鴨神社|鴨神社]]) ==
'''Kamo Shrine''' (鴨神社) is a Shinto shrine whose name includes the character "Kamo" (鴨).
* [[鴨神社_(玉城町)]] * [[:en:Kamo shrines|Kamo Shrine (Kamo-sha)]] ([[:en:Kyoto Prefecture|Kyoto]], [[:en:Kyoto|Kyoto City]]) - Another name for [[:en:Kamo shrines|Kamo Shrines]], which collectively refers to [[:en:Kamigamo Shrine|Kamigamo Shrine]] and [[:en:Shimogamo Shrine|Shimogamo Shrine]]. * {{ill|Kamo Shrine (Tamaki)|ja|Kamo Shrine (Ise)|lt=Kamo Shrine (Ise)|lt=鴨神社 (玉城町)|lt=Kamo {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q11398885]] Wikidata name: (none) [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 07:19, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q11398885]] Wikidata name: (none) [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 03:59, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} '''Kamo Shrine''' (加茂神社・賀茂神社) refers to Shinto shrines with the name "Kamo."
Most of these shrines were branches of the famous [[:en:Kamo shrines|Kamo Shrines]], [[:en:Kamigamo Shrine|Kamigamo Shrine]] (Upper Kamo Shrine) and [[:en:Shimogamo Shrine|Shimogamo Shrine]] (Lower Kamo Shrine), located in Kyoto. Many shrines are also historically tied to the ancient [[:en:Kamo clan|Kamo-no-agatanushi]] clan (賀茂県主氏).
There are multiple spellings so this article will cover all of them

== Kamo Shrine ([[:ja:鴨神社|鴨神社]]) ==
'''Kamo Shrine''' (鴨神社) is a Shinto shrine whose name includes the character "Kamo" (鴨).

{{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q11398885]] Wikidata name: (none) [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 07:19, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q11398885]] Wikidata name: (none) [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 03:59, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} '''Kamo Shrine''' (加茂神社・賀茂神社) refers to Shinto shrines with the name "Kamo."
Most of these shrines were branches of the famous [[:en:Kamo shrines|Kamo Shrines]], [[:en:Kamigamo Shrine|Kamigamo Shrine]] (Upper Kamo Shrine) and [[:en:Shimogamo Shrine|Shimogamo Shrine]] (Lower Kamo Shrine), located in Kyoto. Many shrines are also historically tied to the ancient [[:en:Kamo clan|Kamo-no-agatanushi]] clan (賀茂県主氏).
There are multiple spellings so this article will cover all of them

== Kamo Shrine ([[:ja:鴨神社|鴨神社]]) ==
'''Kamo Shrine''' (鴨神社) is a Shinto shrine whose name includes the character "Kamo" (鴨).

to [[:en:Kamigamo Shrine|Kamigamo Shrine]] and [[:en:Shimogamo Shrine|Shimogamo Shrine]]. * {{ill|Kamo Shrine (Tamaki)|ja|Kamo Shrine (Ise)|lt=Kamo Shrine (Ise)|lt=鴨神社 (玉城町)|lt=Kamo {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q11398885]] Wikidata name: (none) [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 07:19, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q11398885]] Wikidata name: (none) [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 03:59, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} '''Kamo Shrine''' (加茂神社・賀茂神社) refers to Shinto shrines with the name "Kamo."
Most of these shrines were branches of the famous [[:en:Kamo shrines|Kamo Shrines]], [[:en:Kamigamo Shrine|Kamigamo Shrine]] (Upper Kamo Shrine) and [[:en:Shimogamo Shrine|Shimogamo Shrine]] (Lower Kamo Shrine), located in Kyoto. Many shrines are also historically tied to the ancient [[:en:Kamo clan|Kamo-no-agatanushi]] clan (賀茂県主氏).
There are multiple spellings so this article will cover all of them

== Kamo Shrine ([[:ja:鴨神社|鴨神社]]) ==
'''Kamo Shrine''' (鴨神社) is a Shinto shrine whose name includes the character "Kamo" (鴨).
* [[鴨神社_(玉城町)]] * [[:en:Kamo shrines|Kamo Shrine (Kamo-sha)]] ([[:en:Kyoto Prefecture|Kyoto]], [[:en:Kyoto|Kyoto City]]) - Another name for [[:en:Kamo shrines|Kamo Shrines]], which collectively refers to [[:en:Kamigamo Shrine|Kamigamo Shrine]] and [[:en:Shimogamo Shrine|Shimogamo Shrine]]. * {{ill|Kamo Shrine (Tamaki)|ja|Kamo Shrine (Ise)|lt=Kamo Shrine (Ise)|lt=鴨神社 (玉城町)|lt=Kamo {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q11398885]] Wikidata name: (none) [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 07:19, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q11398885]] Wikidata name: (none) [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 03:59, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} '''Kamo Shrine''' (加茂神社・賀茂神社) refers to Shinto shrines with the name "Kamo."
Most of these shrines were branches of the famous [[:en:Kamo shrines|Kamo Shrines]], [[:en:Kamigamo Shrine|Kamigamo Shrine]] (Upper Kamo Shrine) and [[:en:Shimogamo Shrine|Shimogamo Shrine]] (Lower Kamo Shrine), located in Kyoto. Many shrines are also historically tied to the ancient [[:en:Kamo clan|Kamo-no-agatanushi]] clan (賀茂県主氏).
There are multiple spellings so this article will cover all of them

== Kamo Shrine ([[:ja:鴨神社|鴨神社]]) ==
'''Kamo Shrine''' (鴨神社) is a Shinto shrine whose name includes the character "Kamo" (鴨).

{{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q11398885]] Wikidata name: (none) [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 07:19, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q11398885]] Wikidata name: (none) [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 03:59, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} '''Kamo Shrine''' (加茂神社・賀茂神社) refers to Shinto shrines with the name "Kamo."
Most of these shrines were branches of the famous [[:en:Kamo shrines|Kamo Shrines]], [[:en:Kamigamo Shrine|Kamigamo Shrine]] (Upper Kamo Shrine) and [[:en:Shimogamo Shrine|Shimogamo Shrine]] (Lower Kamo Shrine), located in Kyoto. Many shrines are also historically tied to the ancient [[:en:Kamo clan|Kamo-no-agatanushi]] clan (賀茂県主氏).
There are multiple spellings so this article will cover all of them

<!--for some reason this has an independent Japanese article [[:ja:鴨神社]]--><!--note do not remove this category during translation--><!--Kamo Shrine (disambiguation)--><!--Kamo Shrine (disambiguation)--> [[Category:Kamo Shrines]] [[Category:Shrine disambiguations]] [[Category:Disambiguation pages]] <!--jawiki derived wikidata interwikis--> [[Category:translated pages with valid en interwikis]] [[Category:pages without english wikidata name]] [[Category:pages without english wikidata name]]

{{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q11398885]] Wikidata name: (none) [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 07:19, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q11398885]] Wikidata name: (none) [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 03:59, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} '''Kamo Shrine''' (加茂神社・賀茂神社) refers to Shinto shrines with the name "Kamo."
Most of these shrines were branches of the famous [[:en:Kamo shrines|Kamo Shrines]], [[:en:Kamigamo Shrine|Kamigamo Shrine]] (Upper Kamo Shrine) and [[:en:Shimogamo Shrine|Shimogamo Shrine]] (Lower Kamo Shrine), located in Kyoto. Many shrines are also historically tied to the ancient [[:en:Kamo clan|Kamo-no-agatanushi]] clan (賀茂県主氏).
There are multiple spellings so this article will cover all of them

== Kamo Shrine ([[:ja:鴨神社|鴨神社]]) ==

<!--for some reason this has an independent Japanese article [[:ja:鴨神社]]--><!--note do not remove this category during translation--><!--Kamo Shrine (disambiguation)--><!--Kamo Shrine (disambiguation)--> [[Category:Kamo Shrines]] [[Category:Shrine disambiguations]] [[Category:Disambiguation pages]] <!--jawiki derived wikidata interwikis--> [[Category:translated pages with valid en interwikis]] [[Category:pages without english wikidata name]] [[Category:pages without english wikidata name]]

{{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q11398885]] Wikidata name: (none) [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 07:19, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q11398885]] Wikidata name: (none) [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 03:59, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} '''Kamo Shrine''' (加茂神社・賀茂神社) refers to Shinto shrines with the name "Kamo."
Most of these shrines were branches of the famous [[:en:Kamo shrines|Kamo Shrines]], [[:en:Kamigamo Shrine|Kamigamo Shrine]] (Upper Kamo Shrine) and [[:en:Shimogamo Shrine|Shimogamo Shrine]] (Lower Kamo Shrine), located in Kyoto. Many shrines are also historically tied to the ancient [[:en:Kamo clan|Kamo-no-agatanushi]] clan (賀茂県主氏).
There are multiple spellings so this article will cover all of them

== Kamo Shrine ([[:ja:鴨神社|鴨神社]]) ==
'''Kamo Shrine''' (鴨神社) is a Shinto shrine whose name includes the character "Kamo" (鴨).
* [[鴨神社_(玉城町)]] * [[:en:Kamo shrines|Kamo Shrine (Kamo-sha)]] ([[:en:Kyoto Prefecture|Kyoto]], [[:en:Kyoto|Kyoto City]]) - Another name for [[:en:Kamo shrines|Kamo Shrines]], which collectively refers to [[:en:Kamigamo Shrine|Kamigamo Shrine]] and [[:en:Shimogamo Shrine|Shimogamo Shrine]]. * {{ill|Kamo Shrine (Tamaki)|ja|Kamo Shrine (Ise)|lt=Kamo Shrine (Ise)|lt=鴨神社 (玉城町)|lt=Kamo {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q11398885]] Wikidata name: (none) [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 07:19, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q11398885]] Wikidata name: (none) [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 03:59, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} '''Kamo Shrine''' (加茂神社・賀茂神社) refers to Shinto shrines with the name "Kamo."
Most of these shrines were branches of the famous [[:en:Kamo shrines|Kamo Shrines]], [[:en:Kamigamo Shrine|Kamigamo Shrine]] (Upper Kamo Shrine) and [[:en:Shimogamo Shrine|Shimogamo Shrine]] (Lower Kamo Shrine), located in Kyoto. Many shrines are also historically tied to the ancient [[:en:Kamo clan|Kamo-no-agatanushi]] clan (賀茂県主氏).
There are multiple spellings so this article will cover all of them

'''Kamo Shrine''' (鴨神社) is a Shinto shrine whose name includes the character "Kamo" (鴨).

to [[:en:Kamigamo Shrine|Kamigamo Shrine]] and [[:en:Shimogamo Shrine|Shimogamo Shrine]]. * {{ill|Kamo Shrine (Tamaki)|ja|Kamo Shrine (Ise)|lt=Kamo Shrine (Ise)|lt=鴨神社 (玉城町)|lt=Kamo <!--for some reason this has an independent Japanese article [[:ja:鴨神社]]--><!--note do not remove this category during translation--><!--Kamo Shrine (disambiguation)--><!--Kamo Shrine (disambiguation)--> [[Category:Kamo Shrines]] [[Category:Shrine disambiguations]] [[Category:Disambiguation pages]] <!--jawiki derived wikidata interwikis--> [[Category:translated pages with valid en interwikis]] [[Category:pages without english wikidata name]] [[Category:pages without english wikidata name]]

{{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q11398885]] Wikidata name: (none) [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 07:19, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q11398885]] Wikidata name: (none) [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 03:59, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} '''Kamo Shrine''' (加茂神社・賀茂神社) refers to Shinto shrines with the name "Kamo."
Most of these shrines were branches of the famous [[:en:Kamo shrines|Kamo Shrines]], [[:en:Kamigamo Shrine|Kamigamo Shrine]] (Upper Kamo Shrine) and [[:en:Shimogamo Shrine|Shimogamo Shrine]] (Lower Kamo Shrine), located in Kyoto. Many shrines are also historically tied to the ancient [[:en:Kamo clan|Kamo-no-agatanushi]] clan (賀茂県主氏).
There are multiple spellings so this article will cover all of them

== Kamo Shrine ([[:ja:鴨神社|鴨神社]]) ==
'''Kamo Shrine''' (鴨神社) is a Shinto shrine whose name includes the character "Kamo" (鴨).

<!--for some reason this has an independent Japanese article [[:ja:鴨神社]]--><!--note do not remove this category during translation--><!--Kamo Shrine (disambiguation)--><!--Kamo Shrine (disambiguation)--> [[Category:Kamo Shrines]] [[Category:Shrine disambiguations]] [[Category:Disambiguation pages]] <!--jawiki derived wikidata interwikis--> [[Category:translated pages with valid en interwikis]] [[Category:pages without english wikidata name]] [[Category:pages without english wikidata name]]

{{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q11398885]] Wikidata name: (none) [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 07:19, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q11398885]] Wikidata name: (none) [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 03:59, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} '''Kamo Shrine''' (加茂神社・賀茂神社) refers to Shinto shrines with the name "Kamo."
Most of these shrines were branches of the famous [[:en:Kamo shrines|Kamo Shrines]], [[:en:Kamigamo Shrine|Kamigamo Shrine]] (Upper Kamo Shrine) and [[:en:Shimogamo Shrine|Shimogamo Shrine]] (Lower Kamo Shrine), located in Kyoto. Many shrines are also historically tied to the ancient [[:en:Kamo clan|Kamo-no-agatanushi]] clan (賀茂県主氏).
There are multiple spellings so this article will cover all of them

== Kamo Shrine ([[:ja:鴨神社|鴨神社]]) ==
'''Kamo Shrine''' (鴨神社) is a Shinto shrine whose name includes the character "Kamo" (鴨).

to [[:en:Kamigamo Shrine|Kamigamo Shrine]] and [[:en:Shimogamo Shrine|Shimogamo Shrine]]. * {{ill|Kamo Shrine (Tamaki)|ja|Kamo Shrine (Ise)|lt=Kamo Shrine (Ise)|lt=鴨神社 (玉城町)|lt=Kamo {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q11398885]] Wikidata name: (none) [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 07:19, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q11398885]] Wikidata name: (none) [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 03:59, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} '''Kamo Shrine''' (加茂神社・賀茂神社) refers to Shinto shrines with the name "Kamo."
Most of these shrines were branches of the famous [[:en:Kamo shrines|Kamo Shrines]], [[:en:Kamigamo Shrine|Kamigamo Shrine]] (Upper Kamo Shrine) and [[:en:Shimogamo Shrine|Shimogamo Shrine]] (Lower Kamo Shrine), located in Kyoto. Many shrines are also historically tied to the ancient [[:en:Kamo clan|Kamo-no-agatanushi]] clan (賀茂県主氏).
There are multiple spellings so this article will cover all of them

== Kamo Shrine ([[:ja:鴨神社|鴨神社]]) ==
'''Kamo Shrine''' (鴨神社) is a Shinto shrine whose name includes the character "Kamo" (鴨).

to [[:en:Kamigamo Shrine|Kamigamo Shrine]] and [[:en:Shimogamo Shrine|Shimogamo Shrine]]. * {{ill|Kamo Shrine (Tamaki)|ja|Kamo Shrine (Ise)|lt=Kamo Shrine (Ise)|lt=鴨神社 (玉城町)|lt=Kamo {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q11398885]] Wikidata name: (none) [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 07:19, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q11398885]] Wikidata name: (none) [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 03:59, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} '''Kamo Shrine''' (加茂神社・賀茂神社) refers to Shinto shrines with the name "Kamo."
Most of these shrines were branches of the famous [[:en:Kamo shrines|Kamo Shrines]], [[:en:Kamigamo Shrine|Kamigamo Shrine]] (Upper Kamo Shrine) and [[:en:Shimogamo Shrine|Shimogamo Shrine]] (Lower Kamo Shrine), located in Kyoto. Many shrines are also historically tied to the ancient [[:en:Kamo clan|Kamo-no-agatanushi]] clan (賀茂県主氏).
There are multiple spellings so this article will cover all of them

== Kamo Shrine ([[:ja:鴨神社|鴨神社]]) ==
'''Kamo Shrine''' (鴨神社) is a Shinto shrine whose name includes the character "Kamo" (鴨).

{{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q11398885]] Wikidata name: (none) [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 07:19, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q11398885]] Wikidata name: (none) [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 03:59, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} '''Kamo Shrine''' (加茂神社・賀茂神社) refers to Shinto shrines with the name "Kamo."
Most of these shrines were branches of the famous [[:en:Kamo shrines|Kamo Shrines]], [[:en:Kamigamo Shrine|Kamigamo Shrine]] (Upper Kamo Shrine) and [[:en:Shimogamo Shrine|Shimogamo Shrine]] (Lower Kamo Shrine), located in Kyoto. Many shrines are also historically tied to the ancient [[:en:Kamo clan|Kamo-no-agatanushi]] clan (賀茂県主氏).
There are multiple spellings so this article will cover all of them

== Kamo Shrine ([[:ja:鴨神社|鴨神社]]) ==
'''Kamo Shrine''' (鴨神社) is a Shinto shrine whose name includes the character "Kamo" (鴨).
* [[鴨神社_(玉城町)]] * [[:en:Kamo shrines|Kamo Shrine (Kamo-sha)]] ([[:en:Kyoto Prefecture|Kyoto]], [[:en:Kyoto|Kyoto City]]) - Another name for [[:en:Kamo shrines|Kamo Shrines]], which collectively refers to [[:en:Kamigamo Shrine|Kamigamo Shrine]] and [[:en:Shimogamo Shrine|Shimogamo Shrine]]. * {{ill|Kamo Shrine (Tamaki)|ja|Kamo Shrine (Ise)|lt=Kamo Shrine (Ise)|lt=鴨神社 (玉城町)|lt=Kamo {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q11398885]] Wikidata name: (none) [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 07:19, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q11398885]] Wikidata name: (none) [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 03:59, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} '''Kamo Shrine''' (加茂神社・賀茂神社) refers to Shinto shrines with the name "Kamo."
Most of these shrines were branches of the famous [[:en:Kamo shrines|Kamo Shrines]], [[:en:Kamigamo Shrine|Kamigamo Shrine]] (Upper Kamo Shrine) and [[:en:Shimogamo Shrine|Shimogamo Shrine]] (Lower Kamo Shrine), located in Kyoto. Many shrines are also historically tied to the ancient [[:en:Kamo clan|Kamo-no-agatanushi]] clan (賀茂県主氏).
There are multiple spellings so this article will cover all of them

== Kamo Shrine ([[:ja:鴨神社|鴨神社]]) ==
'''Kamo Shrine''' (鴨神社) is a Shinto shrine whose name includes the character "Kamo" (鴨).
* [[鴨神社_(玉城町)]] * [[:en:Kamo shrines|Kamo Shrine (Kamo-sha)]] ([[:en:Kyoto Prefecture|Kyoto]], [[:en:Kyoto|Kyoto City]]) - Another name for [[:en:Kamo shrines|Kamo Shrines]], which collectively refers to [[:en:Kamigamo Shrine|Kamigamo Shrine]] and [[:en:Shimogamo Shrine|Shimogamo Shrine]]. * {{ill|Kamo Shrine (Tamaki)|ja|Kamo Shrine (Ise)|lt=Kamo Shrine (Ise)|lt=鴨神社 (玉城町)|lt=Kamo {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q11398885]] Wikidata name: (none) [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 07:19, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q11398885]] Wikidata name: (none) [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 03:59, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} '''Kamo Shrine''' (加茂神社・賀茂神社) refers to Shinto shrines with the name "Kamo."
Most of these shrines were branches of the famous [[:en:Kamo shrines|Kamo Shrines]], [[:en:Kamigamo Shrine|Kamigamo Shrine]] (Upper Kamo Shrine) and [[:en:Shimogamo Shrine|Shimogamo Shrine]] (Lower Kamo Shrine), located in Kyoto. Many shrines are also historically tied to the ancient [[:en:Kamo clan|Kamo-no-agatanushi]] clan (賀茂県主氏).
There are multiple spellings so this article will cover all of them

== Kamo Shrine ([[:ja:鴨神社|鴨神社]]) ==
'''Kamo Shrine''' (鴨神社) is a Shinto shrine whose name includes the character "Kamo" (鴨).

{{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q11398885]] Wikidata name: (none) [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 07:19, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q11398885]] Wikidata name: (none) [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 03:59, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} '''Kamo Shrine''' (加茂神社・賀茂神社) refers to Shinto shrines with the name "Kamo."
Most of these shrines were branches of the famous [[:en:Kamo shrines|Kamo Shrines]], [[:en:Kamigamo Shrine|Kamigamo Shrine]] (Upper Kamo Shrine) and [[:en:Shimogamo Shrine|Shimogamo Shrine]] (Lower Kamo Shrine), located in Kyoto. Many shrines are also historically tied to the ancient [[:en:Kamo clan|Kamo-no-agatanushi]] clan (賀茂県主氏).
There are multiple spellings so this article will cover all of them

== Kamo Shrine ([[:ja:鴨神社|鴨神社]]) ==

{{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q11398885]] Wikidata name: (none) [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 07:19, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q11398885]] Wikidata name: (none) [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 03:59, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} '''Kamo Shrine''' (加茂神社・賀茂神社) refers to Shinto shrines with the name "Kamo."
Most of these shrines were branches of the famous [[:en:Kamo shrines|Kamo Shrines]], [[:en:Kamigamo Shrine|Kamigamo Shrine]] (Upper Kamo Shrine) and [[:en:Shimogamo Shrine|Shimogamo Shrine]] (Lower Kamo Shrine), located in Kyoto. Many shrines are also historically tied to the ancient [[:en:Kamo clan|Kamo-no-agatanushi]] clan (賀茂県主氏).
There are multiple spellings so this article will cover all of them

== Kamo Shrine ([[:ja:鴨神社|鴨神社]]) ==
'''Kamo Shrine''' (鴨神社) is a Shinto shrine whose name includes the character "Kamo" (鴨).

to [[:en:Kamigamo Shrine|Kamigamo Shrine]] and [[:en:Shimogamo Shrine|Shimogamo Shrine]]. * {{ill|Kamo Shrine (Tamaki)|ja|Kamo Shrine (Ise)|lt=Kamo Shrine (Ise)|lt=鴨神社 (玉城町)|lt=Kamo {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q11398885]] Wikidata name: (none) [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 07:19, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q11398885]] Wikidata name: (none) [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 03:59, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} '''Kamo Shrine''' (加茂神社・賀茂神社) refers to Shinto shrines with the name "Kamo."
Most of these shrines were branches of the famous [[:en:Kamo shrines|Kamo Shrines]], [[:en:Kamigamo Shrine|Kamigamo Shrine]] (Upper Kamo Shrine) and [[:en:Shimogamo Shrine|Shimogamo Shrine]] (Lower Kamo Shrine), located in Kyoto. Many shrines are also historically tied to the ancient [[:en:Kamo clan|Kamo-no-agatanushi]] clan (賀茂県主氏).
There are multiple spellings so this article will cover all of them

'''Kamo Shrine''' (鴨神社) is a Shinto shrine whose name includes the character "Kamo" (鴨).

to [[:en:Kamigamo Shrine|Kamigamo Shrine]] and [[:en:Shimogamo Shrine|Shimogamo Shrine]]. * {{ill|Kamo Shrine (Tamaki)|ja|Kamo Shrine (Ise)|lt=Kamo Shrine (Ise)|lt=鴨神社 (玉城町)|lt=Kamo {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q11398885]] Wikidata name: (none) [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 07:19, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q11398885]] Wikidata name: (none) [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 03:59, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} '''Kamo Shrine''' (加茂神社・賀茂神社) refers to Shinto shrines with the name "Kamo."
Most of these shrines were branches of the famous [[:en:Kamo shrines|Kamo Shrines]], [[:en:Kamigamo Shrine|Kamigamo Shrine]] (Upper Kamo Shrine) and [[:en:Shimogamo Shrine|Shimogamo Shrine]] (Lower Kamo Shrine), located in Kyoto. Many shrines are also historically tied to the ancient [[:en:Kamo clan|Kamo-no-agatanushi]] clan (賀茂県主氏).
There are multiple spellings so this article will cover all of them

== Kamo Shrine ([[:ja:鴨神社|鴨神社]]) ==
'''Kamo Shrine''' (鴨神社) is a Shinto shrine whose name includes the character "Kamo" (鴨).

{{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q11398885]] Wikidata name: (none) [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 07:19, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q11398885]] Wikidata name: (none) [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 03:59, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} '''Kamo Shrine''' (加茂神社・賀茂神社) refers to Shinto shrines with the name "Kamo."
Most of these shrines were branches of the famous [[:en:Kamo shrines|Kamo Shrines]], [[:en:Kamigamo Shrine|Kamigamo Shrine]] (Upper Kamo Shrine) and [[:en:Shimogamo Shrine|Shimogamo Shrine]] (Lower Kamo Shrine), located in Kyoto. Many shrines are also historically tied to the ancient [[:en:Kamo clan|Kamo-no-agatanushi]] clan (賀茂県主氏).
There are multiple spellings so this article will cover all of them

== Kamo Shrine ([[:ja:鴨神社|鴨神社]]) ==
'''Kamo Shrine''' (鴨神社) is a Shinto shrine whose name includes the character "Kamo" (鴨).

to [[:en:Kamigamo Shrine|Kamigamo Shrine]] and [[:en:Shimogamo Shrine|Shimogamo Shrine]]. * {{ill|Kamo Shrine (Tamaki)|ja|Kamo Shrine (Ise)|lt=Kamo Shrine (Ise)|lt=鴨神社 (玉城町)|lt=Kamo {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q11398885]] Wikidata name: (none) [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 07:19, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q11398885]] Wikidata name: (none) [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 03:59, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} '''Kamo Shrine''' (加茂神社・賀茂神社) refers to Shinto shrines with the name "Kamo."
Most of these shrines were branches of the famous [[:en:Kamo shrines|Kamo Shrines]], [[:en:Kamigamo Shrine|Kamigamo Shrine]] (Upper Kamo Shrine) and [[:en:Shimogamo Shrine|Shimogamo Shrine]] (Lower Kamo Shrine), located in Kyoto. Many shrines are also historically tied to the ancient [[:en:Kamo clan|Kamo-no-agatanushi]] clan (賀茂県主氏).
There are multiple spellings so this article will cover all of them

== Kamo Shrine ([[:ja:鴨神社|鴨神社]]) ==
'''Kamo Shrine''' (鴨神社) is a Shinto shrine whose name includes the character "Kamo" (鴨).

{{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q11398885]] Wikidata name: (none) [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 07:19, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q11398885]] Wikidata name: (none) [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 03:59, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} '''Kamo Shrine''' (加茂神社・賀茂神社) refers to Shinto shrines with the name "Kamo."
Most of these shrines were branches of the famous [[:en:Kamo shrines|Kamo Shrines]], [[:en:Kamigamo Shrine|Kamigamo Shrine]] (Upper Kamo Shrine) and [[:en:Shimogamo Shrine|Shimogamo Shrine]] (Lower Kamo Shrine), located in Kyoto. Many shrines are also historically tied to the ancient [[:en:Kamo clan|Kamo-no-agatanushi]] clan (賀茂県主氏).
There are multiple spellings so this article will cover all of them

== Kamo Shrine ([[:ja:鴨神社|鴨神社]]) ==
'''Kamo Shrine''' (鴨神社) is a Shinto shrine whose name includes the character "Kamo" (鴨).

<!--for some reason this has an independent Japanese article [[:ja:鴨神社]]--><!--note do not remove this category during translation--><!--Kamo Shrine (disambiguation)--><!--Kamo Shrine (disambiguation)--> [[Category:Kamo Shrines]] [[Category:Shrine disambiguations]] [[Category:Disambiguation pages]] <!--jawiki derived wikidata interwikis--> [[Category:translated pages with valid en interwikis]] [[Category:pages without english wikidata name]] [[Category:pages without english wikidata name]]

{{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q11398885]] Wikidata name: (none) [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 07:19, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q11398885]] Wikidata name: (none) [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 03:59, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} '''Kamo Shrine''' (加茂神社・賀茂神社) refers to Shinto shrines with the name "Kamo."
Most of these shrines were branches of the famous [[:en:Kamo shrines|Kamo Shrines]], [[:en:Kamigamo Shrine|Kamigamo Shrine]] (Upper Kamo Shrine) and [[:en:Shimogamo Shrine|Shimogamo Shrine]] (Lower Kamo Shrine), located in Kyoto. Many shrines are also historically tied to the ancient [[:en:Kamo clan|Kamo-no-agatanushi]] clan (賀茂県主氏).
There are multiple spellings so this article will cover all of them

== Kamo Shrine ([[:ja:鴨神社|鴨神社]]) ==
'''Kamo Shrine''' (鴨神社) is a Shinto shrine whose name includes the character "Kamo" (鴨).
* [[鴨神社_(玉城町)]] * [[:en:Kamo shrines|Kamo Shrine (Kamo-sha)]] ([[:en:Kyoto Prefecture|Kyoto]], [[:en:Kyoto|Kyoto City]]) - Another name for [[:en:Kamo shrines|Kamo Shrines]], which collectively refers to [[:en:Kamigamo Shrine|Kamigamo Shrine]] and [[:en:Shimogamo Shrine|Shimogamo Shrine]]. * {{ill|Kamo Shrine (Tamaki)|ja|Kamo Shrine (Ise)|lt=Kamo Shrine (Ise)|lt=鴨神社 (玉城町)|lt=Kamo {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q11398885]] Wikidata name: (none) [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 07:19, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q11398885]] Wikidata name: (none) [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 03:59, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} '''Kamo Shrine''' (加茂神社・賀茂神社) refers to Shinto shrines with the name "Kamo."
Most of these shrines were branches of the famous [[:en:Kamo shrines|Kamo Shrines]], [[:en:Kamigamo Shrine|Kamigamo Shrine]] (Upper Kamo Shrine) and [[:en:Shimogamo Shrine|Shimogamo Shrine]] (Lower Kamo Shrine), located in Kyoto. Many shrines are also historically tied to the ancient [[:en:Kamo clan|Kamo-no-agatanushi]] clan (賀茂県主氏).
There are multiple spellings so this article will cover all of them

== Kamo Shrine ([[:ja:鴨神社|鴨神社]]) ==
'''Kamo Shrine''' (鴨神社) is a Shinto shrine whose name includes the character "Kamo" (鴨).

<!--for some reason this has an independent Japanese article [[:ja:鴨神社]]--><!--note do not remove this category during translation--><!--Kamo Shrine (disambiguation)--><!--Kamo Shrine (disambiguation)--> [[Category:Kamo Shrines]] [[Category:Shrine disambiguations]] [[Category:Disambiguation pages]] <!--jawiki derived wikidata interwikis--> [[Category:translated pages with valid en interwikis]] [[Category:pages without english wikidata name]] [[Category:pages without english wikidata name]]

{{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q11398885]] Wikidata name: (none) [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 07:19, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q11398885]] Wikidata name: (none) [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 03:59, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} '''Kamo Shrine''' (加茂神社・賀茂神社) refers to Shinto shrines with the name "Kamo."
Most of these shrines were branches of the famous [[:en:Kamo shrines|Kamo Shrines]], [[:en:Kamigamo Shrine|Kamigamo Shrine]] (Upper Kamo Shrine) and [[:en:Shimogamo Shrine|Shimogamo Shrine]] (Lower Kamo Shrine), located in Kyoto. Many shrines are also historically tied to the ancient [[:en:Kamo clan|Kamo-no-agatanushi]] clan (賀茂県主氏).
There are multiple spellings so this article will cover all of them

== Kamo Shrine ([[:ja:鴨神社|鴨神社]]) ==

{{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q11398885]] Wikidata name: (none) [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 07:19, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q11398885]] Wikidata name: (none) [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 03:59, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} '''Kamo Shrine''' (加茂神社・賀茂神社) refers to Shinto shrines with the name "Kamo."
Most of these shrines were branches of the famous [[:en:Kamo shrines|Kamo Shrines]], [[:en:Kamigamo Shrine|Kamigamo Shrine]] (Upper Kamo Shrine) and [[:en:Shimogamo Shrine|Shimogamo Shrine]] (Lower Kamo Shrine), located in Kyoto. Many shrines are also historically tied to the ancient [[:en:Kamo clan|Kamo-no-agatanushi]] clan (賀茂県主氏).
There are multiple spellings so this article will cover all of them

== Kamo Shrine ([[:ja:鴨神社|鴨神社]]) ==
'''Kamo Shrine''' (鴨神社) is a Shinto shrine whose name includes the character "Kamo" (鴨).
* [[鴨神社_(玉城町)]] * [[:en:Kamo shrines|Kamo Shrine (Kamo-sha)]] ([[:en:Kyoto Prefecture|Kyoto]], [[:en:Kyoto|Kyoto City]]) - Another name for [[:en:Kamo shrines|Kamo Shrines]], which collectively refers {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q11398885]] Wikidata name: (none) [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 07:19, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q11398885]] Wikidata name: (none) [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 03:59, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} '''Kamo Shrine''' (加茂神社・賀茂神社) refers to Shinto shrines with the name "Kamo."
Most of these shrines were branches of the famous [[:en:Kamo shrines|Kamo Shrines]], [[:en:Kamigamo Shrine|Kamigamo Shrine]] (Upper Kamo Shrine) and [[:en:Shimogamo Shrine|Shimogamo Shrine]] (Lower Kamo Shrine), located in Kyoto. Many shrines are also historically tied to the ancient [[:en:Kamo clan|Kamo-no-agatanushi]] clan (賀茂県主氏).
There are multiple spellings so this article will cover all of them

== Kamo Shrine ([[:ja:鴨神社|鴨神社]]) ==
'''Kamo Shrine''' (鴨神社) is a Shinto shrine whose name includes the character "Kamo" (鴨).

{{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q11398885]] Wikidata name: (none) [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 07:19, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q11398885]] Wikidata name: (none) [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 03:59, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} '''Kamo Shrine''' (加茂神社・賀茂神社) refers to Shinto shrines with the name "Kamo."
Most of these shrines were branches of the famous [[:en:Kamo shrines|Kamo Shrines]], [[:en:Kamigamo Shrine|Kamigamo Shrine]] (Upper Kamo Shrine) and [[:en:Shimogamo Shrine|Shimogamo Shrine]] (Lower Kamo Shrine), located in Kyoto. Many shrines are also historically tied to the ancient [[:en:Kamo clan|Kamo-no-agatanushi]] clan (賀茂県主氏).
There are multiple spellings so this article will cover all of them

== Kamo Shrine ([[:ja:鴨神社|鴨神社]]) ==
'''Kamo Shrine''' (鴨神社) is a Shinto shrine whose name includes the character "Kamo" (鴨).
* [[鴨神社_(玉城町)]] * [[:en:Kamo shrines|Kamo Shrine (Kamo-sha)]] ([[:en:Kyoto Prefecture|Kyoto]], [[:en:Kyoto|Kyoto City]]) - Another name for [[:en:Kamo shrines|Kamo Shrines]], which collectively refers to [[:en:Kamigamo Shrine|Kamigamo Shrine]] and [[:en:Shimogamo Shrine|Shimogamo Shrine]]. * {{ill|Kamo Shrine (Tamaki)|ja|Kamo Shrine (Ise)|lt=Kamo Shrine (Ise)|lt=鴨神社 (玉城町)|lt=Kamo {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q11398885]] Wikidata name: (none) [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 07:19, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q11398885]] Wikidata name: (none) [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 03:59, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} '''Kamo Shrine''' (加茂神社・賀茂神社) refers to Shinto shrines with the name "Kamo."
Most of these shrines were branches of the famous [[:en:Kamo shrines|Kamo Shrines]], [[:en:Kamigamo Shrine|Kamigamo Shrine]] (Upper Kamo Shrine) and [[:en:Shimogamo Shrine|Shimogamo Shrine]] (Lower Kamo Shrine), located in Kyoto. Many shrines are also historically tied to the ancient [[:en:Kamo clan|Kamo-no-agatanushi]] clan (賀茂県主氏).
There are multiple spellings so this article will cover all of them

== Kamo Shrine ([[:ja:鴨神社|鴨神社]]) ==
'''Kamo Shrine''' (鴨神社) is a Shinto shrine whose name includes the character "Kamo" (鴨).

to [[:en:Kamigamo Shrine|Kamigamo Shrine]] and [[:en:Shimogamo Shrine|Shimogamo Shrine]]. * {{ill|Kamo Shrine (Tamaki)|ja|Kamo Shrine (Ise)|lt=Kamo Shrine (Ise)|lt=鴨神社 (玉城町)|lt=Kamo {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q11398885]] Wikidata name: (none) [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 07:19, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q11398885]] Wikidata name: (none) [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 03:59, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} '''Kamo Shrine''' (加茂神社・賀茂神社) refers to Shinto shrines with the name "Kamo."
Most of these shrines were branches of the famous [[:en:Kamo shrines|Kamo Shrines]], [[:en:Kamigamo Shrine|Kamigamo Shrine]] (Upper Kamo Shrine) and [[:en:Shimogamo Shrine|Shimogamo Shrine]] (Lower Kamo Shrine), located in Kyoto. Many shrines are also historically tied to the ancient [[:en:Kamo clan|Kamo-no-agatanushi]] clan (賀茂県主氏).
There are multiple spellings so this article will cover all of them

== Kamo Shrine ([[:ja:鴨神社|鴨神社]]) ==
'''Kamo Shrine''' (鴨神社) is a Shinto shrine whose name includes the character "Kamo" (鴨).

{{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q11398885]] Wikidata name: (none) [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 07:19, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q11398885]] Wikidata name: (none) [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 03:59, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} '''Kamo Shrine''' (加茂神社・賀茂神社) refers to Shinto shrines with the name "Kamo."
Most of these shrines were branches of the famous [[:en:Kamo shrines|Kamo Shrines]], [[:en:Kamigamo Shrine|Kamigamo Shrine]] (Upper Kamo Shrine) and [[:en:Shimogamo Shrine|Shimogamo Shrine]] (Lower Kamo Shrine), located in Kyoto. Many shrines are also historically tied to the ancient [[:en:Kamo clan|Kamo-no-agatanushi]] clan (賀茂県主氏).
There are multiple spellings so this article will cover all of them

== Kamo Shrine ([[:ja:鴨神社|鴨神社]]) ==
'''Kamo Shrine''' (鴨神社) is a Shinto shrine whose name includes the character "Kamo" (鴨).

{{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q11398885]] Wikidata name: (none) [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 07:19, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q11398885]] Wikidata name: (none) [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 03:59, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} '''Kamo Shrine''' (加茂神社・賀茂神社) refers to Shinto shrines with the name "Kamo."
Most of these shrines were branches of the famous [[:en:Kamo shrines|Kamo Shrines]], [[:en:Kamigamo Shrine|Kamigamo Shrine]] (Upper Kamo Shrine) and [[:en:Shimogamo Shrine|Shimogamo Shrine]] (Lower Kamo Shrine), located in Kyoto. Many shrines are also historically tied to the ancient [[:en:Kamo clan|Kamo-no-agatanushi]] clan (賀茂県主氏).
There are multiple spellings so this article will cover all of them

== Kamo Shrine ([[:ja:鴨神社|鴨神社]]) ==
'''Kamo Shrine''' (鴨神社) is a Shinto shrine whose name includes the character "Kamo" (鴨).
* [[鴨神社_(玉城町)]] * [[:en:Kamo shrines|Kamo Shrine (Kamo-sha)]] ([[:en:Kyoto Prefecture|Kyoto]], [[:en:Kyoto|Kyoto City]]) - Another name for [[:en:Kamo shrines|Kamo Shrines]], which collectively refers to [[:en:Kamigamo Shrine|Kamigamo Shrine]] and [[:en:Shimogamo Shrine|Shimogamo Shrine]]. * {{ill|Kamo Shrine (Tamaki)|ja|Kamo Shrine (Ise)|lt=Kamo Shrine (Ise)|lt=鴨神社 (玉城町)|lt=Kamo {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q11398885]] Wikidata name: (none) [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 07:19, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q11398885]] Wikidata name: (none) [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 03:59, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} '''Kamo Shrine''' (加茂神社・賀茂神社) refers to Shinto shrines with the name "Kamo."
Most of these shrines were branches of the famous [[:en:Kamo shrines|Kamo Shrines]], [[:en:Kamigamo Shrine|Kamigamo Shrine]] (Upper Kamo Shrine) and [[:en:Shimogamo Shrine|Shimogamo Shrine]] (Lower Kamo Shrine), located in Kyoto. Many shrines are also historically tied to the ancient [[:en:Kamo clan|Kamo-no-agatanushi]] clan (賀茂県主氏).
There are multiple spellings so this article will cover all of them

== Kamo Shrine ([[:ja:鴨神社|鴨神社]]) ==
'''Kamo Shrine''' (鴨神社) is a Shinto shrine whose name includes the character "Kamo" (鴨).

{{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q11398885]] Wikidata name: (none) [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 07:19, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q11398885]] Wikidata name: (none) [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 03:59, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} '''Kamo Shrine''' (加茂神社・賀茂神社) refers to Shinto shrines with the name "Kamo."
Most of these shrines were branches of the famous [[:en:Kamo shrines|Kamo Shrines]], [[:en:Kamigamo Shrine|Kamigamo Shrine]] (Upper Kamo Shrine) and [[:en:Shimogamo Shrine|Shimogamo Shrine]] (Lower Kamo Shrine), located in Kyoto. Many shrines are also historically tied to the ancient [[:en:Kamo clan|Kamo-no-agatanushi]] clan (賀茂県主氏).
There are multiple spellings so this article will cover all of them

== Kamo Shrine ([[:ja:鴨神社|鴨神社]]) ==
'''Kamo Shrine''' (鴨神社) is a Shinto shrine whose name includes the character "Kamo" (鴨).

{{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q11398885]] Wikidata name: (none) [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 07:19, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q11398885]] Wikidata name: (none) [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 03:59, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} '''Kamo Shrine''' (加茂神社・賀茂神社) refers to Shinto shrines with the name "Kamo."
Most of these shrines were branches of the famous [[:en:Kamo shrines|Kamo Shrines]], [[:en:Kamigamo Shrine|Kamigamo Shrine]] (Upper Kamo Shrine) and [[:en:Shimogamo Shrine|Shimogamo Shrine]] (Lower Kamo Shrine), located in Kyoto. Many shrines are also historically tied to the ancient [[:en:Kamo clan|Kamo-no-agatanushi]] clan (賀茂県主氏).
There are multiple spellings so this article will cover all of them

== Kamo Shrine ([[:ja:鴨神社|鴨神社]]) ==
'''Kamo Shrine''' (鴨神社) is a Shinto shrine whose name includes the character "Kamo" (鴨).

{{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q11398885]] Wikidata name: (none) [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 07:19, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q11398885]] Wikidata name: (none) [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 03:59, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} '''Kamo Shrine''' (加茂神社・賀茂神社) refers to Shinto shrines with the name "Kamo."
Most of these shrines were branches of the famous [[:en:Kamo shrines|Kamo Shrines]], [[:en:Kamigamo Shrine|Kamigamo Shrine]] (Upper Kamo Shrine) and [[:en:Shimogamo Shrine|Shimogamo Shrine]] (Lower Kamo Shrine), located in Kyoto. Many shrines are also historically tied to the ancient [[:en:Kamo clan|Kamo-no-agatanushi]] clan (賀茂県主氏).
There are multiple spellings so this article will cover all of them

== Kamo Shrine ([[:ja:鴨神社|鴨神社]]) ==
'''Kamo Shrine''' (鴨神社) is a Shinto shrine whose name includes the character "Kamo" (鴨).
* [[鴨神社_(玉城町)]] * [[:en:Kamo shrines|Kamo Shrine (Kamo-sha)]] ([[:en:Kyoto Prefecture|Kyoto]], [[:en:Kyoto|Kyoto City]]) - Another name for [[:en:Kamo shrines|Kamo Shrines]], which collectively refers to [[:en:Kamigamo Shrine|Kamigamo Shrine]] and [[:en:Shimogamo Shrine|Shimogamo Shrine]]. * {{ill|Kamo Shrine (Tamaki)|ja|Kamo Shrine (Ise)|lt=Kamo Shrine (Ise)|lt=鴨神社 (玉城町)|lt=Kamo
