{{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q11398885]] Wikidata name: (none) [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 07:19, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q11398885]] Wikidata name: (none) [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 03:59, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} '''Kamo Shrine''' (加茂神社・賀茂神社) refers to Shinto shrines with the name "Kamo."
Most of these shrines were branches of the famous [[:en:Kamo shrines|Kamo Shrines]], [[:en:Kamigamo Shrine|Kamigamo Shrine]] (Upper Kamo Shrine) and [[:en:Shimogamo Shrine|Shimogamo Shrine]] (Lower Kamo Shrine), located in Kyoto. Many shrines are also historically tied to the ancient [[:en:Kamo clan|Kamo-no-agatanushi]] clan (賀茂県主氏).
There are multiple spellings so this article will cover all of them

== Kamo Shrine ([[:ja:鴨神社|鴨神社]]) ==
'''Kamo Shrine''' (鴨神社) is a Shinto shrine whose name includes the character "Kamo" (鴨).

to [[:en:Kamigamo Shrine|Kamigamo Shrine]] and [[:en:Shimogamo Shrine|Shimogamo Shrine]]. * {{ill|Kamo Shrine (Tamaki)|ja|Kamo Shrine (Ise)|lt=Kamo Shrine (Ise)|lt=鴨神社 (玉城町)|lt=Kamo {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q11398885]] Wikidata name: (none) [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 07:19, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q11398885]] Wikidata name: (none) [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 03:59, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} '''Kamo Shrine''' (加茂神社・賀茂神社) refers to Shinto shrines with the name "Kamo."
Most of these shrines were branches of the famous [[:en:Kamo shrines|Kamo Shrines]], [[:en:Kamigamo Shrine|Kamigamo Shrine]] (Upper Kamo Shrine) and [[:en:Shimogamo Shrine|Shimogamo Shrine]] (Lower Kamo Shrine), located in Kyoto. Many shrines are also historically tied to the ancient [[:en:Kamo clan|Kamo-no-agatanushi]] clan (賀茂県主氏).
There are multiple spellings so this article will cover all of them

== Kamo Shrine ([[:ja:鴨神社|鴨神社]]) ==
'''Kamo Shrine''' (鴨神社) is a Shinto shrine whose name includes the character "Kamo" (鴨).

to [[:en:Kamigamo Shrine|Kamigamo Shrine]] and [[:en:Shimogamo Shrine|Shimogamo Shrine]]. * {{ill|Kamo Shrine (Tamaki)|ja|Kamo Shrine (Ise)|lt=Kamo Shrine (Ise)|lt=鴨神社 (玉城町)|lt=Kamo {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q11398885]] Wikidata name: (none) [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 07:19, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q11398885]] Wikidata name: (none) [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 03:59, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} '''Kamo Shrine''' (加茂神社・賀茂神社) refers to Shinto shrines with the name "Kamo."
Most of these shrines were branches of the famous [[:en:Kamo shrines|Kamo Shrines]], [[:en:Kamigamo Shrine|Kamigamo Shrine]] (Upper Kamo Shrine) and [[:en:Shimogamo Shrine|Shimogamo Shrine]] (Lower Kamo Shrine), located in Kyoto. Many shrines are also historically tied to the ancient [[:en:Kamo clan|Kamo-no-agatanushi]] clan (賀茂県主氏).
There are multiple spellings so this article will cover all of them

== Kamo Shrine ([[:ja:鴨神社|鴨神社]]) ==
'''Kamo Shrine''' (鴨神社) is a Shinto shrine whose name includes the character "Kamo" (鴨).
* [[鴨神社_(玉城町)]] * [[:en:Kamo shrines|Kamo Shrine (Kamo-sha)]] ([[:en:Kyoto Prefecture|Kyoto]], [[:en:Kyoto|Kyoto City]]) - Another name for [[:en:Kamo shrines|Kamo Shrines]], which collectively refers to [[:en:Kamigamo Shrine|Kamigamo Shrine]] and [[:en:Shimogamo Shrine|Shimogamo Shrine]]. * {{ill|Kamo Shrine (Tamaki)|ja|Kamo Shrine (Ise)|lt=Kamo Shrine (Ise)|lt=鴨神社 (玉城町)|lt=Kamo <!--for some reason this has an independent Japanese article [[:ja:鴨神社]]--><!--note do not remove this category during translation--><!--Kamo Shrine (disambiguation)--><!--Kamo Shrine (disambiguation)--> [[Category:Kamo Shrines]] [[Category:Shrine disambiguations]] [[Category:Disambiguation pages]] <!--jawiki derived wikidata interwikis--> [[Category:translated pages with valid en interwikis]] [[Category:pages without english wikidata name]] [[Category:pages without english wikidata name]]

{{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q11398885]] Wikidata name: (none) [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 07:19, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q11398885]] Wikidata name: (none) [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 03:59, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} '''Kamo Shrine''' (加茂神社・賀茂神社) refers to Shinto shrines with the name "Kamo."
Most of these shrines were branches of the famous [[:en:Kamo shrines|Kamo Shrines]], [[:en:Kamigamo Shrine|Kamigamo Shrine]] (Upper Kamo Shrine) and [[:en:Shimogamo Shrine|Shimogamo Shrine]] (Lower Kamo Shrine), located in Kyoto. Many shrines are also historically tied to the ancient [[:en:Kamo clan|Kamo-no-agatanushi]] clan (賀茂県主氏).
There are multiple spellings so this article will cover all of them

== Kamo Shrine ([[:ja:鴨神社|鴨神社]]) ==
'''Kamo Shrine''' (鴨神社) is a Shinto shrine whose name includes the character "Kamo" (鴨).
* [[鴨神社_(玉城町)]] * [[:en:Kamo shrines|Kamo Shrine (Kamo-sha)]] ([[:en:Kyoto Prefecture|Kyoto]], [[:en:Kyoto|Kyoto City]]) - Another name for [[:en:Kamo shrines|Kamo Shrines]], which collectively refers to [[:en:Kamigamo Shrine|Kamigamo Shrine]] and [[:en:Shimogamo Shrine|Shimogamo Shrine]]. * {{ill|Kamo Shrine (Tamaki)|ja|Kamo Shrine (Ise)|lt=Kamo Shrine (Ise)|lt=鴨神社 (玉城町)|lt=Kamo {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q11398885]] Wikidata name: (none) [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 07:19, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q11398885]] Wikidata name: (none) [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 03:59, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} '''Kamo Shrine''' (加茂神社・賀茂神社) refers to Shinto shrines with the name "Kamo."
Most of these shrines were branches of the famous [[:en:Kamo shrines|Kamo Shrines]], [[:en:Kamigamo Shrine|Kamigamo Shrine]] (Upper Kamo Shrine) and [[:en:Shimogamo Shrine|Shimogamo Shrine]] (Lower Kamo Shrine), located in Kyoto. Many shrines are also historically tied to the ancient [[:en:Kamo clan|Kamo-no-agatanushi]] clan (賀茂県主氏).
There are multiple spellings so this article will cover all of them

== Kamo Shrine ([[:ja:鴨神社|鴨神社]]) ==
'''Kamo Shrine''' (鴨神社) is a Shinto shrine whose name includes the character "Kamo" (鴨).

{{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q11398885]] Wikidata name: (none) [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 07:19, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q11398885]] Wikidata name: (none) [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 03:59, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} '''Kamo Shrine''' (加茂神社・賀茂神社) refers to Shinto shrines with the name "Kamo."
Most of these shrines were branches of the famous [[:en:Kamo shrines|Kamo Shrines]], [[:en:Kamigamo Shrine|Kamigamo Shrine]] (Upper Kamo Shrine) and [[:en:Shimogamo Shrine|Shimogamo Shrine]] (Lower Kamo Shrine), located in Kyoto. Many shrines are also historically tied to the ancient [[:en:Kamo clan|Kamo-no-agatanushi]] clan (賀茂県主氏).
There are multiple spellings so this article will cover all of them

== Kamo Shrine ([[:ja:鴨神社|鴨神社]]) ==
'''Kamo Shrine''' (鴨神社) is a Shinto shrine whose name includes the character "Kamo" (鴨).
* [[鴨神社_(玉城町)]] * [[:en:Kamo shrines|Kamo Shrine (Kamo-sha)]] ([[:en:Kyoto Prefecture|Kyoto]], [[:en:Kyoto|Kyoto City]]) - Another name for [[:en:Kamo shrines|Kamo Shrines]], which collectively refers {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q11398885]] Wikidata name: (none) [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 07:19, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q11398885]] Wikidata name: (none) [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 03:59, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} '''Kamo Shrine''' (加茂神社・賀茂神社) refers to Shinto shrines with the name "Kamo."
Most of these shrines were branches of the famous [[:en:Kamo shrines|Kamo Shrines]], [[:en:Kamigamo Shrine|Kamigamo Shrine]] (Upper Kamo Shrine) and [[:en:Shimogamo Shrine|Shimogamo Shrine]] (Lower Kamo Shrine), located in Kyoto. Many shrines are also historically tied to the ancient [[:en:Kamo clan|Kamo-no-agatanushi]] clan (賀茂県主氏).
There are multiple spellings so this article will cover all of them

== Kamo Shrine ([[:ja:鴨神社|鴨神社]]) ==
'''Kamo Shrine''' (鴨神社) is a Shinto shrine whose name includes the character "Kamo" (鴨).

{{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q11398885]] Wikidata name: (none) [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 07:19, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q11398885]] Wikidata name: (none) [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 03:59, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} '''Kamo Shrine''' (加茂神社・賀茂神社) refers to Shinto shrines with the name "Kamo."
Most of these shrines were branches of the famous [[:en:Kamo shrines|Kamo Shrines]], [[:en:Kamigamo Shrine|Kamigamo Shrine]] (Upper Kamo Shrine) and [[:en:Shimogamo Shrine|Shimogamo Shrine]] (Lower Kamo Shrine), located in Kyoto. Many shrines are also historically tied to the ancient [[:en:Kamo clan|Kamo-no-agatanushi]] clan (賀茂県主氏).
There are multiple spellings so this article will cover all of them

== Kamo Shrine ([[:ja:鴨神社|鴨神社]]) ==
'''Kamo Shrine''' (鴨神社) is a Shinto shrine whose name includes the character "Kamo" (鴨).
* [[鴨神社_(玉城町)]] * [[:en:Kamo shrines|Kamo Shrine (Kamo-sha)]] ([[:en:Kyoto Prefecture|Kyoto]], [[:en:Kyoto|Kyoto City]]) - Another name for [[:en:Kamo shrines|Kamo Shrines]], which collectively refers to [[:en:Kamigamo Shrine|Kamigamo Shrine]] and [[:en:Shimogamo Shrine|Shimogamo Shrine]]. * {{ill|Kamo Shrine (Tamaki)|ja|Kamo Shrine (Ise)|lt=Kamo Shrine (Ise)|lt=鴨神社 (玉城町)|lt=Kamo {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q11398885]] Wikidata name: (none) [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 07:19, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q11398885]] Wikidata name: (none) [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 03:59, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} '''Kamo Shrine''' (加茂神社・賀茂神社) refers to Shinto shrines with the name "Kamo."
Most of these shrines were branches of the famous [[:en:Kamo shrines|Kamo Shrines]], [[:en:Kamigamo Shrine|Kamigamo Shrine]] (Upper Kamo Shrine) and [[:en:Shimogamo Shrine|Shimogamo Shrine]] (Lower Kamo Shrine), located in Kyoto. Many shrines are also historically tied to the ancient [[:en:Kamo clan|Kamo-no-agatanushi]] clan (賀茂県主氏).
There are multiple spellings so this article will cover all of them

== Kamo Shrine ([[:ja:鴨神社|鴨神社]]) ==
'''Kamo Shrine''' (鴨神社) is a Shinto shrine whose name includes the character "Kamo" (鴨).

to [[:en:Kamigamo Shrine|Kamigamo Shrine]] and [[:en:Shimogamo Shrine|Shimogamo Shrine]]. * {{ill|Kamo Shrine (Tamaki)|ja|Kamo Shrine (Ise)|lt=Kamo Shrine (Ise)|lt=鴨神社 (玉城町)|lt=Kamo {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q11398885]] Wikidata name: (none) [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 07:19, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q11398885]] Wikidata name: (none) [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 03:59, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} '''Kamo Shrine''' (加茂神社・賀茂神社) refers to Shinto shrines with the name "Kamo."
Most of these shrines were branches of the famous [[:en:Kamo shrines|Kamo Shrines]], [[:en:Kamigamo Shrine|Kamigamo Shrine]] (Upper Kamo Shrine) and [[:en:Shimogamo Shrine|Shimogamo Shrine]] (Lower Kamo Shrine), located in Kyoto. Many shrines are also historically tied to the ancient [[:en:Kamo clan|Kamo-no-agatanushi]] clan (賀茂県主氏).
There are multiple spellings so this article will cover all of them

== Kamo Shrine ([[:ja:鴨神社|鴨神社]]) ==
'''Kamo Shrine''' (鴨神社) is a Shinto shrine whose name includes the character "Kamo" (鴨).

to [[:en:Kamigamo Shrine|Kamigamo Shrine]] and [[:en:Shimogamo Shrine|Shimogamo Shrine]]. * {{ill|Kamo Shrine (Tamaki)|ja|Kamo Shrine (Ise)|lt=Kamo Shrine (Ise)|lt=鴨神社 (玉城町)|lt=Kamo {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q11398885]] Wikidata name: (none) [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 07:19, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q11398885]] Wikidata name: (none) [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 03:59, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} '''Kamo Shrine''' (加茂神社・賀茂神社) refers to Shinto shrines with the name "Kamo."
Most of these shrines were branches of the famous [[:en:Kamo shrines|Kamo Shrines]], [[:en:Kamigamo Shrine|Kamigamo Shrine]] (Upper Kamo Shrine) and [[:en:Shimogamo Shrine|Shimogamo Shrine]] (Lower Kamo Shrine), located in Kyoto. Many shrines are also historically tied to the ancient [[:en:Kamo clan|Kamo-no-agatanushi]] clan (賀茂県主氏).
There are multiple spellings so this article will cover all of them

== Kamo Shrine ([[:ja:鴨神社|鴨神社]]) ==
'''Kamo Shrine''' (鴨神社) is a Shinto shrine whose name includes the character "Kamo" (鴨).

<!--for some reason this has an independent Japanese article [[:ja:鴨神社]]--><!--note do not remove this category during translation--><!--Kamo Shrine (disambiguation)--><!--Kamo Shrine (disambiguation)--> [[Category:Kamo Shrines]] [[Category:Shrine disambiguations]] [[Category:Disambiguation pages]] <!--jawiki derived wikidata interwikis--> [[Category:translated pages with valid en interwikis]] [[Category:pages without english wikidata name]] [[Category:pages without english wikidata name]]

{{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q11398885]] Wikidata name: (none) [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 07:19, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q11398885]] Wikidata name: (none) [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 03:59, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} '''Kamo Shrine''' (加茂神社・賀茂神社) refers to Shinto shrines with the name "Kamo."
Most of these shrines were branches of the famous [[:en:Kamo shrines|Kamo Shrines]], [[:en:Kamigamo Shrine|Kamigamo Shrine]] (Upper Kamo Shrine) and [[:en:Shimogamo Shrine|Shimogamo Shrine]] (Lower Kamo Shrine), located in Kyoto. Many shrines are also historically tied to the ancient [[:en:Kamo clan|Kamo-no-agatanushi]] clan (賀茂県主氏).

== Kamo Shrine ([[:ja:鴨神社|鴨神社]]) ==
'''Kamo Shrine''' (鴨神社) is a Shinto shrine whose name includes the character "Kamo" (鴨).

{{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q11398885]] Wikidata name: (none) [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 07:19, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q11398885]] Wikidata name: (none) [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 03:59, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} '''Kamo Shrine''' (加茂神社・賀茂神社) refers to Shinto shrines with the name "Kamo."
Most of these shrines were branches of the famous [[:en:Kamo shrines|Kamo Shrines]], [[:en:Kamigamo Shrine|Kamigamo Shrine]] (Upper Kamo Shrine) and [[:en:Shimogamo Shrine|Shimogamo Shrine]] (Lower Kamo Shrine), located in Kyoto. Many shrines are also historically tied to the ancient [[:en:Kamo clan|Kamo-no-agatanushi]] clan (賀茂県主氏).
There are multiple spellings so this article will cover all of them

== Kamo Shrine ([[:ja:鴨神社|鴨神社]]) ==

{{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q11398885]] Wikidata name: (none) [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 07:19, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q11398885]] Wikidata name: (none) [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 03:59, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} '''Kamo Shrine''' (加茂神社・賀茂神社) refers to Shinto shrines with the name "Kamo."
Most of these shrines were branches of the famous [[:en:Kamo shrines|Kamo Shrines]], [[:en:Kamigamo Shrine|Kamigamo Shrine]] (Upper Kamo Shrine) and [[:en:Shimogamo Shrine|Shimogamo Shrine]] (Lower Kamo Shrine), located in Kyoto. Many shrines are also historically tied to the ancient [[:en:Kamo clan|Kamo-no-agatanushi]] clan (賀茂県主氏).
There are multiple spellings so this article will cover all of them

== Kamo Shrine ([[:ja:鴨神社|鴨神社]]) ==
'''Kamo Shrine''' (鴨神社) is a Shinto shrine whose name includes the character "Kamo" (鴨).

{{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q11398885]] Wikidata name: (none) [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 07:19, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q11398885]] Wikidata name: (none) [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 03:59, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} '''Kamo Shrine''' (加茂神社・賀茂神社) refers to Shinto shrines with the name "Kamo."
Most of these shrines were branches of the famous [[:en:Kamo shrines|Kamo Shrines]], [[:en:Kamigamo Shrine|Kamigamo Shrine]] (Upper Kamo Shrine) and [[:en:Shimogamo Shrine|Shimogamo Shrine]] (Lower Kamo Shrine), located in Kyoto. Many shrines are also historically tied to the ancient [[:en:Kamo clan|Kamo-no-agatanushi]] clan (賀茂県主氏).
There are multiple spellings so this article will cover all of them

== Kamo Shrine ([[:ja:鴨神社|鴨神社]]) ==
'''Kamo Shrine''' (鴨神社) is a Shinto shrine whose name includes the character "Kamo" (鴨).

{{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q11398885]] Wikidata name: (none) [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 07:19, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q11398885]] Wikidata name: (none) [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 03:59, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} '''Kamo Shrine''' (加茂神社・賀茂神社) refers to Shinto shrines with the name "Kamo."
Most of these shrines were branches of the famous [[:en:Kamo shrines|Kamo Shrines]], [[:en:Kamigamo Shrine|Kamigamo Shrine]] (Upper Kamo Shrine) and [[:en:Shimogamo Shrine|Shimogamo Shrine]] (Lower Kamo Shrine), located in Kyoto. Many shrines are also historically tied to the ancient [[:en:Kamo clan|Kamo-no-agatanushi]] clan (賀茂県主氏).
There are multiple spellings so this article will cover all of them

== Kamo Shrine ([[:ja:鴨神社|鴨神社]]) ==
'''Kamo Shrine''' (鴨神社) is a Shinto shrine whose name includes the character "Kamo" (鴨).

{{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q11398885]] Wikidata name: (none) [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 07:19, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q11398885]] Wikidata name: (none) [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 03:59, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} '''Kamo Shrine''' (加茂神社・賀茂神社) refers to Shinto shrines with the name "Kamo."
Most of these shrines were branches of the famous [[:en:Kamo shrines|Kamo Shrines]], [[:en:Kamigamo Shrine|Kamigamo Shrine]] (Upper Kamo Shrine) and [[:en:Shimogamo Shrine|Shimogamo Shrine]] (Lower Kamo Shrine), located in Kyoto. Many shrines are also historically tied to the ancient [[:en:Kamo clan|Kamo-no-agatanushi]] clan (賀茂県主氏).
There are multiple spellings so this article will cover all of them

== Kamo Shrine ([[:ja:鴨神社|鴨神社]]) ==
'''Kamo Shrine''' (鴨神社) is a Shinto shrine whose name includes the character "Kamo" (鴨).

{{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q11398885]] Wikidata name: (none) [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 07:19, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q11398885]] Wikidata name: (none) [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 03:59, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} '''Kamo Shrine''' (加茂神社・賀茂神社) refers to Shinto shrines with the name "Kamo."
Most of these shrines were branches of the famous [[:en:Kamo shrines|Kamo Shrines]], [[:en:Kamigamo Shrine|Kamigamo Shrine]] (Upper Kamo Shrine) and [[:en:Shimogamo Shrine|Shimogamo Shrine]] (Lower Kamo Shrine), located in Kyoto. Many shrines are also historically tied to the ancient [[:en:Kamo clan|Kamo-no-agatanushi]] clan (賀茂県主氏).
There are multiple spellings so this article will cover all of them

== Kamo Shrine ([[:ja:鴨神社|鴨神社]]) ==
'''Kamo Shrine''' (鴨神社) is a Shinto shrine whose name includes the character "Kamo" (鴨).
* [[鴨神社_(玉城町)]] * [[:en:Kamo shrines|Kamo Shrine (Kamo-sha)]] ([[:en:Kyoto Prefecture|Kyoto]], [[:en:Kyoto|Kyoto City]]) - Another name for [[:en:Kamo shrines|Kamo Shrines]], which collectively refers to [[:en:Kamigamo Shrine|Kamigamo Shrine]] and [[:en:Shimogamo Shrine|Shimogamo Shrine]]. * {{ill|Kamo Shrine (Tamaki)|ja|Kamo Shrine (Ise)|lt=Kamo Shrine (Ise)|lt=鴨神社 (玉城町)|lt=Kamo revision.text:
{{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q11398885]] Wikidata name: (none) [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 07:19, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q11398885]] Wikidata name: (none) [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 03:59, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} '''Kamo Shrine''' (加茂神社・賀茂神社) refers to Shinto shrines with the name "Kamo."
Most of these shrines were branches of the famous [[:en:Kamo shrines|Kamo Shrines]], [[:en:Kamigamo Shrine|Kamigamo Shrine]] (Upper Kamo Shrine) and [[:en:Shimogamo Shrine|Shimogamo Shrine]] (Lower Kamo Shrine), located in Kyoto. Many shrines are also historically tied to the ancient [[:en:Kamo clan|Kamo-no-agatanushi]] clan (賀茂県主氏).
There are multiple spellings so this article will cover all of them

Theories of its location include {{ill|Higashi Kamo Shrine|ja|鴨神社 (坂出市)#東鴨神社|lt=Higashi Kamo Shrine|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Higashi Kamo Shrine|qq=|1=Kamo Shrine (Sakaide)|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11675600}} and {{ill|Nishi Kamo Shrine|ja|鴨神社 (坂出市)#西鴨神社|lt=Nishi Kamo Shrine|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nishi Kamo Shrine|qq=|1=Kamo Shrine (Sakaide)|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11675600}} (both in [[:en:Sakaide, Kagawa|Sakaide]], Kamochō).

{{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q11398885]] Wikidata name: (none) [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 07:19, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q11398885]] Wikidata name: (none) [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 03:59, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} '''Kamo Shrine''' (加茂神社・賀茂神社) refers to Shinto shrines with the name "Kamo."
Most of these shrines were branches of the famous [[:en:Kamo shrines|Kamo Shrines]], [[:en:Kamigamo Shrine|Kamigamo Shrine]] (Upper Kamo Shrine) and [[:en:Shimogamo Shrine|Shimogamo Shrine]] (Lower Kamo Shrine), located in Kyoto. Many shrines are also historically tied to the ancient [[:en:Kamo clan|Kamo-no-agatanushi]] clan (賀茂県主氏).
There are multiple spellings so this article will cover all of them

== Kamo Shrine ([[:ja:鴨神社|鴨神社]]) ==
'''Kamo Shrine''' (鴨神社) is a Shinto shrine whose name includes the character "Kamo" (鴨).
* [[鴨神社_(玉城町)]] * [[:en:Kamo shrines|Kamo Shrine (Kamo-sha)]] ([[:en:Kyoto Prefecture|Kyoto]], [[:en:Kyoto|Kyoto City]]) - Another name for [[:en:Kamo shrines|Kamo Shrines]], which collectively refers to [[:en:Kamigamo Shrine|Kamigamo Shrine]] and [[:en:Shimogamo Shrine|Shimogamo Shrine]]. * {{ill|Kamo Shrine (Tamaki)|ja|Kamo Shrine (Ise)|lt=Kamo Shrine (Ise)|lt=鴨神社 (玉城町)|lt=Kamo <!--for some reason this has an independent Japanese article [[:ja:鴨神社]]--><!--note do not remove this category during translation--><!--Kamo Shrine (disambiguation)--><!--Kamo Shrine (disambiguation)--> [[Category:Kamo Shrines]] [[Category:Shrine disambiguations]] [[Category:Disambiguation pages]] <!--jawiki derived wikidata interwikis--> [[Category:translated pages with valid en interwikis]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]] {{wikidata link|Q11398885}}

{{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q11398885]] Wikidata name: (none) [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 07:19, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q11398885]] Wikidata name: (none) [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 03:59, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} '''Kamo Shrine''' (加茂神社・賀茂神社) refers to Shinto shrines with the name "Kamo."
Most of these shrines were branches of the famous [[:en:Kamo shrines|Kamo Shrines]], [[:en:Kamigamo Shrine|Kamigamo Shrine]] (Upper Kamo Shrine) and [[:en:Shimogamo Shrine|Shimogamo Shrine]] (Lower Kamo Shrine), located in Kyoto. Many shrines are also historically tied to the ancient [[:en:Kamo clan|Kamo-no-agatanushi]] clan (賀茂県主氏).
There are multiple spellings so this article will cover all of them

== Kamo Shrine ([[:ja:鴨神社|鴨神社]]) ==
'''Kamo Shrine''' (鴨神社) is a Shinto shrine whose name includes the character "Kamo" (鴨).

<!--for some reason this has an independent Japanese article [[:ja:鴨神社]]--><!--note do not remove this category during translation--><!--Kamo Shrine (disambiguation)--><!--Kamo Shrine (disambiguation)--> [[Category:Kamo Shrines]] [[Category:Shrine disambiguations]] [[Category:Disambiguation pages]] <!--jawiki derived wikidata interwikis--> [[Category:translated pages with valid en interwikis]] {{wikidata link|Q11398885}}

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:加茂神社]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q11398885]] Wikidata name: (none) [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 07:19, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q11398885]] Wikidata name: (none) [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 03:59, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} '''Kamo Shrine''' (加茂神社・賀茂神社) refers to Shinto shrines with the name "Kamo."
Most of these shrines were branches of the famous [[:en:Kamo shrines|Kamo Shrines]], [[:en:Kamigamo Shrine|Kamigamo Shrine]] (Upper Kamo Shrine) and [[:en:Shimogamo Shrine|Shimogamo Shrine]] (Lower Kamo Shrine), located in Kyoto. Many shrines are also historically tied to the ancient [[:en:Kamo clan|Kamo-no-agatanushi]] clan (賀茂県主氏).
There are multiple spellings so this article will cover all of them

== Kamo Shrine ([[:ja:鴨神社|鴨神社]]) ==
'''Kamo Shrine''' (鴨神社) is a Shinto shrine whose name includes the character "Kamo" (鴨).
* [[鴨神社_(玉城町)]] * [[:en:Kamo shrines|Kamo Shrine (Kamo-sha)]] ([[:en:Kyoto Prefecture|Kyoto]], [[:en:Kyoto|Kyoto City]]) - Another name for [[:en:Kamo shrines|Kamo Shrines]], which collectively refers <!--for some reason this has an independent Japanese article [[:ja:鴨神社]]--><!--note do not remove this category during translation--><!--Kamo Shrine (disambiguation)--><!--Kamo Shrine (disambiguation)--> [[Category:Kamo Shrines]] [[Category:Shrine disambiguations]] [[Category:Disambiguation pages]] <!--jawiki derived wikidata interwikis--> [[Category:translated pages with valid en interwikis]] {{wikidata link|Q11398885}}

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:加茂神社]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q11398885]] Wikidata name: (none) [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 07:19, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q11398885]] Wikidata name: (none) [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 03:59, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} '''Kamo Shrine''' (加茂神社・賀茂神社) refers to Shinto shrines with the name "Kamo."
Most of these shrines were branches of the famous [[:en:Kamo shrines|Kamo Shrines]], [[:en:Kamigamo Shrine|Kamigamo Shrine]] (Upper Kamo Shrine) and [[:en:Shimogamo Shrine|Shimogamo Shrine]] (Lower Kamo Shrine), located in Kyoto. Many shrines are also historically tied to the ancient [[:en:Kamo clan|Kamo-no-agatanushi]] clan (賀茂県主氏).
There are multiple spellings so this article will cover all of them

== Kamo Shrine ([[:ja:鴨神社|鴨神社]]) ==
'''Kamo Shrine''' (鴨神社) is a Shinto shrine whose name includes the character "Kamo" (鴨).

<!--for some reason this has an independent Japanese article [[:ja:鴨神社]]--><!--note do not remove this category during translation--><!--Kamo Shrine (disambiguation)--><!--Kamo Shrine (disambiguation)--> [[Category:Kamo Shrines]] [[Category:Shrine disambiguations]] [[Category:Disambiguation pages]] <!--jawiki derived wikidata interwikis--> [[Category:translated pages with valid en interwikis]] {{wikidata link|Q11398885}}

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:加茂神社]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q11398885]] Wikidata name: (none) [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 07:19, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q11398885]] Wikidata name: (none) [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 03:59, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} '''Kamo Shrine''' (加茂神社・賀茂神社) refers to Shinto shrines with the name "Kamo."
Most of these shrines were branches of the famous [[:en:Kamo shrines|Kamo Shrines]], [[:en:Kamigamo Shrine|Kamigamo Shrine]] (Upper Kamo Shrine) and [[:en:Shimogamo Shrine|Shimogamo Shrine]] (Lower Kamo Shrine), located in Kyoto. Many shrines are also historically tied to the ancient [[:en:Kamo clan|Kamo-no-agatanushi]] clan (賀茂県主氏).
There are multiple spellings so this article will cover all of them

== Kamo Shrine ([[:ja:鴨神社|鴨神社]]) ==
'''Kamo Shrine''' (鴨神社) is a Shinto shrine whose name includes the character "Kamo" (鴨).
* [[鴨神社_(玉城町)]] * [[:en:Kamo shrines|Kamo Shrine (Kamo-sha)]] ([[:en:Kyoto Prefecture|Kyoto]], [[:en:Kyoto|Kyoto City]]) - Another name for [[:en:Kamo shrines|Kamo Shrines]], which collectively refers <!--for some reason this has an independent Japanese article [[:ja:鴨神社]]--><!--note do not remove this category during translation--><!--Kamo Shrine (disambiguation)--><!--Kamo Shrine (disambiguation)--> [[Category:Kamo Shrines]] [[Category:Shrine disambiguations]] [[Category:Disambiguation pages]] <!--jawiki derived wikidata interwikis--> [[Category:translated pages with valid en interwikis]] {{wikidata link|Q11398885}}

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:加茂神社]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q11398885]] Wikidata name: (none) [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 07:19, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q11398885]] Wikidata name: (none) [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 03:59, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} '''Kamo Shrine''' (加茂神社・賀茂神社) refers to Shinto shrines with the name "Kamo."
Most of these shrines were branches of the famous [[:en:Kamo shrines|Kamo Shrines]], [[:en:Kamigamo Shrine|Kamigamo Shrine]] (Upper Kamo Shrine) and [[:en:Shimogamo Shrine|Shimogamo Shrine]] (Lower Kamo Shrine), located in Kyoto. Many shrines are also historically tied to the ancient [[:en:Kamo clan|Kamo-no-agatanushi]] clan (賀茂県主氏).

== Kamo Shrine ([[:ja:鴨神社|鴨神社]]) ==
'''Kamo Shrine''' (鴨神社) is a Shinto shrine whose name includes the character "Kamo" (鴨).

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:加茂神社]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q11398885]] Wikidata name: (none) [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 07:19, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q11398885]] Wikidata name: (none) [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 03:59, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} '''Kamo Shrine''' (加茂神社・賀茂神社) refers to Shinto shrines with the name "Kamo."
Most of these shrines were branches of the famous [[:en:Kamo shrines|Kamo Shrines]], [[:en:Kamigamo Shrine|Kamigamo Shrine]] (Upper Kamo Shrine) and [[:en:Shimogamo Shrine|Shimogamo Shrine]] (Lower Kamo Shrine), located in Kyoto. Many shrines are also historically tied to the ancient [[:en:Kamo clan|Kamo-no-agatanushi]] clan (賀茂県主氏).
There are multiple spellings so this article will cover all of them

== Kamo Shrine ([[:ja:鴨神社|鴨神社]]) ==

<!--for some reason this has an independent Japanese article [[:ja:鴨神社]]--><!--note do not remove this category during translation--><!--Kamo Shrine (disambiguation)--><!--Kamo Shrine (disambiguation)--> [[Category:Kamo Shrines]] [[Category:Shrine disambiguations]] [[Category:Disambiguation pages]] <!--jawiki derived wikidata interwikis--> [[Category:translated pages with valid en interwikis]] {{wikidata link|Q11398885}}

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:加茂神社]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q11398885]] Wikidata name: (none) [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 07:19, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q11398885]] Wikidata name: (none) [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 03:59, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} '''Kamo Shrine''' (加茂神社・賀茂神社) refers to Shinto shrines with the name "Kamo."
Most of these shrines were branches of the famous [[:en:Kamo shrines|Kamo Shrines]], [[:en:Kamigamo Shrine|Kamigamo Shrine]] (Upper Kamo Shrine) and [[:en:Shimogamo Shrine|Shimogamo Shrine]] (Lower Kamo Shrine), located in Kyoto. Many shrines are also historically tied to the ancient [[:en:Kamo clan|Kamo-no-agatanushi]] clan (賀茂県主氏).
There are multiple spellings so this article will cover all of them

== Kamo Shrine ([[:ja:鴨神社|鴨神社]]) ==
'''Kamo Shrine''' (鴨神社) is a Shinto shrine whose name includes the character "Kamo" (鴨).

<!--for some reason this has an independent Japanese article [[:ja:鴨神社]]--><!--note do not remove this category during translation--><!--Kamo Shrine (disambiguation)--><!--Kamo Shrine (disambiguation)--> [[Category:Kamo Shrines]] [[Category:Shrine disambiguations]] [[Category:Disambiguation pages]] <!--jawiki derived wikidata interwikis--> [[Category:translated pages with valid en interwikis]] {{wikidata link|Q11398885}}

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:加茂神社]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q11398885]] Wikidata name: (none) [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 07:19, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q11398885]] Wikidata name: (none) [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 03:59, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} '''Kamo Shrine''' (加茂神社・賀茂神社) refers to Shinto shrines with the name "Kamo."
Most of these shrines were branches of the famous [[:en:Kamo shrines|Kamo Shrines]], [[:en:Kamigamo Shrine|Kamigamo Shrine]] (Upper Kamo Shrine) and [[:en:Shimogamo Shrine|Shimogamo Shrine]] (Lower Kamo Shrine), located in Kyoto. Many shrines are also historically tied to the ancient [[:en:Kamo clan|Kamo-no-agatanushi]] clan (賀茂県主氏).
There are multiple spellings so this article will cover all of them

== Kamo Shrine ([[:ja:鴨神社|鴨神社]]) ==

revision.text:
<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:加茂神社]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q11398885]] Wikidata name: (none) [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 07:19, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q11398885]] Wikidata name: (none) [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 03:59, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} '''Kamo Shrine''' (加茂神社・賀茂神社) refers to Shinto shrines with the name "Kamo."
Most of these shrines were branches of the famous [[:en:Kamo shrines|Kamo Shrines]], [[:en:Kamigamo Shrine|Kamigamo Shrine]] (Upper Kamo Shrine) and [[:en:Shimogamo Shrine|Shimogamo Shrine]] (Lower Kamo Shrine), located in Kyoto. Many shrines are also historically tied to the ancient [[:en:Kamo clan|Kamo-no-agatanushi]] clan (賀茂県主氏).
There are multiple spellings so this article will cover all of them

== Kamo Shrine ([[:ja:鴨神社|鴨神社]]) ==
'''Kamo Shrine''' (鴨神社) is a Shinto shrine whose name includes the character "Kamo" (鴨).
* [[鴨神社_(玉城町)]] * [[:en:Kamo shrines|Kamo Shrine (Kamo-sha)]] ([[:en:Kyoto Prefecture|Kyoto]], [[:en:Kyoto|Kyoto City]]) - Another name for [[:en:Kamo shrines|Kamo Shrines]], which collectively refers to [[:en:Kamigamo Shrine|Kamigamo Shrine]] and [[:en:Shimogamo Shrine|Shimogamo Shrine]]. * {{ill|Kamo Shrine (Tamaki)|ja|Kamo Shrine (Ise)|lt=Kamo Shrine (Ise)|lt=鴨神社 (玉城町)|lt=Kamo <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:加茂神社]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q11398885]] Wikidata name: (none) [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 07:19, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q11398885]] Wikidata name: (none) [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 03:59, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} '''Kamo Shrine''' (加茂神社・賀茂神社) refers to Shinto shrines with the name "Kamo."
Most of these shrines were branches of the famous [[:en:Kamo shrines|Kamo Shrines]], [[:en:Kamigamo Shrine|Kamigamo Shrine]] (Upper Kamo Shrine) and [[:en:Shimogamo Shrine|Shimogamo Shrine]] (Lower Kamo Shrine), located in Kyoto. Many shrines are also historically tied to the ancient [[:en:Kamo clan|Kamo-no-agatanushi]] clan (賀茂県主氏).
There are multiple spellings so this article will cover all of them

== Kamo Shrine ([[:ja:鴨神社|鴨神社]]) ==
'''Kamo Shrine''' (鴨神社) is a Shinto shrine whose name includes the character "Kamo" (鴨).
* [[鴨神社_(玉城町)]] * [[:en:Kamo shrines|Kamo Shrine (Kamo-sha)]] ([[:en:Kyoto Prefecture|Kyoto]], [[:en:Kyoto|Kyoto City]]) - Another name for [[:en:Kamo shrines|Kamo Shrines]], which collectively refers to [[:en:Kamigamo Shrine|Kamigamo Shrine]] and [[:en:Shimogamo Shrine|Shimogamo Shrine]]. * {{ill|Kamo Shrine (Tamaki)|ja|Kamo Shrine (Ise)|lt=Kamo Shrine (Ise)|lt=鴨神社 (玉城町)|lt=Kamo <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:加茂神社]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q11398885]] Wikidata name: (none) [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 07:19, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q11398885]] Wikidata name: (none) [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 03:59, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} '''Kamo Shrine''' (加茂神社・賀茂神社) refers to Shinto shrines with the name "Kamo."
Most of these shrines were branches of the famous [[:en:Kamo shrines|Kamo Shrines]], [[:en:Kamigamo Shrine|Kamigamo Shrine]] (Upper Kamo Shrine) and [[:en:Shimogamo Shrine|Shimogamo Shrine]] (Lower Kamo Shrine), located in Kyoto. Many shrines are also historically tied to the ancient [[:en:Kamo clan|Kamo-no-agatanushi]] clan (賀茂県主氏).
There are multiple spellings so this article will cover all of them

== Kamo Shrine ([[:ja:鴨神社|鴨神社]]) ==

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:加茂神社]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q11398885]] Wikidata name: (none) [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 07:19, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q11398885]] Wikidata name: (none) [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 03:59, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} '''Kamo Shrine''' (加茂神社・賀茂神社) refers to Shinto shrines with the name "Kamo."
Most of these shrines were branches of the famous [[:en:Kamo shrines|Kamo Shrines]], [[:en:Kamigamo Shrine|Kamigamo Shrine]] (Upper Kamo Shrine) and [[:en:Shimogamo Shrine|Shimogamo Shrine]] (Lower Kamo Shrine), located in Kyoto. Many shrines are also historically tied to the ancient [[:en:Kamo clan|Kamo-no-agatanushi]] clan (賀茂県主氏).
There are multiple spellings so this article will cover all of them

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:加茂神社]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q11398885]] Wikidata name: (none) [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 07:19, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q11398885]] Wikidata name: (none) [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 03:59, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} '''Kamo Shrine''' (加茂神社・賀茂神社) refers to Shinto shrines with the name "Kamo."
Most of these shrines were branches of the famous [[:en:Kamo shrines|Kamo Shrines]], [[:en:Kamigamo Shrine|Kamigamo Shrine]] (Upper Kamo Shrine) and [[:en:Shimogamo Shrine|Shimogamo Shrine]] (Lower Kamo Shrine), located in Kyoto. Many shrines are also historically tied to the ancient [[:en:Kamo clan|Kamo-no-agatanushi]] clan (賀茂県主氏).
There are multiple spellings so this article will cover all of them

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:加茂神社]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q11398885]] Wikidata name: (none) [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 07:19, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q11398885]] Wikidata name: (none) [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 03:59, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} '''Kamo Shrine''' (加茂神社・賀茂神社) refers to Shinto shrines with the name "Kamo."
Most of these shrines were branches of the famous [[:en:Kamo shrines|Kamo Shrines]], [[:en:Kamigamo Shrine|Kamigamo Shrine]] (Upper Kamo Shrine) and [[:en:Shimogamo Shrine|Shimogamo Shrine]] (Lower Kamo Shrine), located in Kyoto. Many shrines are also historically tied to the ancient [[:en:Kamo clan|Kamo-no-agatanushi]] clan (賀茂県主氏).
There are multiple spellings so this article will cover all of them

== Kamo Shrine ([[:ja:鴨神社|鴨神社]]) ==

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:加茂神社]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q11398885]] Wikidata name: (none) [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 07:19, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q11398885]] Wikidata name: (none) [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 03:59, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} '''Kamo Shrine''' (加茂神社・賀茂神社) refers to Shinto shrines with the name "Kamo."
Most of these shrines were branches of the famous [[:en:Kamo shrines|Kamo Shrines]], [[:en:Kamigamo Shrine|Kamigamo Shrine]] (Upper Kamo Shrine) and [[:en:Shimogamo Shrine|Shimogamo Shrine]] (Lower Kamo Shrine), located in Kyoto. Many shrines are also historically tied to the ancient [[:en:Kamo clan|Kamo-no-agatanushi]] clan (賀茂県主氏).
There are multiple spellings so this article will cover all of them

== Kamo Shrine ([[:ja:鴨神社|鴨神社]]) ==
'''Kamo Shrine''' (鴨神社) is a Shinto shrine whose name includes the character "Kamo" (鴨).
* [[鴨神社_(玉城町)]] * [[:en:Kamo shrines|Kamo Shrine (Kamo-sha)]] ([[:en:Kyoto Prefecture|Kyoto]], [[:en:Kyoto|Kyoto City]]) - Another name for [[:en:Kamo shrines|Kamo Shrines]], which collectively refers to [[:en:Kamigamo Shrine|Kamigamo Shrine]] and [[:en:Shimogamo Shrine|Shimogamo Shrine]]. * {{ill|Kamo Shrine (Tamaki)|ja|Kamo Shrine (Ise)|lt=Kamo Shrine (Ise)|lt=鴨神社 (玉城町)|lt=Kamo <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:加茂神社]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:加茂神社]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q11398885]] Wikidata name: (none) [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 07:19, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q11398885]] Wikidata name: (none) [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 03:59, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} '''Kamo Shrine''' (加茂神社・賀茂神社) refers to Shinto shrines with the name "Kamo."
Most of these shrines were branches of the famous [[:en:Kamo shrines|Kamo Shrines]], [[:en:Kamigamo Shrine|Kamigamo Shrine]] (Upper Kamo Shrine) and [[:en:Shimogamo Shrine|Shimogamo Shrine]] (Lower Kamo Shrine), located in Kyoto. Many shrines are also historically tied to the ancient [[:en:Kamo clan|Kamo-no-agatanushi]] clan (賀茂県主氏).
There are multiple spellings so this article will cover all of them

<!--for some reason this has an independent Japanese article [[:ja:鴨神社]]--><!--note do not remove this category during translation--><!--Kamo Shrine (disambiguation)--><!--Kamo Shrine (disambiguation)--> [[Category:Kamo Shrines]] [[Category:Shrine disambiguations]] [[Category:Disambiguation pages]] <!--jawiki derived wikidata interwikis--> [[Category:translated pages with valid en interwikis]] {{wikidata link|Q11398885}} [[Category:Wikidata has short description]] [[Category:Wikidata has English label]]

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:加茂神社]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:加茂神社]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:加茂神社]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q11398885]] Wikidata name: (none) [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 07:19, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q11398885]] Wikidata name: (none) [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 03:59, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} '''Kamo Shrine''' (加茂神社・賀茂神社) refers to Shinto shrines with the name "Kamo."
Most of these shrines were branches of the famous [[:en:Kamo shrines|Kamo Shrines]], [[:en:Kamigamo Shrine|Kamigamo Shrine]] (Upper Kamo Shrine) and [[:en:Shimogamo Shrine|Shimogamo Shrine]] (Lower Kamo Shrine), located in Kyoto. Many shrines are also historically tied to the ancient [[:en:Kamo clan|Kamo-no-agatanushi]] clan (賀茂県主氏).
There are multiple spellings so this article will cover all of them

<!--for some reason this has an independent Japanese article [[:ja:鴨神社]]--><!--note do not remove this category during translation--><!--Kamo Shrine (disambiguation)--><!--Kamo Shrine (disambiguation)--> [[Category:Kamo Shrines]] [[Category:Shrine disambiguations]] [[Category:Disambiguation pages]] <!--jawiki derived wikidata interwikis--> [[Category:translated pages with valid en interwikis]] {{wikidata link|Q11398885}} [[Category:Wikidata has short description]] [[Category:Wikidata has English label]]

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:加茂神社]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:加茂神社]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:加茂神社]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:加茂神社]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q11398885]] Wikidata name: (none) [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 07:19, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q11398885]] Wikidata name: (none) [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 03:59, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} '''Kamo Shrine''' (加茂神社・賀茂神社) refers to Shinto shrines with the name "Kamo."
Most of these shrines were branches of the famous [[:en:Kamo shrines|Kamo Shrines]], [[:en:Kamigamo Shrine|Kamigamo Shrine]] (Upper Kamo Shrine) and [[:en:Shimogamo Shrine|Shimogamo Shrine]] (Lower Kamo Shrine), located in Kyoto. Many shrines are also historically tied to the ancient [[:en:Kamo clan|Kamo-no-agatanushi]] clan (賀茂県主氏).
There are multiple spellings so this article will cover all of them

revision.text:
<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:加茂神社]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:加茂神社]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:加茂神社]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:加茂神社]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q11398885]] Wikidata name: (none) [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 07:19, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q11398885]] Wikidata name: (none) [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 03:59, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} '''Kamo Shrine''' (加茂神社・賀茂神社) refers to Shinto shrines with the name "Kamo."
Most of these shrines were branches of the famous [[:en:Kamo shrines|Kamo Shrines]], [[:en:Kamigamo Shrine|Kamigamo Shrine]] (Upper Kamo Shrine) and [[:en:Shimogamo Shrine|Shimogamo Shrine]] (Lower Kamo Shrine), located in Kyoto. Many shrines are also historically tied to the ancient [[:en:Kamo clan|Kamo-no-agatanushi]] clan (賀茂県主氏).
There are multiple spellings so this article will cover all of them

== Kamo Shrine ([[:ja:鴨神社|鴨神社]]) ==
'''Kamo Shrine''' (鴨神社) is a Shinto shrine whose name includes the character "Kamo" (鴨).

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:加茂神社]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:加茂神社]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:加茂神社]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:加茂神社]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q11398885]] Wikidata name: (none) [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 07:19, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q11398885]] Wikidata name: (none) [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 03:59, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} '''Kamo Shrine''' (加茂神社・賀茂神社) refers to Shinto shrines with the name "Kamo."
Most of these shrines were branches of the famous [[:en:Kamo shrines|Kamo Shrines]], [[:en:Kamigamo Shrine|Kamigamo Shrine]] (Upper Kamo Shrine) and [[:en:Shimogamo Shrine|Shimogamo Shrine]] (Lower Kamo Shrine), located in Kyoto. Many shrines are also historically tied to the ancient [[:en:Kamo clan|Kamo-no-agatanushi]] clan (賀茂県主氏).
There are multiple spellings so this article will cover all of them

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:加茂神社]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:加茂神社]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:加茂神社]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:加茂神社]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q11398885]] Wikidata name: (none) [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 07:19, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q11398885]] Wikidata name: (none) [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 03:59, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} '''Kamo Shrine''' (加茂神社・賀茂神社) refers to Shinto shrines with the name "Kamo."
Most of these shrines were branches of the famous [[:en:Kamo shrines|Kamo Shrines]], [[:en:Kamigamo Shrine|Kamigamo Shrine]] (Upper Kamo Shrine) and [[:en:Shimogamo Shrine|Shimogamo Shrine]] (Lower Kamo Shrine), located in Kyoto. Many shrines are also historically tied to the ancient [[:en:Kamo clan|Kamo-no-agatanushi]] clan (賀茂県主氏).
There are multiple spellings so this article will cover all of them

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:加茂神社]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:加茂神社]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:加茂神社]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:加茂神社]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q11398885]] Wikidata name: (none) [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 07:19, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q11398885]] Wikidata name: (none) [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 03:59, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} '''Kamo Shrine''' (加茂神社・賀茂神社) refers to Shinto shrines with the name "Kamo."
Most of these shrines were branches of the famous [[:en:Kamo shrines|Kamo Shrines]], [[:en:Kamigamo Shrine|Kamigamo Shrine]] (Upper Kamo Shrine) and [[:en:Shimogamo Shrine|Shimogamo Shrine]] (Lower Kamo Shrine), located in Kyoto. Many shrines are also historically tied to the ancient [[:en:Kamo clan|Kamo-no-agatanushi]] clan (賀茂県主氏).
There are multiple spellings so this article will cover all of them

revision.comment: v25: Convert interwiki links [[:en:...]] to {{ill}} templates
revision.text:
<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:加茂神社]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:加茂神社]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:加茂神社]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:加茂神社]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q11398885]] Wikidata name: (none) [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 07:19, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q11398885]] Wikidata name: (none) [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 03:59, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} '''Kamo Shrine''' (加茂神社・賀茂神社) refers to Shinto shrines with the name "Kamo."
Most of these shrines were branches of the famous {{ill|Kamo shrines|en|Kamo shrines|WD=Q1306606|lt=Kamo Shrines}}, {{ill|Kamigamo Shrine|en|Kamigamo Shrine|WD=Q700448|lt=Kamigamo Shrine}} (Upper Kamo Shrine) and {{ill|Shimogamo Shrine|en|Shimogamo Shrine|WD=Q701620|lt=Shimogamo Shrine}} (Lower Kamo Shrine), located in Kyoto. Many shrines are also historically tied to the ancient {{ill|Kamo clan|en|Kamo clan|WD=Q2975287|lt=Kamo-no-agatanushi}} clan (賀茂県主氏).
There are multiple spellings so this article will cover all of them

== Kamo Shrine ([[:ja:鴨神社|鴨神社]]) ==
'''Kamo Shrine''' (鴨神社) is a Shinto shrine whose name includes the character "Kamo" (鴨).

City}}) - Another name for {{ill|Kamo shrines|en|Kamo shrines|WD=Q1306606|lt=Kamo Shrines}}, which collectively refers to {{ill|Kamigamo Shrine|en|Kamigamo Shrine|WD=Q700448|lt=Kamigamo Shrine}} and {{ill|Shimogamo Shrine|en|Shimogamo Shrine|WD=Q701620|lt=Shimogamo Shrine}}. * {{ill|Kamo Shrine (Tamaki)|ja|Kamo Shrine (Ise)|lt=Kamo Shrine (Ise)|lt=鴨神社 (玉城町)|lt=Kamo Mie|en|Tamaki, Mie|WD=Q587348|lt=Tamaki}}, Yamagami) * {{ill|Kamo Shrine (Takatuki)|ja|鴨神社 (高槻市)|lt=Kamo Shrine|lt_ja=鴨神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamo Shrine (Takatuki)|1=Kamo revision.text:
<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:加茂神社]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:加茂神社]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:加茂神社]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:加茂神社]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q11398885]] Wikidata name: (none) [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 07:19, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q11398885]] Wikidata name: (none) [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 03:59, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} '''Kamo Shrine''' (加茂神社・賀茂神社) refers to Shinto shrines with the name "Kamo."
Most of these shrines were branches of the famous {{ill|Kamo shrines|en|Kamo shrines|WD=Q1306606|lt=Kamo Shrines}}, {{ill|Kamigamo Shrine|en|Kamigamo Shrine|WD=Q700448|lt=Kamigamo Shrine}} (Upper Kamo Shrine) and {{ill|Shimogamo Shrine|en|Shimogamo Shrine|WD=Q701620|lt=Shimogamo Shrine}} (Lower Kamo Shrine), located in Kyoto. Many shrines are also historically tied to the ancient {{ill|Kamo clan|en|Kamo clan|WD=Q2975287|lt=Kamo-no-agatanushi}} clan (賀茂県主氏).
There are multiple spellings so this article will cover all of them

== Kamo Shrine ([[:ja:鴨神社|鴨神社]]) ==
'''Kamo Shrine''' (鴨神社) is a Shinto shrine whose name includes the character "Kamo" (鴨).
* [[鴨神社_(玉城町)]] * {{ill|Kamo shrines|en|Kamo shrines|WD=Q1306606|lt=Kamo Shrine (Kamo-sha)}} ({{ill|Kyoto Prefecture|en|Kyoto Prefecture|WD=Q120730|lt=Kyoto}}, {{ill|Kyoto|en|Kyoto|WD=Q34600|lt=Kyoto City}}) - Another name for {{ill|Kamo shrines|en|Kamo shrines|WD=Q1306606|lt=Kamo Shrines}}, which collectively refers to {{ill|Kamigamo Shrine|en|Kamigamo Shrine|WD=Q700448|lt=Kamigamo Shrine}} and <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:加茂神社]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:加茂神社]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:加茂神社]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:加茂神社]]
'''Kamo Shrine''' (加茂神社・賀茂神社) refers to Shinto shrines with the name "Kamo."
Most of these shrines were branches of the famous {{ill|Kamo shrines|en|Kamo shrines|WD=Q1306606|lt=Kamo Shrines}}, {{ill|Kamigamo Shrine|en|Kamigamo Shrine|WD=Q700448|lt=Kamigamo Shrine}} (Upper Kamo Shrine) and {{ill|Shimogamo Shrine|en|Shimogamo Shrine|WD=Q701620|lt=Shimogamo Shrine}} (Lower Kamo Shrine), located in Kyoto. Many shrines are also historically tied to the ancient {{ill|Kamo clan|en|Kamo clan|WD=Q2975287|lt=Kamo-no-agatanushi}} clan (賀茂県主氏).
There are multiple spellings so this article will cover all of them

== Kamo Shrine ([[:ja:鴨神社|鴨神社]]) ==
'''Kamo Shrine''' (鴨神社) is a Shinto shrine whose name includes the character "Kamo" (鴨).
* [[鴨神社_(玉城町)]] * {{ill|Kamo shrines|en|Kamo shrines|WD=Q1306606|lt=Kamo Shrine (Kamo-sha)}} ({{ill|Kyoto Prefecture|en|Kyoto Prefecture|WD=Q120730|lt=Kyoto}}, {{ill|Kyoto|en|Kyoto|WD=Q34600|lt=Kyoto City}}) - Another name for {{ill|Kamo shrines|en|Kamo shrines|WD=Q1306606|lt=Kamo Shrines}}, which collectively refers to {{ill|Kamigamo Shrine|en|Kamigamo Shrine|WD=Q700448|lt=Kamigamo Shrine}} and {{ill|Shimogamo Shrine|en|Shimogamo Shrine|WD=Q701620|lt=Shimogamo Shrine}}. * {{ill|Kamo Shrine (Tamaki)|ja|Kamo Shrine (Ise)|lt=Kamo Shrine (Ise)|lt=鴨神社 (玉城町)|lt=Kamo <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:加茂神社]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:加茂神社]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:加茂神社]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:加茂神社]]
'''Kamo Shrine''' (加茂神社・賀茂神社) refers to Shinto shrines with the name "Kamo."
Most of these shrines were branches of the famous {{ill|Kamo shrines|en|Kamo shrines|WD=Q1306606|lt=Kamo Shrines}}, {{ill|Kamigamo Shrine|en|Kamigamo Shrine|WD=Q700448|lt=Kamigamo Shrine}} (Upper Kamo Shrine) and {{ill|Shimogamo Shrine|en|Shimogamo Shrine|WD=Q701620|lt=Shimogamo Shrine}} (Lower Kamo Shrine), located in Kyoto. Many shrines are also historically tied to the ancient {{ill|Kamo clan|en|Kamo clan|WD=Q2975287|lt=Kamo-no-agatanushi}} clan (賀茂県主氏).
There are multiple spellings so this article will cover all of them

== Kamo Shrine ([[:ja:鴨神社|鴨神社]]) ==
'''Kamo Shrine''' (鴨神社) is a Shinto shrine whose name includes the character "Kamo" (鴨).

City}}) - Another name for {{ill|Kamo shrines|en|Kamo shrines|WD=Q1306606|lt=Kamo Shrines}}, which collectively refers to {{ill|Kamigamo Shrine|en|Kamigamo Shrine|WD=Q700448|lt=Kamigamo Shrine}} and revision.text:
<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:加茂神社]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:加茂神社]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:加茂神社]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:加茂神社]]
'''Kamo Shrine''' (加茂神社・賀茂神社) refers to Shinto shrines with the name "Kamo."
Most of these shrines were branches of the famous {{ill|Kamo shrines|en|Kamo shrines|WD=Q1306606|lt=Kamo Shrines}}, {{ill|Kamigamo Shrine|en|Kamigamo Shrine|WD=Q700448|lt=Kamigamo Shrine}} (Upper Kamo Shrine) and {{ill|Shimogamo Shrine|en|Shimogamo Shrine|WD=Q701620|lt=Shimogamo Shrine}} (Lower Kamo Shrine), located in Kyoto. Many shrines are also historically tied to the ancient {{ill|Kamo clan|en|Kamo clan|WD=Q2975287|lt=Kamo-no-agatanushi}} clan (賀茂県主氏).
There are multiple spellings so this article will cover all of them

== Kamo Shrine ([[:ja:鴨神社|鴨神社]]) ==
'''Kamo Shrine''' (鴨神社) is a Shinto shrine whose name includes the character "Kamo" (鴨).

City}}) - Another name for {{ill|Kamo shrines|en|Kamo shrines|WD=Q1306606|lt=Kamo Shrines}}, which collectively refers to {{ill|Kamigamo Shrine|en|Kamigamo Shrine|WD=Q700448|lt=Kamigamo Shrine}} and <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:加茂神社]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:加茂神社]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:加茂神社]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:加茂神社]]
'''Kamo Shrine''' (加茂神社・賀茂神社) refers to Shinto shrines with the name "Kamo."
Most of these shrines were branches of the famous {{ill|Kamo shrines|en|Kamo shrines|WD=Q1306606|lt=Kamo Shrines|1=Kamo shrines}}, {{ill|Kamigamo Shrine|en|Kamigamo Shrine|WD=Q700448|lt=Kamigamo Shrine|1=Kamigamo Shrine}} (Upper Kamo Shrine) and {{ill|Shimogamo Shrine|en|Shimogamo Shrine|WD=Q701620|lt=Shimogamo Shrine|1=Shimogamo Shrine}} (Lower Kamo Shrine), located in Kyoto. Many shrines are also historically tied to the ancient {{ill|Kamo clan|en|Kamo clan|WD=Q2975287|lt=Kamo-no-agatanushi|1=Kamo clan}} clan (賀茂県主氏).
There are multiple spellings so this article will cover all of them

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:加茂神社]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:加茂神社]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:加茂神社]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:加茂神社]]
'''Kamo Shrine''' (加茂神社・賀茂神社) refers to Shinto shrines with the name "Kamo."
Most of these shrines were branches of the famous {{ill|Kamo shrines|en|Kamo shrines|WD=Q1306606|lt=Kamo Shrines|1=Kamo shrines}}, {{ill|Kamigamo Shrine|en|Kamigamo Shrine|WD=Q700448|lt=Kamigamo Shrine|1=Kamigamo Shrine}} (Upper Kamo Shrine) and {{ill|Shimogamo Shrine|en|Shimogamo Shrine|WD=Q701620|lt=Shimogamo Shrine|1=Shimogamo Shrine}} (Lower Kamo Shrine), located in Kyoto. Many shrines are also historically tied to the ancient {{ill|Kamo clan|en|Kamo clan|WD=Q2975287|lt=Kamo-no-agatanushi|1=Kamo clan}} clan (賀茂県主氏).
There are multiple spellings so this article will cover all of them

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:加茂神社]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:加茂神社]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:加茂神社]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:加茂神社]]
'''Kamo Shrine''' (加茂神社・賀茂神社) refers to Shinto shrines with the name "Kamo."
Most of these shrines were branches of the famous {{ill|Kamo shrines|en|Kamo shrines|qid=Q1306606|lt=Kamo Shrines|1=Kamo shrines}}, {{ill|Kamigamo Shrine|en|Kamigamo Shrine|qid=Q700448|lt=Kamigamo Shrine|1=Kamigamo Shrine}} (Upper Kamo Shrine) and {{ill|Shimogamo Shrine|en|Shimogamo Shrine|qid=Q701620|lt=Shimogamo Shrine|1=Shimogamo Shrine}} (Lower Kamo Shrine), located in Kyoto. Many shrines are also historically tied to the ancient {{ill|Kamo clan|en|Kamo clan|qid=Q2975287|lt=Kamo-no-agatanushi|1=Kamo clan}} clan (賀茂県主氏).
There are multiple spellings so this article will cover all of them

Prefecture|en|Tokushima Prefecture|qid=Q160734|lt=Tokushima Prefecture|1=Tokushima Prefecture}}, {{ill|Higashimiyoshi|en|Higashimiyoshi|qid=Q1206006|lt=Higashimiyoshi|1=Higashimiyoshi}}, Kamo) * {{ill|Kamo Shrine (Sakaide)|ja|鴨神社 (坂出市)|lt=Kamo Shrine|lt_ja=鴨神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamo Shrine (Sakaide)|1=Kamo Shrine (Sakaide)|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11675600}} - An {{ill|Sanuki Province|en|Sanuki Province|qid=Q929682|lt=Ancient shrine of Sanuki Province|1=Sanuki Province}}. Theories of its location include {{ill|Higashi Kamo Shrine|ja|鴨神社 (坂出市)#東鴨神社|lt=Higashi Kamo Shrine|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Higashi Kamo Shrine|1=Kamo Shrine (Sakaide)|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11675600}} and {{ill|Nishi Kamo Shrine|ja|鴨神社 (坂出市)#西鴨神社|lt=Nishi Kamo Shrine|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nishi Kamo Shrine|1=Kamo Shrine (Sakaide)|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11675600}} (both in {{ill|Sakaide, Kagawa|en|Sakaide, Kagawa|qid=Q855059|lt=Sakaide|1=Sakaide, Kagawa}}, Kamochō).

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:加茂神社]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:加茂神社]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:加茂神社]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:加茂神社]]
'''Kamo Shrine''' (加茂神社・賀茂神社) refers to Shinto shrines with the name "Kamo."
Most of these shrines were branches of the famous {{ill|Kamo shrines|en|Kamo shrines|qid=Q1306606|lt=Kamo Shrines|1=Kamo shrines}}, {{ill|Kamigamo Shrine|en|Kamigamo Shrine|qid=Q700448|lt=Kamigamo Shrine|1=Kamigamo Shrine}} (Upper Kamo Shrine) and {{ill|Shimogamo Shrine|en|Shimogamo Shrine|qid=Q701620|lt=Shimogamo Shrine|1=Shimogamo Shrine}} (Lower Kamo Shrine), located in Kyoto. Many shrines are also historically tied to the ancient {{ill|Kamo clan|en|Kamo clan|qid=Q2975287|lt=Kamo-no-agatanushi|1=Kamo clan}} clan (賀茂県主氏).
There are multiple spellings so this article will cover all of them

== Kamo Shrine ([[:ja:鴨神社|鴨神社]]) ==
'''Kamo Shrine''' (鴨神社) is a Shinto shrine whose name includes the character "Kamo" (鴨).
* [[鴨神社_(玉城町)]] * {{ill|Kamo shrines|en|Kamo shrines|qid=Q1306606|lt=Kamo Shrine (Kamo-sha)|1=Kamo shrines}} ({{ill|Kyoto Prefecture|en|Kyoto Prefecture|qid=Q120730|lt=Kyoto|1=Kyoto Prefecture}}, {{ill|Kyoto|en|Kyoto|qid=Q34600|lt=Kyoto City|1=Kyoto}}) - Another name for {{ill|Kamo shrines|en|Kamo shrines|qid=Q1306606|lt=Kamo Shrines|1=Kamo shrines}}, which collectively refers to {{ill|Kamigamo Shrine|en|Kamigamo Shrine|qid=Q700448|lt=Kamigamo Shrine|1=Kamigamo Shrine}} and {{ill|Shimogamo Shrine|en|Shimogamo Shrine|qid=Q701620|lt=Shimogamo Shrine|1=Shimogamo Shrine}}. * revision.comment: Bot: strip HTML comments; consolidate 8 interlanguage link(s) into wikidata link template (has duplicate-language pairs) [[github:EmmaLeonhart/shintowiki-scripts/actions/runs/25083821807|commit triggered pipeline]]
revision.text:
'''Kamo Shrine''' (加茂神社・賀茂神社) refers to Shinto shrines with the name "Kamo."
Most of these shrines were branches of the famous {{ill|Kamo shrines|en|Kamo shrines|qid=Q1306606|lt=Kamo Shrines|1=Kamo shrines}}, {{ill|Kamigamo Shrine|en|Kamigamo Shrine|qid=Q700448|lt=Kamigamo Shrine|1=Kamigamo Shrine}} (Upper Kamo Shrine) and {{ill|Shimogamo Shrine|en|Shimogamo Shrine|qid=Q701620|lt=Shimogamo Shrine|1=Shimogamo Shrine}} (Lower Kamo Shrine), located in Kyoto. Many shrines are also historically tied to the ancient {{ill|Kamo clan|en|Kamo clan|qid=Q2975287|lt=Kamo-no-agatanushi|1=Kamo clan}} clan (賀茂県主氏).
There are multiple spellings so this article will cover all of them

== Kamo Shrine ([[:ja:鴨神社|鴨神社]]) ==
'''Kamo Shrine''' (鴨神社) is a Shinto shrine whose name includes the character "Kamo" (鴨).
